Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/***************************************************************************************************\n *  File:        src/module_30.ts\n *  Project:     PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 system_monitoring\n *  Description: Metric-to-Social-Signal enrichment module.\n *\n *  This unit demonstrates several architectural patterns used throughout the code-base:\n *    \u2022 Strategy Pattern        \u2013 Pluggable strategies for fetching social signals\n *    \u2022 Chain of Responsibility \u2013 Multi-step metric enrichment pipeline\n *    \u2022 Observer Pattern        \u2013 Publish enriched metrics to interested downstream components\n *    \u2022 Command Pattern         \u2013 Encapsulate enrichment execution for scheduler / worker pools\n *\n *  NOTE: External project-specific dependencies (e.g. KafkaProducer, ConfigService) are assumed to\n *        exist elsewhere in the monorepo. Import paths are kept relative to convey intent.\n ***************************************************************************************************/\n\nimport EventEmitter from 'events';\nimport { randomUUID } from 'crypto';\nimport pino from 'pino';\n\nimport { Metric, EnrichedMetric, SocialSignal } from './types/metrics';\nimport { ConfigService } from './core/config';\nimport { KafkaProducer } from './infrastructure/messaging/kafkaProducer';\nimport { RedisClientType } from './infrastructure/cache/redisTypes';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logger\n * -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nconst logger = pino({\n  name: 'metric-social-enrichment',\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Strategy Pattern \u2013 Social Signal Providers\n * -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/** Context object passed to strategies (kept intentionally minimal). */\nexport interface MetricContext {\n  metric: Metric;\n  correlationId: string;\n}\n\n/** Contract for all social-signal retrieval strategies. */\nexport interface SocialSignalStrategy {\n  /**\n   * Fetch social signals related to the supplied metric.\n   * Implementations MUST be side-effect free and idempotent.\n   */\n  fetchSignals(ctx: MetricContext): Promise<SocialSignal | null>;\n}\n\n/* ----- Strategy #1: Redis Cache ----------------------------------------------------------------*/\nclass RedisSignalStrategy implements SocialSignalStrategy {\n  constructor(private readonly redis: RedisClientType) {}\n\n  async fetchSignals(ctx: MetricContext): Promise<SocialSignal | null> {\n    const cacheKey = `social-signal:${ctx.metric.entityId}`;\n    try {\n      const raw = await this.redis.get(cacheKey);\n      if (!raw) return null;\n\n      logger.debug({ cacheKey, correlationId: ctx.correlationId }, 'Social signal retrieved from cache');\n      return JSON.parse(raw) as SocialSignal;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.warn({ err, cacheKey }, 'Failed to fetch social signal from Redis');\n      return null;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* ----- Strategy #2: Live API -------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nclass LiveApiSignalStrategy implements SocialSignalStrategy {\n  /**\n   * A lightweight HTTP client is injected (axios-like interface).\n   * Provided by platform DI container / service mesh sidecar.\n   */\n  constructor(private readonly http: { get<T>(url: string): Promise<{ data: T }> }) {}\n\n  async fetchSignals(ctx: MetricContext): Promise<SocialSignal | null> {\n    const endpoint = `/signals/${ctx.metric.entityType}/${ctx.metric.entityId}`;\n    try {\n      const { data } = await this.http.get<SocialSignal>(endpoint);\n      logger.debug({ endpoint, correlationId: ctx.correlationId }, 'Social signal retrieved from live API');\n      return data;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err, endpoint }, 'Failed to fetch social signal via Live API');\n      return null;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* ----- Strategy Selector / Factory -------------------------------------------------------------*/\nclass SocialSignalStrategyFactory {\n  static build(config: ConfigService, deps: { redis?: RedisClientType; http?: any }): SocialSignalStrategy {\n    const source = config.get<string>('signals.source', 'cache-first');\n\n    if (source === 'live-only') {\n      return new LiveApiSignalStrategy(deps.http);\n    }\n\n    if (source === 'cache-only') {\n      return new RedisSignalStrategy(deps.redis!);\n    }\n\n    // Default strategy: try cache, then live API if needed.\n    return new (class CompositeStrategy implements SocialSignalStrategy {\n      private readonly cache = new RedisSignalStrategy(deps.redis!);\n      private readonly live = new LiveApiSignalStrategy(deps.http);\n\n      async fetchSignals(ctx: MetricContext): Promise<SocialSignal | null> {\n        return (await this.cache.fetchSignals(ctx)) ?? (await this.live.fetchSignals(ctx));\n      }\n    })();\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Chain of Responsibility \u2013 Metric Enrichment Pipeline\n * -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nabstract class EnrichmentHandler {\n  private next: EnrichmentHandler | undefined;\n\n  setNext(handler: EnrichmentHandler): EnrichmentHandler {\n    this.next = handler;\n    return handler;\n  }\n\n  async handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<EnrichedMetric> {\n    const processed = await this.process(metric);\n    if (this.next) {\n      return this.next.handle(processed);\n    }\n    return processed;\n  }\n\n  protected abstract process(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<EnrichedMetric>;\n}\n\n/* ----- Handler #1: Inject Social Signals -------------------------------------------------------*/\nclass SocialSignalEnrichmentHandler extends EnrichmentHandler {\n  constructor(private readonly strategy: SocialSignalStrategy) {\n    super();\n  }\n\n  protected async process(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<EnrichedMetric> {\n    const ctx: MetricContext = {\n      metric,\n      correlationId: metric.correlationId ?? randomUUID(),\n    };\n\n    const signals = await this.strategy.fetchSignals(ctx);\n    return {\n      ...metric,\n      correlationId: ctx.correlationId,\n      socialSignal: signals ?? undefined,\n    };\n  }\n}\n\n/* ----- Handler #2: Add Derived Tags -------------------------------------------------------------*/\nclass TaggingEnrichmentHandler extends EnrichmentHandler {\n  protected async process(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<EnrichedMetric> {\n    const trending = metric.socialSignal?.likes && metric.socialSignal.likes > 10_000;\n    const tags = new Set(metric.tags ?? []);\n\n    if (trending) tags.add('trending');\n    if (metric.socialSignal?.comments) tags.add('has-comments');\n\n    return {\n      ...metric,\n      tags: Array.from(tags),\n    };\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Observer Pattern \u2013 Enrichment Event Bus\n * -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nexport interface EnrichedMetricListener {\n  (metric: EnrichedMetric): void | Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Simple event bus (in-memory). For cross-process propagation use Kafka topic instead.\n */\nclass EnrichmentEventBus extends EventEmitter {\n  emitMetric(metric: EnrichedMetric): void {\n    this.emit('metric', metric);\n  }\n\n  subscribe(listener: EnrichedMetricListener): () => void {\n    this.on('metric', listener);\n    return () => this.off('metric', listener);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Command Pattern \u2013 EnrichMetricCommand\n * -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nexport class EnrichMetricCommand {\n  readonly name = 'EnrichMetricCommand';\n  constructor(public readonly payload: Metric) {}\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * MetricEnrichmentOrchestrator \u2013 Public API\n * -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nexport class MetricEnrichmentOrchestrator {\n  private readonly bus = new EnrichmentEventBus();\n  private readonly pipeline: EnrichmentHandler;\n  private readonly producer: KafkaProducer;\n\n  constructor(config: ConfigService, deps: { redis?: RedisClientType; http: any; producer: KafkaProducer }) {\n    const strategy = SocialSignalStrategyFactory.build(config, deps);\n\n    // Build pipeline\n    this.pipeline = new SocialSignalEnrichmentHandler(strategy);\n    this.pipeline.setNext(new TaggingEnrichmentHandler());\n\n    this.producer = deps.producer;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Execute the enrichment pipeline for a single metric and publish the result.\n   */\n  async execute(cmd: EnrichMetricCommand): Promise<void> {\n    const baseMetric: EnrichedMetric = { ...cmd.payload, correlationId: randomUUID() };\n\n    try {\n      const enriched = await this.pipeline.handle(baseMetric);\n      await this.producer.send('enriched-metrics', enriched);\n      this.bus.emitMetric(enriched);\n\n      logger.info(\n        {\n          entityId: enriched.entityId,\n          correlationId: enriched.correlationId,\n          tags: enriched.tags,\n        },\n        'Metric successfully enriched and published',\n      );\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err, entityId: cmd.payload.entityId }, 'Failed to enrich metric');\n      throw err;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Consumers (e.g. alerting engine) subscribe to enriched metrics.\n   */\n  onEnrichedMetric(listener: EnrichedMetricListener): () => void {\n    return this.bus.subscribe(listener);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Convenience bootstrap for lightweight usage (e.g. CLI scripts, tests)\n * -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nlet _singleton: MetricEnrichmentOrchestrator | undefined;\n\n/**\n * Acquire singleton orchestrator instance. Lazily constructs required dependencies\n * using default process-level configuration.\n */\nexport async function getMetricEnrichmentOrchestrator(): Promise<MetricEnrichmentOrchestrator> {\n  if (_singleton) return _singleton;\n\n  const config = await ConfigService.bootstrap();\n  const redis = await config.optional<boolean>('redis.enabled', true)\n    ? (await import('./infrastructure/cache/redisClient')).redisClient\n    : undefined;\n\n  const httpClient = await import('./infrastructure/http/client'); // dynamic import to avoid circular deps\n  const producer = await KafkaProducer.connect(config);\n\n  _singleton = new MetricEnrichmentOrchestrator(config, {\n    redis,\n    http: httpClient.default,\n    producer,\n  });\n\n  return _singleton;\n}\n```\n",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_30.ts",
    "numLines": 291,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 291
  },
  "type": "text"

{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**\n *  src/module_34.ts\n *\n *  CorrelatedMetricsAnalyzer\n *  -------------------------------------------------------------\n *  Consumes raw infrastructure metrics from Kafka, enriches them\n *  with live social-interaction signals and evaluates whether an\n *  alert or an auto-scaling action must be triggered. Evaluation\n *  follows a Chain-of-Responsibility pipeline, and scaling actions\n *  are delegated to interchangeable Strategy implementations.\n *\n *  Architectural patterns showcased:\n *    \u2022 Event-Driven ingestion (Kafka)\n *    \u2022 Chain-of-Responsibility (metric evaluators)\n *    \u2022 Strategy Pattern (scaling decision)\n *\n *  NOTE:\n *  \u2011 External service clients (Kafka, NATS, social-signal back-end)\n *    are intentionally lightweight to keep this single-file demo\n *    self-contained. In the real codebase they live in dedicated\n *    packages with resiliency, metrics and tracing built-in.\n *  -------------------------------------------------------------\n */\n\nimport { Kafka, Consumer, KafkaMessage, logLevel } from 'kafkajs';\nimport { connect, NatsConnection, StringCodec } from 'nats';\n\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n// Domain types\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n\n/**\n * Raw metric emitted by the telemetry pipeline.\n */\nexport interface MetricEvent {\n  tenantId: string;\n  hostId: string;\n  timestamp: number;         // Unix epoch millis\n  cpu: number;               // percentage (0-100)\n  memory: number;            // percentage (0-100)\n  p95LatencyMs: number;      // API latency\n  reqPerSec: number;         // throughput\n}\n\n/**\n * Enriched social-interaction context for a given tenant.\n */\nexport interface SocialSignal {\n  tenantId: string;\n  likeRate: number;          // likes per second\n  shareRate: number;         // shares per second\n  commentRate: number;       // comments per second\n  sentimentScore: number;    // \u20111 .. 1\n  isTrending: boolean;       // heuristic flag\n}\n\n/**\n * Combined context forwarded through the evaluator chain.\n */\nexport interface EvaluationContext {\n  metric: MetricEvent;\n  social: SocialSignal;\n}\n\n/**\n * Decision produced by the evaluator chain.\n */\nexport interface AlertDecision {\n  shouldAlert: boolean;\n  severity: 'info' | 'warning' | 'critical';\n  reason: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Decision that drives downstream auto-scaling. Produced by\n * Strategy implementations.\n */\nexport interface ScalingDecision {\n  action: 'scale_out' | 'scale_in' | 'no_op';\n  replicasDelta: number;      // positive => add, negative => remove\n  rationale: string;\n}\n\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n// Social-signal client (stub)\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n\nclass SocialSignalClient {\n  /**\n   * Fetches latest social metrics for the specified tenant. In the\n   * actual platform this call queries a Redis cache or hits a\n   * high-throughput gRPC micro-service.\n   */\n  async fetch(tenantId: string): Promise<SocialSignal> {\n    // TODO: Replace stub with real RPC call.\n    return {\n      tenantId,\n      likeRate: Math.random() * 500,\n      shareRate: Math.random() * 120,\n      commentRate: Math.random() * 200,\n      sentimentScore: Math.random() * 2 - 1,\n      isTrending: Math.random() > 0.85,\n    };\n  }\n}\n\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n// Chain-of-Responsibility for metric evaluation\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n\nabstract class MetricHandler {\n  protected next?: MetricHandler;\n\n  withNext(handler: MetricHandler): MetricHandler {\n    this.next = handler;\n    return handler;\n  }\n\n  async handlePayload(ctx: EvaluationContext): Promise<AlertDecision> {\n    const decision = await this.evaluate(ctx);\n\n    // Short-circuit if current handler triggers an alert:\n    if (decision.shouldAlert || !this.next) {\n      return decision;\n    }\n\n    // Otherwise continue processing downstream:\n    return this.next.handlePayload(ctx);\n  }\n\n  protected abstract evaluate(ctx: EvaluationContext): Promise<AlertDecision>;\n}\n\n/**\n * CPU usage anomaly detector.\n */\nclass CpuUsageHandler extends MetricHandler {\n  protected async evaluate(ctx: EvaluationContext): Promise<AlertDecision> {\n    const { cpu } = ctx.metric;\n    const isHot = cpu > 85;\n    const severity: AlertDecision['severity'] =\n      cpu > 95 ? 'critical' : 'warning';\n\n    if (isHot) {\n      return {\n        shouldAlert: true,\n        severity,\n        reason: `CPU at ${cpu.toFixed(1)}%`,\n      };\n    }\n    return { shouldAlert: false, severity: 'info', reason: '' };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Memory pressure detector.\n */\nclass MemoryUsageHandler extends MetricHandler {\n  protected async evaluate(ctx: EvaluationContext): Promise<AlertDecision> {\n    const { memory } = ctx.metric;\n    const isOomRisk = memory > 90;\n\n    if (isOomRisk) {\n      return {\n        shouldAlert: true,\n        severity: memory > 97 ? 'critical' : 'warning',\n        reason: `Memory utilization at ${memory.toFixed(1)}%`,\n      };\n    }\n    return { shouldAlert: false, severity: 'info', reason: '' };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Latency spike detector that factors in social trending context.\n */\nclass LatencySpikeHandler extends MetricHandler {\n  protected async evaluate(ctx: EvaluationContext): Promise<AlertDecision> {\n    const { p95LatencyMs } = ctx.metric;\n    const trendingFactor = ctx.social.isTrending ? 0.8 : 1; // trending = more strict\n\n    if (p95LatencyMs * trendingFactor > 400) {\n      return {\n        shouldAlert: true,\n        severity: p95LatencyMs > 800 ? 'critical' : 'warning',\n        reason: `P95 latency ${p95LatencyMs.toFixed(0)}ms (trending=${ctx.social.isTrending})`,\n      };\n    }\n    return { shouldAlert: false, severity: 'info', reason: '' };\n  }\n}\n\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n// Scaling strategy pattern\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n\ninterface ScalingStrategy {\n  decide(ctx: EvaluationContext, alert: AlertDecision): Promise<ScalingDecision>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Reactive scaling strategy: add replicas when any alert occurs.\n */\nclass ReactiveScalingStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  async decide(_: EvaluationContext, alert: AlertDecision): Promise<ScalingDecision> {\n    if (!alert.shouldAlert) {\n      return { action: 'no_op', replicasDelta: 0, rationale: 'All clear' };\n    }\n\n    const delta = alert.severity === 'critical' ? 3 : 1;\n    return {\n      action: 'scale_out',\n      replicasDelta: delta,\n      rationale: `Reactive strategy for ${alert.reason}`,\n    };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Predictive strategy looks at social trending signals to\n * proactively scale out before an alert fires.\n */\nclass PredictiveScalingStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  async decide(ctx: EvaluationContext): Promise<ScalingDecision> {\n    const { likeRate, shareRate, isTrending } = ctx.social;\n    const highSocialTraffic = (likeRate + shareRate) > 500 || isTrending;\n\n    if (highSocialTraffic) {\n      const delta = Math.ceil((likeRate + shareRate) / 500); // heuristic\n      return {\n        action: 'scale_out',\n        replicasDelta: delta,\n        rationale: `Predictive social surge (likeRate=${likeRate.toFixed(0)}, shareRate=${shareRate.toFixed(0)})`,\n      };\n    }\n\n    return { action: 'no_op', replicasDelta: 0, rationale: 'Social calm' };\n  }\n}\n\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n// Alerting and scaling command publishers (stubs)\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n\nclass AlertingService {\n  async send(decision: AlertDecision, ctx: EvaluationContext): Promise<void> {\n    // Real implementation pushes to PagerDuty, Slack or OpsGenie.\n    console.log(\n      `[ALERT] tenant=${ctx.metric.tenantId} host=${ctx.metric.hostId} severity=${decision.severity} reason=\"${decision.reason}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nclass ScalingCommandPublisher {\n  private nc?: NatsConnection;\n  private readonly codec = StringCodec();\n\n  constructor(private readonly natsUrl: string) {}\n\n  async connect(): Promise<void> {\n    this.nc = await connect({ servers: this.natsUrl });\n  }\n\n  async publish(decision: ScalingDecision, tenantId: string): Promise<void> {\n    if (!this.nc) {\n      throw new Error('NATS not connected');\n    }\n    const payload = JSON.stringify({ ...decision, tenantId, timestamp: Date.now() });\n    this.nc.publish('autoscale.command', this.codec.encode(payload));\n  }\n\n  async close(): Promise<void> {\n    await this.nc?.drain();\n  }\n}\n\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n// Main analyzer orchestrator\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n\nexport class CorrelatedMetricsAnalyzer {\n  private readonly kafka: Kafka;\n  private consumer!: Consumer;\n  private readonly evaluatorChain: MetricHandler;\n  private readonly socialClient = new SocialSignalClient();\n  private readonly alertingSvc = new AlertingService();\n  private readonly scalingPublisher: ScalingCommandPublisher;\n\n  private readonly strategies: ScalingStrategy[] = [\n    new PredictiveScalingStrategy(),\n    new ReactiveScalingStrategy(),\n  ];\n\n  constructor(\n    kafkaBrokers: string[],\n    private readonly natsUrl: string,\n    private readonly groupId = 'correlated-metrics-analyzer'\n  ) {\n    this.kafka = new Kafka({ brokers: kafkaBrokers, logLevel: logLevel.ERROR });\n    // Compose handler chain\n    this.evaluatorChain = new CpuUsageHandler()\n      .withNext(new MemoryUsageHandler())\n      .withNext(new LatencySpikeHandler());\n\n    this.scalingPublisher = new ScalingCommandPublisher(this.natsUrl);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Bootstraps Kafka + NATS connections and starts consuming.\n   */\n  async start(): Promise<void> {\n    await this.scalingPublisher.connect();\n\n    this.consumer = this.kafka.consumer({ groupId: this.groupId });\n    await this.consumer.connect();\n    await this.consumer.subscribe({ topic: 'system.metrics', fromBeginning: false });\n\n    await this.consumer.run({\n      eachMessage: async ({ message }) => {\n        try {\n          const metric = this.deserializeMetric(message);\n          const social = await this.socialClient.fetch(metric.tenantId);\n\n          const ctx: EvaluationContext = { metric, social };\n          const alert = await this.evaluatorChain.handlePayload(ctx);\n\n          // Fire alert if needed:\n          if (alert.shouldAlert) {\n            await this.alertingSvc.send(alert, ctx);\n          }\n\n          // Determine scaling action via first strategy that proposes an action:\n          for (const strategy of this.strategies) {\n            const scaling = await strategy.decide(ctx, alert);\n            if (scaling.action !== 'no_op') {\n              await this.scalingPublisher.publish(scaling, metric.tenantId);\n              break;\n            }\n          }\n        } catch (err) {\n          // Production code: push to error monitoring/metrics\n          console.error('[CorrelatedMetricsAnalyzer] Processing error', err);\n        }\n      },\n    });\n\n    console.info('[CorrelatedMetricsAnalyzer] Started.');\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Gracefully stops all connections.\n   */\n  async stop(): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      await this.consumer?.disconnect();\n      await this.scalingPublisher.close();\n    } finally {\n      console.info('[CorrelatedMetricsAnalyzer] Stopped.');\n    }\n  }\n\n  ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n  // Helpers\n  ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n\n  private deserializeMetric(msg: KafkaMessage): MetricEvent {\n    if (!msg.value) {\n      throw new Error('Empty Kafka message');\n    }\n    const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(msg.value.toString());\n\n    // Basic runtime validation (replace with io-ts / zod in real code).\n    const required = ['tenantId', 'hostId', 'timestamp', 'cpu', 'memory', 'p95LatencyMs', 'reqPerSec'];\n    for (const field of required) {\n      if (!(field in (parsed as Record<string, unknown>))) {\n        throw new Error(`MetricEvent missing ${field}`);\n      }\n    }\n    return parsed as MetricEvent;\n  }\n}\n\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n// Entrypoint (only executed when run directly with ts-node / node)\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  // Boot parameters via env vars for containerization friendliness.\n  const brokers = process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS?.split(',') ?? ['localhost:9092'];\n  const natsUrl = process.env.NATS_URL ?? 'nats://localhost:4222';\n\n  const analyzer = new CorrelatedMetricsAnalyzer(brokers, natsUrl);\n\n  analyzer.start().catch((err) => {\n    console.error('[CorrelatedMetricsAnalyzer] Fatal startup error', err);\n    process.exit(1);\n  });\n\n  // SIGTERM/SIGINT for graceful shutdown in Kubernetes\n  const shutdown = async () => {\n    console.log('[CorrelatedMetricsAnalyzer] Caught termination signal');\n    await analyzer.stop();\n    process.exit(0);\n  };\n\n  process.on('SIGTERM', shutdown);\n  process.on('SIGINT', shutdown);\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_34.ts",
    "numLines": 410,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 410
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**\n * Integration-style unit tests for the most critical coordination path:\n *   1) Social/Infra metric correlation (SentimentCorrelator)\n *   2) Capacity remediation trigger (CapacityOrchestrator)\n *\n * The tests lean on Jest\u2019s mocking facilities to isolate collaborators while still\n * exercising real-world behaviour (e.g. strategy selection, error bubbling, etc.).\n *\n * NOTE:\n *   \u2013 All imports reference the \u201csrc/\u201d tree as it exists in the production code-base.\n *   \u2013 test setup/teardown is idempotent so the file can be executed in parallel CI.\n */\n\nimport { jest } from '@jest/globals';\nimport { SentimentCorrelator } from '../src/core/analytics/SentimentCorrelator';\nimport { CapacityOrchestrator } from '../src/core/orchestration/CapacityOrchestrator';\nimport { MetricSample } from '../src/types/metrics';\nimport { SocialEvent } from '../src/types/social';\nimport { ScaleCommand } from '../src/core/orchestration/commands/ScaleCommand';\nimport { Logger } from '../src/shared/logging/Logger';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                        \u2500\u2500 Shared mocks & test scaffolding \u2500\u2500               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n// A deterministic timestamp used throughout this test-suite.\nconst NOW = new Date('2033-04-05T12:00:00.000Z');\n\n// Freeze time so every component acts as if \u201cnow\u201d is our constant value.\njest.useFakeTimers().setSystemTime(NOW);\n\n// Mock the cross-cutting logger so the test output stays clean.\njest.mock('../src/shared/logging/Logger', () => ({\n  Logger: {\n    child: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({\n      info: jest.fn(),\n      warn: jest.fn(),\n      error: jest.fn(),\n      debug: jest.fn(),\n    }),\n  },\n}));\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                               \u2500\u2500 Fixtures \u2500\u2500                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nconst sampleMetrics: MetricSample[] = [\n  {\n    timestamp: new Date(NOW.getTime() - 30_000), // 30 seconds ago\n    podId: 'api-gateway-66dd98cc7b-xzsa1',\n    cpu: 0.83,\n    mem: 0.71,\n    rps: 14_200,\n  },\n  {\n    timestamp: new Date(NOW.getTime() - 10_000), // 10 seconds ago\n    podId: 'api-gateway-66dd98cc7b-xzsa1',\n    cpu: 0.91,\n    mem: 0.77,\n    rps: 17_300,\n  },\n];\n\nconst socialEvents: SocialEvent[] = [\n  {\n    timestamp: new Date(NOW.getTime() - 32_000), // ~ same timeframe\n    type: 'HASHTAG_TREND',\n    payload: {\n      hashtag: '#SolarEclipse',\n      region: 'US-CA',\n      projectedReach: 1_800_000,\n    },\n  },\n  {\n    timestamp: new Date(NOW.getTime() - 11_000),\n    type: 'INFLUENCER_LIVE',\n    payload: {\n      influencerId: '0xF00DBABE',\n      followerCount: 3_400_000,\n    },\n  },\n];\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                        \u2500\u2500 SentimentCorrelator tests \u2500\u2500                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ndescribe('SentimentCorrelator', () => {\n  it('correlates infra metrics with social spikes correctly', async () => {\n    const correlator = new SentimentCorrelator();\n\n    const result = await correlator.correlate(sampleMetrics, socialEvents);\n\n    expect(result).toMatchObject({\n      correlationWindow: { from: expect.any(Date), to: expect.any(Date) },\n      correlatedMetrics: expect.arrayContaining(sampleMetrics),\n      socialAmplifiers: expect.arrayContaining(socialEvents),\n      correlationScore: expect.any(Number),\n    });\n\n    // Ensure the correlation score is > 0 (meaning there is some influence).\n    expect(result.correlationScore).toBeGreaterThan(0.5);\n  });\n\n  it('returns an empty correlation when no temporal overlap exists', async () => {\n    const correlator = new SentimentCorrelator();\n\n    const farPastMetrics: MetricSample[] = [\n      {\n        timestamp: new Date('2029-01-01T00:00:00Z'),\n        podId: 'legacy-pod-123',\n        cpu: 0.22,\n        mem: 0.18,\n        rps: 120,\n      },\n    ];\n\n    const result = await correlator.correlate(farPastMetrics, socialEvents);\n\n    expect(result.correlationScore).toBe(0);\n    expect(result.socialAmplifiers).toHaveLength(0);\n    expect(result.correlatedMetrics).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n\n  it('fails fast when invoked with malformed data', async () => {\n    const correlator = new SentimentCorrelator();\n\n    // @ts-expect-error \u2013 intentionally passing invalid payload to test guard-rails\n    await expect(correlator.correlate(null, undefined)).rejects.toThrow(\n      /Invalid .* payload/i,\n    );\n  });\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                     \u2500\u2500 CapacityOrchestrator integration tests \u2500\u2500           */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ndescribe('CapacityOrchestrator', () => {\n  const mockSendCommand = jest.fn();\n  const orchestrator = new CapacityOrchestrator({\n    commandBus: {\n      send: mockSendCommand,\n    } as never, // Narrowing for test purposes\n    logger: Logger.child({ module: 'CapacityOrchestratorTest' }),\n  });\n\n  afterEach(() => {\n    jest.clearAllMocks();\n  });\n\n  it('emits a ScaleCommand when correlation score breaches SLA', async () => {\n    const mockCorrelation = {\n      correlationWindow: {\n        from: new Date(NOW.getTime() - 60_000),\n        to: NOW,\n      },\n      correlatedMetrics: sampleMetrics,\n      socialAmplifiers: socialEvents,\n      correlationScore: 0.92,\n    };\n\n    await orchestrator.evaluateAndAct(mockCorrelation);\n\n    expect(mockSendCommand).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);\n\n    const [command] = mockSendCommand.mock.calls[0];\n    expect(command).toBeInstanceOf(ScaleCommand);\n    expect((command as ScaleCommand).desiredReplicas).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n  });\n\n  it('does not emit ScaleCommand when system is within healthy bounds', async () => {\n    const healthyCorrelation = {\n      correlationWindow: {\n        from: new Date(NOW.getTime() - 60_000),\n        to: NOW,\n      },\n      correlatedMetrics: sampleMetrics,\n      socialAmplifiers: [],\n      correlationScore: 0.1, // Low score => normal ops\n    };\n\n    await orchestrator.evaluateAndAct(healthyCorrelation);\n\n    expect(mockSendCommand).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n  });\n\n  it('logs & bubbles up unexpected errors from downstream handlers', async () => {\n    const faultyCorrelation = {\n      correlationWindow: {\n        from: new Date(NOW.getTime() - 60_000),\n        to: NOW,\n      },\n      correlatedMetrics: sampleMetrics,\n      socialAmplifiers: socialEvents,\n      correlationScore: 0.88,\n    };\n\n    // Force the command bus to throw.\n    mockSendCommand.mockImplementationOnce(() => {\n      throw new Error('Kafka timeout');\n    });\n\n    await expect(orchestrator.evaluateAndAct(faultyCorrelation)).rejects.toThrow(\n      /Kafka timeout/,\n    );\n  });\n});\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/tests/test_main.ts",

{
  "file_path": "/app/project/tests/test_utils.ts"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**\n * tests/test_utils.ts\n *\n * A shared test-helper library for the PulseSphere SocialOps code-base.\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------\n * All Jest/Vitest test-suites should import from this module instead of\n * re-implementing ad-hoc mocks or helpers.  The utilities below are\n * crafted to cover a wide range of common testing scenarios inside\n * a large, event-driven, micro-service landscape:\n *\n *  1.  Synthetic telemetry & social-signal generators\n *  2.  An in-memory event-bus that mimics Kafka / NATS semantics\n *  3.  Async \u201ceventually\u201d expectations (handy for polling-based tests)\n *  4.  Console spies & environment isolation helpers\n *\n * The goal is to keep individual test-files lean and focused on domain\n * behaviour while centralising repetitive scaffolding concerns.\n */\n\nimport { EventEmitter } from 'events';\nimport { randomUUID } from 'crypto';\nimport { Mock, vi } from 'vitest'; // Works for Jest as well when using `jest` instead of `vi`\nimport ms from 'ms';\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                         Domain-level fixtures                       */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nexport type UserInteractionType =\n  | 'like'\n  | 'comment'\n  | 'share'\n  | 'view'\n  | 'reaction'\n  | 'follow';\n\nexport interface TelemetryEvent {\n  id: string;                // UUID v4\n  timestamp: number;         // epoch ms\n  service: string;           // \u201cfeed-api\u201d, \u201ctimeline-svc\u201d, \u2026\n  host: string;              // \u201cip-10-0-0-12\u201d\n  metric: string;            // \u201ccpu_usage\u201d, \u201clatency_p50\u201d\n  value: number;             // numeric value\n  tags: Record<string, string>;\n  userInteraction?: {\n    type: UserInteractionType;\n    delta: number;           // how many interactions happened in this frame\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate a realistic, deterministic (if seed passed) TelemetryEvent.\n */\nexport function createMockTelemetryEvent(\n  overrides: Partial<TelemetryEvent> = {},\n): TelemetryEvent {\n  const defaults: TelemetryEvent = {\n    id: randomUUID(),\n    timestamp: Date.now(),\n    service: `svc-${Math.floor(Math.random() * 10)}`,\n    host: `ip-10-0-0-${Math.floor(Math.random() * 255)}`,\n    metric: 'latency_p95',\n    value: Number((Math.random() * 800).toFixed(2)),\n    tags: {\n      env: 'test',\n      region: 'us-east-1',\n    },\n  };\n\n  // Randomly sprinkle a social interaction unless explicitly disabled\n  if (\n    overrides.userInteraction === undefined &&\n    Math.random() > 0.5\n  ) {\n    defaults.userInteraction = {\n      type: pickRandom<UserInteractionType>([\n        'like',\n        'comment',\n        'share',\n        'view',\n        'reaction',\n        'follow',\n      ]),\n      delta: Math.floor(Math.random() * 200),\n    };\n  }\n\n  return { ...defaults, ...overrides };\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                         Event-Bus test-double                       */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/**\n * Lightweight in-memory pub/sub implementation that imitates the most\n * essential semantics of Kafka/NATS used throughout PulseSphere.\n *\n *  - Topics are plain strings\n *  - Messages are delivered in-order (sync inside the same tick)\n *  - Back-pressure & re-delivery are *not* simulated\n */\nexport class InMemoryEventBus {\n  private emitter = new EventEmitter();\n\n  public publish<T>(topic: string, payload: T): void {\n    this.emitter.emit(topic, payload);\n  }\n\n  public subscribe<T>(\n    topic: string,\n    handler: (payload: T) => void | Promise<void>,\n  ): () => void {\n    this.emitter.on(topic, handler as any);\n\n    // Return unsubscribe fn\n    return () => this.emitter.off(topic, handler as any);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * For tests that need to wait until *all* listeners processed the\n   * published message (when handlers are async).\n   */\n  public async publishAndDrain<T>(\n    topic: string,\n    payload: T,\n  ): Promise<void> {\n    const listeners = this.emitter.listeners(topic);\n\n    const results = listeners.map(async (listener) => listener(payload));\n    this.emitter.emit(topic, payload);\n    // Wait until every handler has been awaited\n    await Promise.allSettled(results);\n  }\n\n  public clearAll(): void {\n    this.emitter.removeAllListeners();\n  }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                       Asynchronous expect helper                   */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/**\n * Waits until the supplied expectation callback stops throwing or the\n * timeout is reached. Helpful for eventual-consistency scenarios,\n * especially with message queues or retry loops.\n *\n * Example:\n *   await expectEventually(() => {\n *     expect(storage.get('myKey')).toBeDefined();\n *   });\n */\nexport async function expectEventually(\n  assertion: () => void | Promise<void>,\n  opts: {\n    timeout?: number | string;   // integer ms or \u201c1s\u201d, \u201c500ms\u201d\n    interval?: number | string;  // polling interval\n  } = {},\n): Promise<void> {\n  const timeout =\n    typeof opts.timeout === 'string'\n      ? ms(opts.timeout)\n      : opts.timeout ?? 2_000;\n  const interval =\n    typeof opts.interval === 'string'\n      ? ms(opts.interval)\n      : opts.interval ?? 50;\n\n  const startedAt = Date.now();\n  let lastErr: unknown;\n\n  // eslint-disable-next-line no-constant-condition\n  while (true) {\n    try {\n      await assertion();\n      return; // success \ud83c\udf89\n    } catch (err) {\n      lastErr = err;\n      const elapsed = Date.now() - startedAt;\n      if (elapsed >= timeout) {\n        throw lastErr;\n      }\n      await wait(interval);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nconst wait = (t: number) => new Promise((res) => setTimeout(res, t));\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                     Console & env isolation helpers                */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\ntype ConsoleMethod = 'log' | 'info' | 'warn' | 'error' | 'debug';\n\ninterface ConsoleSpies {\n  log: Mock;\n  info: Mock;\n  warn: Mock;\n  error: Mock;\n  debug: Mock;\n}\n\n/**\n * Silence console output for noisy tests *and* give you access to the\n * captured calls (returned mocks Reset via `restoreConsole`).\n */\nexport function mockConsole(): ConsoleSpies {\n  const original: Record<ConsoleMethod, (...args: any[]) => void> = {\n    log: console.log,\n    info: console.info,\n    warn: console.warn,\n    error: console.error,\n    debug: console.debug,\n  };\n\n  const spies = {} as ConsoleSpies;\n\n  (Object.keys(original) as ConsoleMethod[]).forEach((method) => {\n    // @ts-ignore \u2013 dynamic assignment is fine here\n    spies[method] = vi.fn();\n    console[method] = spies[method] as unknown as typeof console.log;\n  });\n\n  // Provide a restore hook on the spy object set\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment\n  // @ts-ignore\n  spies.restore = () => {\n    (Object.keys(original) as ConsoleMethod[]).forEach((method) => {\n      console[method] = original[method];\n    });\n  };\n\n  return spies;\n}\n\n/**\n * Temporarily override process.env keys for the duration of the test.\n *\n * Usage:\n *   const restoreEnv = withEnv({ NODE_ENV: 'production' });\n *   // ... run code\n *   restoreEnv(); // resets previous values\n */\nexport function withEnv(\n  envOverrides: NodeJS.ProcessEnv,\n): () => void {\n  const previous = { ...process.env };\n\n  Object.entries(envOverrides).forEach(([key, val]) => {\n    process.env[key] = val;\n  });\n\n  return () => {\n    process.env = previous;\n  };\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                             Test data pools                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/** A curated list of real-world endpoints we can reuse in tests */\nexport const SERVICE_NAMES = [\n  'user-svc',\n  'feed-api',\n  'search-edge',\n  'notification-worker',\n  'media-transcoder',\n  'analytics-ingestor',\n] as const;\n\n/** Some helper pick-random functions */\nexport function pickRandom<T>(array: ReadonlyArray<T>): T {\n  return array[Math.floor(Math.random() * array.length)];\n}\n\nexport function pickRandomService(): typeof SERVICE_NAMES[number] {\n  return pickRandom(SERVICE_NAMES);\n}\n\n/** Deep clone helper \u2013 useful when you want to ensure immutability */\nexport function deepClone<T>(obj: T): T {\n  return JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj));\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Default export \u2013 convenient aggregate                              */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nexport default {\n  createMockTelemetryEvent,\n  InMemoryEventBus,\n  expectEventually,\n  mockConsole,\n  withEnv,\n  pickRandom,\n  pickRandomService,\n  deepClone,\n};\n```\n",
    "filePath": "/app/project/tests/test_utils.ts",
    "numLines": 305,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 305
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_31.ts"

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**************************************************************************************************\n *  File:        src/module_31.ts\n *  Project:     PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 system_monitoring\n *  Description: Correlates low-level infrastructure metrics with real-time social-interaction\n *               signals in order to detect anomalies that are likely to impact user experience\n *               during viral traffic spikes.  The module listens to:\n *                 \u2022 Kafka   \u2013 infrastructure metrics topic\n *                 \u2022 NATS    \u2013 social-signal subject (likes/comments/shares/streams)\n *               and produces:\n *                 \u2022 Alert events via the internal AlertPublisher (fan-out to PagerDuty, Slack\u2026)\n *\n *  Architectural patterns showcased:\n *     \u2022 Observer Pattern \u2013 RxJS observable streams\n *     \u2022 Strategy Pattern \u2013 pluggable anomaly-detection algorithms\n *     \u2022 Chain-of-Responsibility \u2013 strategies executed until one raises an alert\n *\n *  NOTE: All concrete implementations are self-contained to keep the example runnable in\n *        isolation. Replace stubs with your own infrastructure-specific classes as needed.\n **************************************************************************************************/\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 External Dependencies \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nimport { Kafka, EachMessagePayload } from 'kafkajs';\nimport { connect, NatsConnection, Subscription } from 'nats';\nimport {\n  bufferTime,\n  filter,\n  map,\n  merge,\n  Observable,\n  Subject,\n  withLatestFrom,\n} from 'rxjs';\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Types \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nexport interface MetricEvent {\n  timestamp: number;       // epoch-ms\n  host: string;            // e.g. ip-10-1-1-42\n  metric: string;          // e.g. cpu_load\n  value: number;           // e.g. 0.88\n}\n\nexport interface SocialSignalEvent {\n  timestamp: number;       // epoch-ms\n  signal: 'like' | 'comment' | 'share' | 'view';\n  count: number;           // delta since previous sample\n  topic: string;           // hashtag / live-stream id / post id\n}\n\nexport interface CombinedEvent extends MetricEvent {\n  socialDelta: number;     // count of social-interaction deltas in the correlation window\n}\n\nexport interface AnomalyAlert {\n  timestamp: number;\n  severity: 'LOW' | 'MEDIUM' | 'HIGH';\n  reason: string;\n  meta: Record<string, unknown>;\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Logger (local stub) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nclass Logger {\n  constructor(private scope: string) {}\n  info(msg: string, ...rest: unknown[]): void  { console.info (`[INFO] [${this.scope}]`, msg, ...rest); }\n  warn(msg: string, ...rest: unknown[]): void  { console.warn (`[WARN] [${this.scope}]`, msg, ...rest); }\n  error(msg: string, ...rest: unknown[]): void { console.error(`[ERR ] [${this.scope}]`, msg, ...rest); }\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Alert Publisher (stub) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nclass AlertPublisher {\n  constructor(private readonly cfg: Config['alerting'], private readonly logger: Logger) {}\n\n  /**\n   * Fan-out alert to any configured integrations (PagerDuty, Slack, etc.).\n   * In production this should be async/non-blocking to avoid back-pressure on detectors.\n   */\n  async publish(alert: AnomalyAlert): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      // Replace console with real integrations (HTTP POST, gRPC, etc.)\n      this.logger.warn(`Dispatching alert \u21d2 ${alert.severity}: ${alert.reason}`, alert.meta);\n    } catch (err) {\n      this.logger.error('Failed to publish alert', err);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Runtime Configuration Loader \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nclass Config {\n  /* Centralised configuration shape */\n  public kafkaBrokers: string[];\n  public metricTopic: string;\n  public natsServers: string;\n  public socialSubject: string;\n\n  public bufferWindowMs: number;\n  public zScoreThreshold: number;\n  public zScoreWindow: number;\n  public cpuThreshold: number;\n  public memThreshold: number;\n\n  public alerting: {\n    slackWebhook?: string;\n    pagerDutyKey?: string;\n  };\n\n  /* Hydrate configuration from environment variables */\n  static loadFromEnv(): Config {\n    const cfg = new Config();\n    cfg.kafkaBrokers   = (process.env.PS_KAFKA_BROKERS ?? 'localhost:9092').split(',');\n    cfg.metricTopic    = process.env.PS_KAFKA_METRIC_TOPIC      ?? 'infra.metrics';\n    cfg.natsServers    = process.env.PS_NATS_SERVERS            ?? 'nats://localhost:4222';\n    cfg.socialSubject  = process.env.PS_NATS_SOCIAL_SUBJECT     ?? 'social.signals';\n\n    cfg.bufferWindowMs = +(process.env.PS_BUFFER_WINDOW_MS      ?? 15_000);\n    cfg.zScoreThreshold= +(process.env.PS_ZSCORE_THRESHOLD      ?? 3);\n    cfg.zScoreWindow   = +(process.env.PS_ZSCORE_WINDOW         ?? 20);\n    cfg.cpuThreshold   = +(process.env.PS_CPU_THRESHOLD         ?? 0.90);\n    cfg.memThreshold   = +(process.env.PS_MEM_THRESHOLD         ?? 0.90);\n\n    cfg.alerting = {\n      slackWebhook : process.env.PS_ALERT_SLACK_WEBHOOK,\n      pagerDutyKey : process.env.PS_ALERT_PD_KEY,\n    };\n\n    return cfg;\n  }\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Strategy-Pattern Interfaces \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\ninterface AnomalyDetectionStrategy {\n  /** Return list of alerts generated for the given time-window */\n  detect(window: ReadonlyArray<CombinedEvent>): AnomalyAlert[];\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Concrete Strategy: Z-Score (generic) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nclass ZScoreStrategy implements AnomalyDetectionStrategy {\n  private readonly history: number[] = [];\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly threshold: number, // e.g. 3 \u21d2 \u00b13\u03c3\n    private readonly windowSize: number,\n    private readonly logger: Logger,\n  ) {}\n\n  detect(window: ReadonlyArray<CombinedEvent>): AnomalyAlert[] {\n    const alerts: AnomalyAlert[] = [];\n\n    // Track only metric values; keep ring-buffer of last N\n    for (const ev of window) {\n      this.history.push(ev.value);\n      if (this.history.length > this.windowSize) this.history.shift();\n\n      if (this.history.length < this.windowSize) continue; // not enough data yet\n\n      const mean = this.history.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / this.history.length;\n      const variance =\n        this.history.reduce((a, b) => a + (b - mean) ** 2, 0) / this.history.length;\n      const stdDev = Math.sqrt(variance);\n\n      // Avoid divide-by-0\n      if (stdDev === 0) continue;\n\n      const zScore = (ev.value - mean) / stdDev;\n      if (Math.abs(zScore) >= this.threshold) {\n        // escalate severity if social delta is simultaneously high\n        const severity: AnomalyAlert['severity'] =\n          ev.socialDelta > 50\n            ? 'HIGH'\n            : Math.abs(zScore) > this.threshold * 1.5\n            ? 'MEDIUM'\n            : 'LOW';\n\n        alerts.push({\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          severity,\n          reason: `Metric ${ev.metric} on ${ev.host} outlier \u2013 z=${zScore.toFixed(\n            2,\n          )}, socialDelta=${ev.socialDelta}`,\n          meta: { host: ev.host, metric: ev.metric, value: ev.value, zScore },\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (alerts.length) {\n      this.logger.info(`Z-Score detected ${alerts.length} alert(s)`);\n    }\n    return alerts;\n  }\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Concrete Strategy: Static Thresholds w/ Social Amplification \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nclass ThresholdStrategy implements AnomalyDetectionStrategy {\n  constructor(\n    private readonly cpuThreshold: number,\n    private readonly memThreshold: number,\n    private readonly logger: Logger,\n  ) {}\n\n  detect(window: ReadonlyArray<CombinedEvent>): AnomalyAlert[] {\n    const alerts: AnomalyAlert[] = [];\n\n    for (const ev of window) {\n      if (\n        (ev.metric === 'cpu_load' && ev.value >= this.cpuThreshold) ||\n        (ev.metric === 'mem_util' && ev.value >= this.memThreshold)\n      ) {\n        const severity: AnomalyAlert['severity'] =\n          ev.socialDelta > 100 ? 'HIGH' : 'MEDIUM';\n\n        alerts.push({\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          severity,\n          reason: `Threshold exceeded: ${ev.metric}=${(ev.value * 100).toFixed(1)}%`,\n          meta: { host: ev.host, metric: ev.metric, value: ev.value, socialDelta: ev.socialDelta },\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (alerts.length) {\n      this.logger.info(`ThresholdStrategy detected ${alerts.length} alert(s)`);\n    }\n    return alerts;\n  }\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Detector (Delegates to Strategies) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nclass SocialSentimentAnomalyDetector {\n  constructor(\n    private readonly strategies: AnomalyDetectionStrategy[],\n    private readonly alertPublisher: AlertPublisher,\n    private readonly logger: Logger,\n  ) {}\n\n  async analyze(window: ReadonlyArray<CombinedEvent>): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      for (const strategy of this.strategies) {\n        const alerts = strategy.detect(window);\n        for (const alert of alerts) {\n          await this.alertPublisher.publish(alert);\n          // Chain-of-Responsibility: stop evaluating subsequent strategies if one fires a HIGH alert.\n          if (alert.severity === 'HIGH') return;\n        }\n      }\n    } catch (err) {\n      this.logger.error('Failed to analyse window', err);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Stream Bootstrapper \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nexport async function startSentimentAnomalyDetector(): Promise<void> {\n  const cfg     = Config.loadFromEnv();\n  const logger  = new Logger('SentimentDetector');\n  const alerts  = new AlertPublisher(cfg.alerting, logger);\n\n  /* \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013 Kafka (Infrastructure Metrics) \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013 */\n  const kafka         = new Kafka({ clientId: 'pulsesphere-metrics', brokers: cfg.kafkaBrokers });\n  const metricCons    = kafka.consumer({ groupId: 'pulsesphere-metrics-group' });\n  await metricCons.connect();\n  await metricCons.subscribe({ topic: cfg.metricTopic, fromBeginning: false });\n\n  const metricStream$ = new Subject<MetricEvent>();\n  metricCons.run({\n    autoCommit: true,\n    eachMessage: async ({ message }: EachMessagePayload) => {\n      if (!message.value) return;\n      try {\n        const evt = JSON.parse(message.value.toString()) as MetricEvent;\n        metricStream$.next(evt);\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error('Unable to parse metric event', err);\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  /* \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013 NATS (Social Interaction Signals) \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013 */\n  const natsConn: NatsConnection = await connect({ servers: cfg.natsServers.split(',') });\n  const socialSub: Subscription  = natsConn.subscribe(cfg.socialSubject);\n\n  const socialStream$ = new Subject<SocialSignalEvent>();\n  (async () => {\n    for await (const msg of socialSub) {\n      try {\n        socialStream$.next(JSON.parse(msg.data.toString()) as SocialSignalEvent);\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error('Unable to parse social signal', err);\n      }\n    }\n  })().catch((err) => logger.error('NATS subscription loop terminated', err));\n\n  /* \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013 Stream Correlation / Windowing \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013 */\n  const combined$ = metricStream$.pipe(\n    withLatestFrom(socialStream$),\n    map(([metric, social]): CombinedEvent => ({\n      ...metric,\n      socialDelta: social.count,\n    })),\n  );\n\n  const windowed$ = combined$.pipe(\n    bufferTime(cfg.bufferWindowMs),\n    filter((bucket) => bucket.length > 0),\n  );\n\n  /* \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013 Instantiate Detector & Strategies \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013 */\n  const detector = new SocialSentimentAnomalyDetector(\n    [\n      new ZScoreStrategy(cfg.zScoreThreshold, cfg.zScoreWindow, logger),\n      new ThresholdStrategy(cfg.cpuThreshold, cfg.memThreshold, logger),\n    ],\n    alerts,\n    logger,\n  );\n\n  windowed$.subscribe({\n    next: (window) => detector.analyze(window),\n    error: (err) => logger.error('Windowed stream error', err),\n  });\n\n  process.on('SIGINT', async () => {\n    logger.info('Gracefully shutting down detector\u2026');\n    await metricCons.disconnect().catch(() => void 0);\n    natsConn.close();\n    process.exit(0);\n  });\n\n  logger.info('SocialSentimentAnomalyDetector started \u2014 monitoring streams.');\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Auto-start when executed directly \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nif (require.main === module) {\n  // tslint:disable-next-line: no-floating-promises\n  startSentimentAnomalyDetector().catch((err) => {\n    // eslint-disable-next-line no-console\n    console.error('[FATAL] Failed to bootstrap SocialSentimentAnomalyDetector', err);\n    process.exit(1);\n  });\n}\n```\n",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_31.ts",
    "numLines": 340,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 340
  },
  "type": "text"

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps\n * -------------------------------------------------------\n * Module: module_17 \u2013 Social-Aware Auto-Scaler & Alerting\n *\n * This module wires together multiple classic patterns to\n * provide a production-grade, socially-aware auto-scaling\n * component.\n *\n * Patterns in use\n *  \u2022 Observer            \u2013 MetricEventBus (EventEmitter)\n *  \u2022 Strategy            \u2013 ScalingStrategy implementations\n *  \u2022 Command             \u2013 Scale{Up,Down}Command\n *  \u2022 Chain-of-Responsibility \u2013 AlertHandler chain\n *  \u2022 Event Driven        \u2013 Kafka consumer feeding the bus\n *\n * External deps\n *  \u2022 kafkajs             \u2013 High-throughput metric ingestion\n *  \u2022 axios               \u2013 Mock service-mesh HTTP client\n *\n * Author:  PulseSphere SRE Platform Team\n * -------------------------------------------------------\n */\n\nimport { EventEmitter } from 'events';\nimport { Kafka, EachMessagePayload, logLevel as KafkaLogLevel } from 'kafkajs';\nimport axios, { AxiosInstance } from 'axios';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                               Static typings                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/** A single enriched metric coming from pulse-fusion pipeline. */\nexport interface EnrichedMetric {\n  readonly appId: string;\n  readonly cpuPct: number;               // 0-100\n  readonly memPct: number;               // 0-100\n  readonly sentimentScore: number;       // \u20131 .. +1\n  readonly trendingScore: number;        // 0 .. 1 (probability of virality)\n  readonly timestamp: number;            // epoch ms\n}\n\nexport enum ScalingDecision {\n  SCALE_UP   = 'SCALE_UP',\n  SCALE_DOWN = 'SCALE_DOWN',\n  STABLE     = 'STABLE',\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                               Observer Layer                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Central in-process event bus \u2013 translates Kafka messages into local events\n * that strategies/handlers can subscribe to without needing Kafka semantics.\n */\nclass MetricEventBus extends EventEmitter {\n  static readonly EVT_METRIC = 'metric';\n\n  emitMetric(metric: EnrichedMetric): void {\n    this.emit(MetricEventBus.EVT_METRIC, metric);\n  }\n\n  onMetric(listener: (metric: EnrichedMetric) => void): void {\n    this.on(MetricEventBus.EVT_METRIC, listener);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                           Strategy & Command Layer                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/** Scaling command contract (Command Pattern). */\ninterface ScalingCommand {\n  execute(): Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/** Concrete command \u2013 scale up by N replicas. */\nclass ScaleUpCommand implements ScalingCommand {\n  constructor(\n    private readonly meshClient: ServiceMeshClient,\n    private readonly appId: string,\n    private readonly factor: number,\n  ) {}\n\n  public async execute(): Promise<void> {\n    await this.meshClient.scale(this.appId, this.factor);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Concrete command \u2013 scale down by N replicas. */\nclass ScaleDownCommand implements ScalingCommand {\n  constructor(\n    private readonly meshClient: ServiceMeshClient,\n    private readonly appId: string,\n    private readonly factor: number,\n  ) {}\n\n  public async execute(): Promise<void> {\n    await this.meshClient.scale(this.appId, -this.factor);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Strategy contract \u2013 returns a Command or undefined when no action. */\ninterface ScalingStrategy {\n  evaluate(metric: EnrichedMetric): ScalingCommand | undefined;\n}\n\n/**\n * CPU-centric strategy \u2013 reacts when usage is beyond a threshold.\n * This is a simplistic threshold-based algorithm; production code\n * would rely on predictive analytics or RL models.\n */\nclass HighCpuStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  constructor(\n    private readonly meshClient: ServiceMeshClient,\n    private readonly highWaterMarkPct = 75,\n    private readonly lowWaterMarkPct  = 35,\n  ) {}\n\n  evaluate(metric: EnrichedMetric): ScalingCommand | undefined {\n    if (metric.cpuPct >= this.highWaterMarkPct) {\n      const factor = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(metric.cpuPct / 25));\n      return new ScaleUpCommand(this.meshClient, metric.appId, factor);\n    }\n    if (metric.cpuPct <= this.lowWaterMarkPct) {\n      return new ScaleDownCommand(this.meshClient, metric.appId, 1);\n    }\n    return undefined;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Sentiment spike strategy \u2013 anticipates virality before infrastructure\n * gets hammered. Combines sentiment & trending scores.\n */\nclass SentimentSpikeStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  constructor(\n    private readonly meshClient: ServiceMeshClient,\n    private readonly threshold = 0.85,\n  ) {}\n\n  evaluate(metric: EnrichedMetric): ScalingCommand | undefined {\n    if (metric.trendingScore >= this.threshold && metric.sentimentScore > 0) {\n      // For viral events we aggressively over-provision.\n      return new ScaleUpCommand(this.meshClient, metric.appId, 3);\n    }\n    return undefined;\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                          Chain-of-Responsibility Layer                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ninterface AlertHandler {\n  setNext(handler: AlertHandler): AlertHandler;\n  handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void>;\n}\n\nabstract class BaseAlertHandler implements AlertHandler {\n  private nextHandler?: AlertHandler;\n\n  public setNext(handler: AlertHandler): AlertHandler {\n    this.nextHandler = handler;\n    return handler;\n  }\n\n  public async handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void> {\n    if (this.nextHandler) {\n      await this.nextHandler.handle(metric);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/** Debug-level handler \u2013 just noisy logs in dev environments. */\nclass DebugAlertHandler extends BaseAlertHandler {\n  async handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void> {\n    if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production') {\n      console.debug(\n        `[DEBUG] Metric \u2192 cpu:${metric.cpuPct}% mem:${metric.memPct}% ` +\n        `sentiment:${metric.sentimentScore.toFixed(2)} trending:${metric.trendingScore}`,\n      );\n    }\n    await super.handle(metric);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Warning-level handler \u2013 triggers standard alert channel. */\nclass WarningAlertHandler extends BaseAlertHandler {\n  async handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void> {\n    if (metric.cpuPct > 85 || metric.memPct > 85) {\n      await AlertChannel.slack(\n        `\u26a0\ufe0f High resource usage on app ${metric.appId}: ` +\n        `CPU ${metric.cpuPct}% / MEM ${metric.memPct}%`,\n      );\n    }\n    await super.handle(metric);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Critical handler \u2013 immediate pager-duty. */\nclass CriticalAlertHandler extends BaseAlertHandler {\n  async handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void> {\n    const isCrit = metric.cpuPct > 95 || metric.memPct > 95 || metric.trendingScore >= 0.95;\n    if (isCrit) {\n      await AlertChannel.pagerDuty(\n        `\ud83d\udea8 CRITICAL: ${metric.appId} likely overloaded! ` +\n        `cpu=${metric.cpuPct}% mem=${metric.memPct}% trending=${metric.trendingScore}`,\n      );\n    }\n    await super.handle(metric);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Service-mesh client                           */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nclass ServiceMeshClient {\n  private readonly http: AxiosInstance;\n\n  constructor(\n    readonly baseURL: string = process.env.SERVICE_MESH_BASE_URL ?? 'http://mesh-gateway',\n    readonly timeout = 5_000,\n  ) {\n    this.http = axios.create({ baseURL: this.baseURL, timeout: this.timeout });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Scale an application up/down by \"factor\" replicas.\n   * This interacts with the platform orchestrator through\n   * the mesh sidecar.\n   */\n  public async scale(appId: string, factor: number): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      await this.http.post(`/apps/${appId}/scale`, { factor });\n      console.info(`[Scale] ${appId} scaled by ${factor}`);\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.error(`[Scale] Failed to scale ${appId}:`, err);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Alerting helpers                              */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nclass AlertChannel {\n  static async slack(message: string): Promise<void> {\n    // Placeholder: in production we'd push to Slack webhook\n    console.log(`[Slack] ${message}`);\n  }\n\n  static async pagerDuty(message: string): Promise<void> {\n    // Placeholder: integration key & routing will sit in vault\n    console.log(`[PagerDuty] ${message}`);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                            Main Orchestrator Class                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport class SocialAwareAutoScaler {\n  private readonly kafka: Kafka;\n  private readonly meshClient = new ServiceMeshClient();\n  private readonly eventBus = new MetricEventBus();\n  private readonly strategies: ScalingStrategy[] = [];\n  private readonly alertChain: AlertHandler;\n  private isShuttingDown = false;\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly kafkaBrokers: string[] = (process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS ?? 'localhost:9092').split(','),\n    private readonly groupId = 'social-auto-scaler',\n    private readonly topic = 'pulse.metric.enriched.v1',\n  ) {\n    this.kafka = new Kafka({\n      clientId: 'pulse-auto-scaler',\n      brokers: this.kafkaBrokers,\n      logLevel: KafkaLogLevel.NOTHING,\n    });\n\n    /* Strategy registry */\n    this.strategies.push(\n      new HighCpuStrategy(this.meshClient),\n      new SentimentSpikeStrategy(this.meshClient),\n    );\n\n    /* Alert chain bootstrap */\n    const debug   = new DebugAlertHandler();\n    const warn    = new WarningAlertHandler();\n    const crit    = new CriticalAlertHandler();\n    debug.setNext(warn).setNext(crit);\n    this.alertChain = debug; // entrypoint\n\n    /* Wiring observer */\n    this.eventBus.onMetric(async (metric) => {\n      await this.alertChain.handle(metric);\n      await this.applyStrategies(metric);\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Initialize kafka consumer & begin event pump.\n   */\n  public async start(): Promise<void> {\n    const consumer = this.kafka.consumer({ groupId: this.groupId });\n    await consumer.connect();\n    await consumer.subscribe({ topic: this.topic, fromBeginning: false });\n\n    console.info('[AutoScaler] started.');\n\n    await consumer.run({\n      eachMessage: async (payload: EachMessagePayload) => {\n        if (this.isShuttingDown) {\n          await consumer.disconnect();\n          return;\n        }\n        const metric = this.parseMetric(payload);\n        if (metric) this.eventBus.emitMetric(metric);\n      },\n    });\n\n    /* Graceful shutdown binding */\n    process.once('SIGINT' , () => this.shutdown());\n    process.once('SIGTERM', () => this.shutdown());\n  }\n\n  private async applyStrategies(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void> {\n    for (const strategy of this.strategies) {\n      const cmd = strategy.evaluate(metric);\n      if (cmd) {\n        try {\n          await cmd.execute();\n          break; // one strategy acted \u2013 avoid conflicting actions\n        } catch (err) {\n          console.error('[AutoScaler] Command failed:', err);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Converts Kafka payload into EnrichedMetric object with validation.\n   */\n  private parseMetric({ message }: EachMessagePayload): EnrichedMetric | undefined {\n    try {\n      if (!message.value) return;\n      const obj = JSON.parse(message.value.toString());\n\n      /* Lightweight validation */\n      const metric: EnrichedMetric = {\n        appId          : obj.appId,\n        cpuPct         : Number(obj.cpuPct),\n        memPct         : Number(obj.memPct),\n        sentimentScore : Number(obj.sentimentScore),\n        trendingScore  : Number(obj.trendingScore),\n        timestamp      : Number(obj.timestamp),\n      };\n\n      if (!metric.appId || Number.isNaN(metric.cpuPct)) {\n        throw new Error('Invalid metric shape');\n      }\n      return metric;\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.warn('[AutoScaler] Discarded malformed metric:', err);\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Shutdown hook \u2013 ensures consumer disconnect & drains inflight tasks.\n   */\n  private async shutdown(): Promise<void> {\n    if (this.isShuttingDown) return;\n    this.isShuttingDown = true;\n\n    console.info('[AutoScaler] Shutting down\u2026');\n    // Additional cleanup (HTTP, etc.) could be awaited here.\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                     Module bootstrap (only when run directly)              */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nif (require.main === module) {\n  (async () => {\n    try {\n      const scaler = new SocialAwareAutoScaler();\n      await scaler.start();\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.error('[AutoScaler] Fatal:', err);\n      process.exitCode = 1;\n    }\n  })();\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_17.ts",
    "numLines": 399,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 399
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
