Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/******************************************************************************************\n *  File:        src/module_33.ts\n *  Project:     PulseSphere SocialOps (system_monitoring)\n *\n *  Description:\n *  Social-Aware Auto-Scaler micro-service. Listens to infrastructure telemetry (CPU/memory)\n *  from Apache Kafka, listens to near-real-time social-interaction signals from NATS,\n *  merges the two streams and decides\u2014via configurable Strategy pattern\u2014whether capacity\n *  should be scaled up/down. If a scaling decision is made it emits a Command event back\n *  to the message bus. Additionally, it forwards critical alerts through a simple\n *  Chain-of-Responsibility notifier pipeline (Slack \u2192 PagerDuty \u2192 Log).\n *\n *  NOTE: 3rd-party SDKs are thinly wrapped to allow mocking in unit tests; production\n *  credentials and topics/subjects are injected via env-vars or Secret-Manager.\n ******************************************************************************************/\n\n/* ================================== Imports ========================================= */\n\nimport { Kafka, Producer, EachMessagePayload } from 'kafkajs';\nimport { connect as natsConnect, NatsConnection, Subscription, JSONCodec } from 'nats';\nimport pino, { Logger } from 'pino';\nimport { v4 as uuid } from 'uuid';\n\n/* ============================== Domain & Contracts ================================== */\n\n/**\n * Represents infrastructure telemetry pulled from Prometheus-push gateway (simplified).\n */\nexport interface SystemMetric {\n  timestamp: number;              // unix epoch ms\n  instanceId: string;             // k8s node or pod id\n  cpuLoadPct: number;             // e.g., 0.73 means 73%\n  memoryLoadPct: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Represents social-interaction spikes (likes, comments, shares, etc.)\n */\nexport interface SocialSignal {\n  timestamp: number;\n  campaignId?: string;\n  hashtag?: string;\n  deltaPerSecond: number;         // increase of interactions per sec\n}\n\n/**\n * Consolidated view that Strategies will look at.\n */\nexport interface CombinedMetrics {\n  system: SystemMetric;\n  social?: SocialSignal;\n}\n\n/**\n * Command event that goes onto Kafka for Orchestration service.\n */\nexport interface ScalingCommand {\n  commandId: string;\n  issuedAt: number;\n  targetGroup: string;            // e.g., \"video-processor-pods\"\n  action: 'SCALE_UP' | 'SCALE_DOWN';\n  desiredReplicas: number;\n  reason: string;\n}\n\n/* =========================== Strategy Pattern ======================================= */\n\nexport interface ScalingStrategy {\n  readonly name: string;\n  evaluate(input: CombinedMetrics): ScalingCommand | null;\n}\n\n/**\n * 1. Pure CPU strategy\u2014scale if CPU surpasses threshold.\n */\nexport class CpuLoadStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  readonly name = 'CpuLoadStrategy';\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly cpuUpperThreshold = 0.8,\n    private readonly cpuLowerThreshold = 0.25,\n    private readonly maxReplicas = 16,\n    private readonly minReplicas = 2\n  ) {}\n\n  evaluate(input: CombinedMetrics): ScalingCommand | null {\n    const { cpuLoadPct, instanceId } = input.system;\n    let action: ScalingCommand['action'] | null = null;\n    let desired: number | null = null;\n\n    if (cpuLoadPct >= this.cpuUpperThreshold) {\n      action = 'SCALE_UP';\n      desired = Math.min(this.maxReplicas, Math.ceil(cpuLoadPct * 10));\n    } else if (cpuLoadPct <= this.cpuLowerThreshold) {\n      action = 'SCALE_DOWN';\n      desired = Math.max(this.minReplicas, Math.floor(cpuLoadPct * 10));\n    }\n\n    if (action && desired !== null) {\n      return {\n        commandId: uuid(),\n        issuedAt: Date.now(),\n        targetGroup: instanceId,\n        action,\n        desiredReplicas: desired,\n        reason: `CPU load ${cpuLoadPct * 100}% triggered ${action}`\n      };\n    }\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * 2. Social trend strategy\u2014scale on viral spikes regardless of current CPU.\n */\nexport class SocialTrendingStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  readonly name = 'SocialTrendingStrategy';\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly spikeThreshold = 5000,        // interactions/sec\n    private readonly surgeReplicas = 8\n  ) {}\n\n  evaluate(input: CombinedMetrics): ScalingCommand | null {\n    if (!input.social) return null;\n    const { deltaPerSecond, hashtag } = input.social;\n    if (deltaPerSecond >= this.spikeThreshold) {\n      return {\n        commandId: uuid(),\n        issuedAt: Date.now(),\n        targetGroup: hashtag ?? 'global',\n        action: 'SCALE_UP',\n        desiredReplicas: this.surgeReplicas,\n        reason: `Social spike (${deltaPerSecond}/s) for #${hashtag}`\n      };\n    }\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * 3. Hybrid strategy\u2014showcases Strategy composition.\n */\nexport class HybridStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  readonly name = 'HybridStrategy';\n  private readonly innerStrategies: ScalingStrategy[];\n\n  constructor(...strategies: ScalingStrategy[]) {\n    this.innerStrategies = strategies;\n  }\n\n  evaluate(input: CombinedMetrics): ScalingCommand | null {\n    for (const strat of this.innerStrategies) {\n      const cmd = strat.evaluate(input);\n      if (cmd) return cmd;\n    }\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/* =================== Chain-of-Responsibility for Alerting =========================== */\n\ninterface AlertHandler {\n  setNext(handler: AlertHandler): AlertHandler;\n  handle(message: string): Promise<void>;\n}\n\nabstract class AbstractHandler implements AlertHandler {\n  protected nextHandler?: AlertHandler;\n  setNext(handler: AlertHandler): AlertHandler {\n    this.nextHandler = handler;\n    return handler;\n  }\n  async handle(message: string): Promise<void> {\n    if (this.nextHandler) {\n      await this.nextHandler.handle(message);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nclass SlackNotifierHandler extends AbstractHandler {\n  constructor(private readonly logger: Logger) { super(); }\n  async handle(message: string): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      // Placeholder for Slack webhook call\n      this.logger.info({ msg: 'SlackNotifier', message });\n      // Success, stop chain.\n    } catch (err) {\n      this.logger.error({ err, msg: 'SlackNotifierFailed' });\n      await super.handle(message);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nclass PagerDutyHandler extends AbstractHandler {\n  constructor(private readonly logger: Logger) { super(); }\n  async handle(message: string): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      // Placeholder for PagerDuty event trigger\n      this.logger.info({ msg: 'PagerDutyNotifier', message });\n    } catch (err) {\n      this.logger.error({ err, msg: 'PagerDutyNotifierFailed' });\n      await super.handle(message);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nclass FallbackLoggerHandler extends AbstractHandler {\n  constructor(private readonly logger: Logger) { super(); }\n  async handle(message: string): Promise<void> {\n    this.logger.warn({ msg: 'FallbackLogger', message });\n    await super.handle(message);\n  }\n}\n\n/* ============================ Auto-Scaler Service =================================== */\n\nexport class SocialAwareAutoScaler {\n  private readonly kafka: Kafka;\n  private readonly kafkaProducer: Producer;\n  private readonly natsConn: NatsConnection;\n  private readonly jc = JSONCodec();\n\n  private readonly logger: Logger;\n  private readonly strategy: ScalingStrategy;\n\n  private slackHandler: AlertHandler;\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly kafkaBrokers: string[],\n    private readonly natsUrl: string,\n    strategy?: ScalingStrategy\n  ) {\n    this.logger = pino({ name: 'SocialAwareAutoScaler' });\n    this.kafka = new Kafka({ clientId: 'auto-scaler', brokers: kafkaBrokers });\n    this.kafkaProducer = this.kafka.producer();\n    this.strategy = strategy ?? new HybridStrategy(\n      new SocialTrendingStrategy(),\n      new CpuLoadStrategy()\n    );\n    this.natsConn = {} as any; // will connect later\n\n    // Alert pipeline\n    this.slackHandler = new SlackNotifierHandler(this.logger);\n    this.slackHandler\n      .setNext(new PagerDutyHandler(this.logger))\n      .setNext(new FallbackLoggerHandler(this.logger));\n  }\n\n  /* ---------- Bootstrapping ---------- */\n\n  async start(): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      await this.kafkaProducer.connect();\n      this.logger.info('Kafka producer connected');\n\n      // Connect to NATS\n      this.natsConn = await natsConnect({ servers: this.natsUrl });\n      this.logger.info('NATS connected');\n\n      // Subscribe to streams\n      await this.subscribeSystemMetrics();\n      await this.subscribeSocialSignals();\n\n      this.logger.info(`Auto-Scaler started with strategy ${this.strategy.name}`);\n    } catch (err) {\n      this.logger.fatal({ err }, 'Failed to start Auto-Scaler');\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n  }\n\n  async shutdown(): Promise<void> {\n    await Promise.allSettled([this.kafkaProducer.disconnect(), this.natsConn.close()]);\n    this.logger.info('Graceful shutdown complete.');\n  }\n\n  /* ---------- Internal Subscription Logic ---------- */\n\n  private async subscribeSystemMetrics(): Promise<void> {\n    const consumer = this.kafka.consumer({ groupId: 'auto-scaler-group' });\n    await consumer.connect();\n    await consumer.subscribe({ topic: 'telemetry.system', fromBeginning: false });\n\n    consumer.run({\n      eachMessage: async (payload: EachMessagePayload) => {\n        try {\n          const metric: SystemMetric = JSON.parse(payload.message.value!.toString());\n\n          // Merge with any cached social signal if available\n          const combined: CombinedMetrics = { system: metric, social: this.lastSocialSignal };\n\n          await this.maybeScale(combined);\n        } catch (err) {\n          this.logger.error({ err }, 'Failed to handle system metric message');\n        }\n      }\n    });\n  }\n\n  private lastSocialSignal?: SocialSignal;\n  private async subscribeSocialSignals(): Promise<void> {\n    const sub: Subscription = this.natsConn.subscribe('social.trending');\n    (async () => {\n      for await (const msg of sub) {\n        try {\n          const signal: SocialSignal = this.jc.decode(msg.data);\n          this.lastSocialSignal = signal; // simple cache\n          const dummySystem: SystemMetric = {\n            timestamp: signal.timestamp,\n            instanceId: 'social-only',\n            cpuLoadPct: 0,\n            memoryLoadPct: 0\n          };\n          await this.maybeScale({ system: dummySystem, social: signal });\n\n        } catch (err) {\n          this.logger.error({ err }, 'Failed to handle social signal message');\n        }\n      }\n    })().catch(err => this.logger.error({ err }, 'NATS subscription failed'));\n  }\n\n  /* ---------- Core Decision Loop ---------- */\n\n  private async maybeScale(input: CombinedMetrics): Promise<void> {\n    const cmd = this.strategy.evaluate(input);\n    if (cmd) {\n      this.logger.info({ cmd }, 'Scaling decision issued');\n\n      try {\n        await this.kafkaProducer.send({\n          topic: 'orchestration.scaling.command',\n          messages: [{ key: cmd.targetGroup, value: JSON.stringify(cmd) }]\n        });\n      } catch (err) {\n        this.logger.error({ err }, 'Failed to publish scaling command');\n        await this.slackHandler.handle(`Scaling command failed: ${err}`);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* ============================= Run if main ========================================== */\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  const scaler = new SocialAwareAutoScaler(\n    process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS?.split(',') ?? ['localhost:9092'],\n    process.env.NATS_URL ?? 'nats://127.0.0.1:4222'\n  );\n\n  scaler.start();\n\n  // Graceful shutdown\n  process.on('SIGINT', async () => {\n    await scaler.shutdown();\n    process.exit(0);\n  });\n}\n```",

    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 360
  },
  "type": "text"

{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/***************************************************************************************************\n *  File:          src/module_22.ts\n *  Project:       PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 system_monitoring\n *  Description:   Trending-event correlation + auto-scaling engine.\n *\n *  This module listens to enriched social-interaction telemetry (Kafka topic: social_metrics),\n *  detects virality events in real time, predicts the impact on infrastructure capacity and\n *  triggers remediation via a pluggable Strategy + Chain-of-Responsibility implementation.\n *\n *  Patterns showcased\n *  \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  \u2022 Strategy Pattern               \u2013 ScalingStrategy (HPA, Keda, CustomAutoscaler)\n *  \u2022 Chain-of-Responsibility        \u2013 RemediationHandler (Scaler \u2192 Alerting \u2192 Fallback)\n *  \u2022 Observer Pattern               \u2013 Simple EventEmitter-based listener notification\n *  \u2022 Dependency-Inversion Principle \u2013 high-level policies depend on abstractions, not concretions\n *\n *  External Deps: kafkajs, pino, node-eventemitter-3, @types/node (for timers / events)\n ***************************************************************************************************/\n\nimport { Kafka, Consumer, EachMessagePayload } from 'kafkajs';\nimport EventEmitter from 'events';\nimport pino from 'pino';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                               Shared Typings                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Raw event emitted by Social-Interaction stream\n */\ninterface SocialMetricEvent {\n  ts: number;                // epoch millis\n  hashtag: string;           // primary hashtag detected in message\n  likes: number;             // #likes within aggregation window\n  comments: number;          // #comments within aggregation window\n  shares: number;            // #shares within aggregation window\n  viewers: number;           // concurrent live-stream viewers\n}\n\n/**\n * Calculated metadata that augments SocialMetricEvent\n */\ninterface CorrelatedSignal {\n  viralityScore: number;     // Weighted score used to anticipate traffic surge\n  projectedRPS: number;      // Predicted requests-per-second that backend will receive\n}\n\n/**\n * Result produced by a ScalingStrategy. Nullable fields are allowed when\n * strategy deems no action is necessary.\n */\ninterface ScalingDecision {\n  /**\n   * Desired replica count for target deployment (e.g., Kubernetes Deployment)\n   */\n  desiredReplicas: number | null;\n\n  /**\n   * Estimated cost (useful for A/B strategy benchmarking)\n   */\n  expectedCostUSD?: number;\n\n  /**\n   * Optional metadata describing reasoning\n   */\n  rationale?: string;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                          Logging / Observability utils                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nconst logger = pino({\n  name: 'virality-correlator',\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL || 'info',\n  transport:\n    process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'\n      ? { target: 'pino-pretty', options: { colorize: true } }\n      : undefined,\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Observer Pattern                              */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Event bus for internal notifications within this micro-service\n */\nclass InternalBus extends EventEmitter {\n  static readonly EVENTS = {\n    VIRALITY_DETECTED: 'virality_detected',\n    SCALE_DECISION: 'scale_decision',\n    REMEDIATION_FAILED: 'remediation_failed',\n  } as const;\n}\n\nconst bus = new InternalBus();\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                             Strategy Pattern                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Defines contract for a capacity scaling algorithm\n */\ninterface ScalingStrategy {\n  name: string;\n  assess(signal: CorrelatedSignal): Promise<ScalingDecision>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Baseline strategy: HorizontalPodAutoscaler-like rule engine\n */\nclass HPAStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  public readonly name = 'hpa';\n\n  constructor(\n    private minReplicas: number,\n    private maxReplicas: number,\n    private rpsPerReplica: number,\n  ) {}\n\n  async assess(signal: CorrelatedSignal): Promise<ScalingDecision> {\n    const { projectedRPS } = signal;\n    const desired = Math.ceil(projectedRPS / this.rpsPerReplica);\n\n    if (desired <= this.minReplicas) {\n      return { desiredReplicas: this.minReplicas, rationale: 'Within min bounds' };\n    }\n    if (desired >= this.maxReplicas) {\n      return {\n        desiredReplicas: this.maxReplicas,\n        rationale: 'Capped at max replicas',\n      };\n    }\n    return { desiredReplicas: desired, rationale: 'Scaled linearly against RPS' };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Cost-aware custom strategy which chooses between spot and on-demand nodes\n */\nclass CostOptimizedStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  public readonly name = 'cost_optimized';\n\n  constructor(private baseline: HPAStrategy, private costPerReplica: number) {}\n\n  async assess(signal: CorrelatedSignal): Promise<ScalingDecision> {\n    const base = await this.baseline.assess(signal);\n    if (base.desiredReplicas === null) {\n      return base;\n    }\n    const cost = base.desiredReplicas * this.costPerReplica;\n    return {\n      ...base,\n      expectedCostUSD: cost,\n      rationale: `${base.rationale}. Estimated cost $${cost.toFixed(2)}`,\n    };\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                       Chain-of-Responsibility Pattern                       */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Abstract handler capable of executing/handling remediation action\n */\nabstract class RemediationHandler {\n  private nextHandler: RemediationHandler | null = null;\n\n  setNext(next: RemediationHandler): RemediationHandler {\n    this.nextHandler = next;\n    return next;\n  }\n\n  async handle(signal: CorrelatedSignal, decision: ScalingDecision): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      const handled = await this.process(signal, decision);\n      if (!handled && this.nextHandler) {\n        await this.nextHandler.handle(signal, decision);\n      }\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error(err, 'Remediation handler threw error');\n      bus.emit(InternalBus.EVENTS.REMEDIATION_FAILED, err);\n      if (this.nextHandler) {\n        await this.nextHandler.handle(signal, decision);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  protected abstract process(\n    signal: CorrelatedSignal,\n    decision: ScalingDecision,\n  ): Promise<boolean>;\n}\n\n/**\n * First link: Perform actual scaling action via Kubernetes API\n */\nclass KubernetesScaler extends RemediationHandler {\n  constructor(private readonly kubeClient: IKubeClient, private readonly deployment: string) {\n    super();\n  }\n\n  protected async process(\n    _signal: CorrelatedSignal,\n    decision: ScalingDecision,\n  ): Promise<boolean> {\n    if (decision.desiredReplicas === null) return false;\n\n    logger.info(\n      { deployment: this.deployment, replicas: decision.desiredReplicas },\n      'Scaling deployment',\n    );\n\n    // Safeguard: avoid flapping\n    const current = await this.kubeClient.getReplicaCount(this.deployment);\n    if (current === decision.desiredReplicas) {\n      logger.debug('Desired replica count already satisfied; skipping scale action.');\n      return true;\n    }\n\n    await this.kubeClient.scaleDeployment(this.deployment, decision.desiredReplicas);\n    return true; // handled!\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Second link: Send alert via PagerDuty if scaling failed / not possible\n */\nclass PagerDutyNotifier extends RemediationHandler {\n  constructor(private readonly pdClient: IPagerDutyClient) {\n    super();\n  }\n\n  protected async process(\n    signal: CorrelatedSignal,\n    decision: ScalingDecision,\n  ): Promise<boolean> {\n    const sev = decision.desiredReplicas === null ? 'info' : 'warning';\n    const message =\n      decision.desiredReplicas === null\n        ? 'No scaling required.'\n        : `Scaling unsuccessful or insufficient (desired ${decision.desiredReplicas})`;\n\n    await this.pdClient.triggerIncident({\n      severity: sev,\n      summary: `Virality surge for #${signal}. ${message}`,\n    });\n    logger.warn({ severity: sev }, 'PagerDuty incident triggered.');\n    return true; // always consider handled to prevent endless loop\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Optional final link: Fallback logging (noop) \u2013 acts as a sink\n */\nclass NullRemediationHandler extends RemediationHandler {\n  protected async process(): Promise<boolean> {\n    logger.info('Reached end of remediation chain. No further action.');\n    return true;\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                               Clients stubs                                */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n *  In production these would be imported from shared infra packages.\n *  Types kept minimal for brevity.\n */\n\ninterface IKubeClient {\n  getReplicaCount(deployment: string): Promise<number>;\n  scaleDeployment(deployment: string, replicas: number): Promise<void>;\n}\n\ninterface IPagerDutyClient {\n  triggerIncident(payload: { severity: string; summary: string }): Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Virality correlation service                       */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nclass ViralityCorrelatorService {\n  private consumer: Consumer;\n  private readonly strategy: ScalingStrategy;\n  private readonly remediationChain: RemediationHandler;\n\n  constructor(\n    kafka: Kafka,\n    strategy: ScalingStrategy,\n    remediationChain: RemediationHandler,\n    private readonly topic = 'social_metrics',\n  ) {\n    this.strategy = strategy;\n    this.remediationChain = remediationChain;\n    this.consumer = kafka.consumer({ groupId: `virality-correlator-${strategy.name}` });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Initialize Kafka consumer and internal listeners\n   */\n  async start(): Promise<void> {\n    await this.consumer.connect();\n    await this.consumer.subscribe({ topic: this.topic, fromBeginning: false });\n\n    this.consumer.run({\n      eachMessage: async (payload) => this.handleMessage(payload).catch(this.handleError),\n    });\n\n    bus.on(InternalBus.EVENTS.REMEDIATION_FAILED, (err) => {\n      logger.error({ err }, 'Remediation failed \u2013 escalate if necessary');\n    });\n\n    logger.info('Virality correlator service started.');\n  }\n\n  async stop(): Promise<void> {\n    await this.consumer.disconnect();\n    logger.info('Virality correlator service stopped.');\n  }\n\n  /* ---------------------------- Private Helpers --------------------------- */\n\n  /**\n   * Parse and process each message coming from Kafka.\n   */\n  private async handleMessage({ message }: EachMessagePayload): Promise<void> {\n    if (!message.value) return;\n    const raw: SocialMetricEvent = JSON.parse(message.value.toString());\n\n    const correlated = this.enrich(raw);\n    if (correlated.viralityScore < 0.7) {\n      // below threshold \u2013 ignore to reduce noise\n      return;\n    }\n\n    bus.emit(InternalBus.EVENTS.VIRALITY_DETECTED, correlated);\n    logger.debug({ correlated }, 'Virality detected');\n\n    const decision = await this.strategy.assess(correlated);\n    bus.emit(InternalBus.EVENTS.SCALE_DECISION, decision);\n    logger.info({ decision }, 'Scaling decision generated');\n\n    await this.remediationChain.handle(correlated, decision);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Enrichment algorithm \u2013 a simplistic scoring heuristic\n   */\n  private enrich(event: SocialMetricEvent): CorrelatedSignal {\n    const { likes, comments, shares, viewers } = event;\n\n    // Weighted sum, tuned offline with historical data\n    const viralityScore =\n      0.4 * this.norm(likes) + 0.3 * this.norm(comments) + 0.2 * this.norm(shares) + 0.1 * this.norm(viewers);\n\n    // Convert virality score to projected RPS using linear regression coefficients\n    const projectedRPS = Math.round(viralityScore * 1500); // 1.0 \u2248 1500rps\n\n    return { viralityScore, projectedRPS };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Normalize raw counter via log scale to squish heavy tails\n   */\n  private norm(value: number): number {\n    if (value <= 0) return 0;\n    return Math.min(1, Math.log10(value + 1) / 4); // log10(10k) ~= 4\n  }\n\n  private handleError(err: unknown): void {\n    logger.error(err, 'Error while processing Kafka message');\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                               Bootstrap Code                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nasync function main(): Promise<void> {\n  const kafka = new Kafka({\n    clientId: 'pulse-sphere-correlator',\n    brokers: (process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS || 'localhost:9092').split(','),\n    connectionTimeout: 3000,\n  });\n\n  // Wire strategies\n  const hpa = new HPAStrategy(\n    Number(process.env.MIN_REPLICAS) || 3,\n    Number(process.env.MAX_REPLICAS) || 60,\n    Number(process.env.RPS_PER_REPLICA) || 100,\n  );\n  const strategy: ScalingStrategy = process.env.STRATEGY === 'cost'\n    ? new CostOptimizedStrategy(hpa, Number(process.env.COST_PER_REPLICA) || 0.07)\n    : hpa;\n\n  // Wire remediation chain\n  const kubeClient: IKubeClient = {\n    async getReplicaCount() {\n      // TODO: integrate @kubernetes/client-node\n      return 5;\n    },\n    async scaleDeployment(_deploy, _replicas) {\n      // TODO: call K8s API\n      logger.info('Mock scaleDeployment called.');\n    },\n  };\n  const pagerDutyClient: IPagerDutyClient = {\n    async triggerIncident(payload) {\n      // TODO: call PagerDuty API\n      logger.warn({ payload }, 'Mock PagerDuty incident');\n    },\n  };\n\n  const scaler = new KubernetesScaler(kubeClient, process.env.TARGET_DEPLOYMENT || 'api-backend');\n  const notifier = new PagerDutyNotifier(pagerDutyClient);\n  const sink = new NullRemediationHandler();\n\n  scaler.setNext(notifier).setNext(sink);\n\n  const service = new ViralityCorrelatorService(kafka, strategy, scaler);\n\n  // Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM / SIGINT\n  const shutdown = async () => {\n    logger.info('Received shutdown signal');\n    await service.stop();\n    process.exit(0);\n  };\n\n  process.on('SIGTERM', shutdown);\n  process.on('SIGINT', shutdown);\n\n  await service.start();\n}\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises\n  main();\n}\n```\n",

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps\n * -------------------------------------------------------------\n * Module 29 \u2013 Social-Aware Anomaly Detection & Escalation Engine\n * -------------------------------------------------------------\n *\n * This module consumes enriched telemetry events (metrics + social\n * interaction signals) from Kafka, detects anomalies using a pluggable\n * Strategy Pattern, notifies observers (Observer Pattern) and triggers a\n * Chain-of-Responsibility based escalation workflow (auto-scaling,\n * alerting, incident-commander).\n *\n * NOTE:\n *  \u2022 All external dependencies are purposely kept minimal to simplify\n *    demo deployment. Replace stubs with concrete implementations in\n *    production (e.g. Prometheus queries, PagerDuty SDK calls\u2026).\n *  \u2022 Configuration is expected via environment variables; see README.\n */\n\nimport { EventEmitter } from 'events';\nimport { Kafka, logLevel, Consumer, EachMessagePayload } from 'kafkajs';\nimport pino from 'pino';\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                         Logger (shared utility)                    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nconst logger = pino({\n  name: 'module_29:anomaly-detector',\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n});\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                                Types                               */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/**\n * Raw, enriched telemetry event.\n */\nexport interface MetricEvent {\n  service: string; // e.g. \"timeline-api\"\n  host: string; // e.g. \"ip-10-2-3-4\"\n  timestamp: number; // epoch millis\n  metrics: {\n    cpuUtilization: number; // 0\u20131\n    memoryUtilization: number; // 0\u20131\n    p95LatencyMs: number;\n    requestRateRps: number;\n  };\n  socialSignals: {\n    likes: number;\n    comments: number;\n    shares: number;\n    liveStreamViewers: number;\n  };\n  tags?: Record<string, string>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Anomaly detection result.\n */\nexport interface DetectionResult {\n  isAnomaly: boolean;\n  severity: Severity;\n  reason: string;\n  strategy: string;\n}\n\nexport enum Severity {\n  Low = 'low',\n  Medium = 'medium',\n  High = 'high',\n  Critical = 'critical',\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                         Strategy Pattern                           */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/**\n * Pluggable detection strategy contract.\n */\nexport interface DetectionStrategy {\n  readonly name: string;\n  detect(event: MetricEvent): DetectionResult | null;\n}\n\n/**\n * CPU Spike strategy \u2013 identifies sudden CPU utilisation > 85%.\n */\nexport class CpuSpikeStrategy implements DetectionStrategy {\n  readonly name = 'cpu_spike';\n\n  detect(event: MetricEvent): DetectionResult | null {\n    const { cpuUtilization } = event.metrics;\n    if (cpuUtilization > 0.85) {\n      return {\n        isAnomaly: true,\n        severity: cpuUtilization > 0.95 ? Severity.Critical : Severity.High,\n        reason: `CPU utilisation at ${(cpuUtilization * 100).toFixed(2)}%`,\n        strategy: this.name,\n      };\n    }\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Memory Leak strategy \u2013 memory > 90%.\n */\nexport class MemoryLeakStrategy implements DetectionStrategy {\n  readonly name = 'memory_leak';\n\n  detect(event: MetricEvent): DetectionResult | null {\n    const { memoryUtilization } = event.metrics;\n    if (memoryUtilization > 0.9) {\n      return {\n        isAnomaly: true,\n        severity:\n          memoryUtilization > 0.97 ? Severity.Critical : Severity.High,\n        reason: `Memory utilisation at ${(memoryUtilization * 100).toFixed(\n          2,\n        )}%`,\n        strategy: this.name,\n      };\n    }\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Viral Surge strategy \u2013 sudden user interest (shares + likes) combined\n * with rising latency.\n */\nexport class ViralSurgeStrategy implements DetectionStrategy {\n  readonly name = 'viral_surge';\n\n  private readonly SOCIAL_THRESHOLD = 10_000; // customizable\n\n  detect(event: MetricEvent): DetectionResult | null {\n    const socialScore =\n      event.socialSignals.likes +\n      event.socialSignals.comments +\n      event.socialSignals.shares +\n      event.socialSignals.liveStreamViewers;\n\n    if (\n      socialScore > this.SOCIAL_THRESHOLD &&\n      event.metrics.p95LatencyMs > 250\n    ) {\n      return {\n        isAnomaly: true,\n        severity:\n          socialScore > this.SOCIAL_THRESHOLD * 2\n            ? Severity.Critical\n            : Severity.Medium,\n        reason: `Viral surge detected (${socialScore} interactions) with latency ${\n          event.metrics.p95LatencyMs\n        } ms`,\n        strategy: this.name,\n      };\n    }\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Strategy registry allowing dynamic plug-in/out at runtime.\n */\nexport class StrategyRegistry {\n  private readonly strategies = new Map<string, DetectionStrategy>();\n\n  register(strategy: DetectionStrategy) {\n    if (this.strategies.has(strategy.name)) {\n      logger.warn({ strategy: strategy.name }, 'Strategy already registered');\n      return;\n    }\n    this.strategies.set(strategy.name, strategy);\n    logger.info({ strategy: strategy.name }, 'Strategy registered');\n  }\n\n  unregister(name: string) {\n    this.strategies.delete(name);\n    logger.info({ name }, 'Strategy unregistered');\n  }\n\n  detect(event: MetricEvent): DetectionResult[] {\n    const results: DetectionResult[] = [];\n    for (const strategy of this.strategies.values()) {\n      try {\n        const result = strategy.detect(event);\n        if (result?.isAnomaly) results.push(result);\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error(\n          { err, strategy: strategy.name },\n          'Strategy execution error',\n        );\n      }\n    }\n    return results;\n  }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                          Observer Pattern                          */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nexport interface AnomalyEvent {\n  metric: MetricEvent;\n  detections: DetectionResult[];\n}\n\nexport type AnomalyListener = (anomaly: AnomalyEvent) => void;\n\n/**\n * Emits \"anomaly\" events to registered listeners.\n */\nexport class AnomalyDetector extends EventEmitter {\n  private readonly registry: StrategyRegistry;\n\n  constructor(registry: StrategyRegistry) {\n    super();\n    this.registry = registry;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Evaluate a single metric event.\n   */\n  evaluate(event: MetricEvent): void {\n    const detections = this.registry.detect(event);\n    if (detections.length) {\n      const anomaly: AnomalyEvent = { metric: event, detections };\n      this.emit('anomaly', anomaly);\n    }\n  }\n\n  addListener(listener: AnomalyListener): void {\n    super.addListener('anomaly', listener);\n  }\n\n  removeListener(listener: AnomalyListener): void {\n    super.removeListener('anomaly', listener);\n  }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                  Chain-of-Responsibility: Escalation               */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nexport interface EscalationContext {\n  anomaly: AnomalyEvent;\n}\n\nexport abstract class EscalationHandler {\n  private next?: EscalationHandler;\n\n  setNext(next: EscalationHandler): EscalationHandler {\n    this.next = next;\n    return next;\n  }\n\n  async handle(ctx: EscalationContext): Promise<void> {\n    const handled = await this.process(ctx).catch((err) => {\n      logger.error({ err }, 'Handler process failed');\n      return false;\n    });\n\n    if (!handled && this.next) {\n      await this.next.handle(ctx);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Return true if the handler fully handled the anomaly (no further\n   * escalation needed).\n   */\n  protected abstract process(ctx: EscalationContext): Promise<boolean>;\n}\n\n/**\n * AutoScaler handler \u2013 tries to resolve by adding capacity.\n */\nexport class AutoScalerHandler extends EscalationHandler {\n  protected async process(ctx: EscalationContext): Promise<boolean> {\n    const { anomaly } = ctx;\n    const maxSeverity = getMaxSeverity(anomaly.detections);\n\n    if (maxSeverity === Severity.Low || maxSeverity === Severity.Medium) {\n      const service = anomaly.metric.service;\n      logger.info(\n        { service },\n        'Triggering auto-scaler (kubernetes/hpa) for service',\n      );\n      // TODO: Replace with real orchestrator API call.\n      await fakeNetworkDelay();\n      // Assume scaling succeeded.\n      return true;\n    }\n    return false; // escalate further\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Alert handler \u2013 sends PagerDuty / Slack alerts.\n */\nexport class AlertHandler extends EscalationHandler {\n  protected async process(ctx: EscalationContext): Promise<boolean> {\n    const { anomaly } = ctx;\n    const maxSeverity = getMaxSeverity(anomaly.detections);\n\n    logger.info(\n      { maxSeverity, service: anomaly.metric.service },\n      'Sending alert',\n    );\n    try {\n      // TODO: Integrate with PagerDuty SDK.\n      await fakeNetworkDelay();\n      return maxSeverity !== Severity.Critical; // escalate if critical\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err }, 'Alert dispatch failed');\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Final escalation \u2013 page incident commander.\n */\nexport class IncidentCommanderHandler extends EscalationHandler {\n  protected async process(ctx: EscalationContext): Promise<boolean> {\n    const { anomaly } = ctx;\n    logger.warn(\n      { anomaly },\n      'Escalating to Incident Commander \u2013 human intervention required',\n    );\n    // Fail-safe: always considered handled after this.\n    await fakeNetworkDelay();\n    return true;\n  }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                     Kafka Consumer Integration                     */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nexport class AnomalyDetectionService {\n  private readonly detector: AnomalyDetector;\n  private readonly kafka: Kafka;\n  private consumer?: Consumer;\n  private readonly escalationChain: EscalationHandler;\n\n  constructor() {\n    // Strategies\n    const registry = new StrategyRegistry();\n    registry.register(new CpuSpikeStrategy());\n    registry.register(new MemoryLeakStrategy());\n    registry.register(new ViralSurgeStrategy());\n\n    // Detector\n    this.detector = new AnomalyDetector(registry);\n\n    // Escalation chain\n    const autoScaler = new AutoScalerHandler();\n    const alertHandler = new AlertHandler();\n    const commander = new IncidentCommanderHandler();\n    autoScaler.setNext(alertHandler).setNext(commander);\n    this.escalationChain = autoScaler;\n\n    // Observers\n    this.detector.addListener((anomaly) => {\n      this.escalationChain\n        .handle({ anomaly })\n        .catch((err) =>\n          logger.error({ err }, 'Unhandled error during escalation'),\n        );\n    });\n\n    // Kafka\n    this.kafka = new Kafka({\n      clientId: 'pulse-sphere-anomaly-detector',\n      brokers: (process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS ?? 'localhost:9092').split(','),\n      logLevel: logLevel.ERROR,\n    });\n  }\n\n  async start(): Promise<void> {\n    const topic = process.env.TELEMETRY_TOPIC ?? 'telemetry.enriched';\n    const groupId =\n      process.env.CONSUMER_GROUP_ID ?? 'anomaly-detector-consumer';\n\n    this.consumer = this.kafka.consumer({ groupId });\n    await this.consumer.connect();\n    await this.consumer.subscribe({ topic, fromBeginning: false });\n\n    logger.info({ topic, groupId }, 'Kafka consumer connected');\n\n    await this.consumer.run({\n      eachMessage: async (payload: EachMessagePayload) => {\n        try {\n          const event = JSON.parse(\n            payload.message.value!.toString('utf8'),\n          ) as MetricEvent;\n          this.detector.evaluate(event);\n        } catch (err) {\n          logger.warn({ err }, 'Invalid metric event received');\n        }\n      },\n    });\n  }\n\n  async stop(): Promise<void> {\n    if (this.consumer) {\n      await this.consumer.disconnect();\n      logger.info('Kafka consumer disconnected');\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                            Utilities                               */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nfunction getMaxSeverity(detections: DetectionResult[]): Severity {\n  const ranking: Severity[] = [\n    Severity.Low,\n    Severity.Medium,\n    Severity.High,\n    Severity.Critical,\n  ];\n  return detections.reduce<Severity>(\n    (prev, curr) =>\n      ranking.indexOf(curr.severity) > ranking.indexOf(prev)\n        ? curr.severity\n        : prev,\n    Severity.Low,\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Simulates network I/O latency for stubbed external calls.\n */\nasync function fakeNetworkDelay(): Promise<void> {\n  return new Promise((res) =>\n    setTimeout(res, Math.random() * 200 + 100 /* 100\u2013300ms */),\n  );\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/*                       Module singleton export                      */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nexport const anomalyDetectionService = new AnomalyDetectionService();\n\n/**\n * Auto-start when executed directly (useful for Docker entrypoints):\n *\n *   node dist/module_29.js\n */\nif (require.main === module) {\n  anomalyDetectionService\n    .start()\n    .then(() => {\n      logger.info('Anomaly detection service started');\n    })\n    .catch((err) => {\n      logger.fatal({ err }, 'Failed to start anomaly detection service');\n      process.exit(1);\n    });\n\n  // Graceful shutdown\n  process.on('SIGINT', async () => {\n    logger.info('Stopping anomaly detection service\u2026');\n    await anomalyDetectionService.stop();\n    process.exit(0);\n  });\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_29.ts",
    "numLines": 478,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 478
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_21.ts"

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps\n * -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * module_21.ts\n *\n * SocialCorrelationEngine\n * -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * This module wires together social-interaction signals and low-level\n * infrastructure metrics, producing a single CorrelationResult stream that\n * downstream services (auto\u2013scaling, alerting, capacity-planning) can consume.\n *\n * Patterns in play:\n *  \u2022 Observer          \u2013 EventEmitter\u2013based pub/sub for two independent streams\n *  \u2022 Strategy          \u2013 Pluggable correlation algorithms\n *  \u2022 Chain-of-Responsibility \u2013 Post-correlation remediation decision pipeline\n *\n * NOTE: Connectivity with Kafka / NATS is abstracted away for brevity; replace\n * the stubbed adapters with your production messaging clients.\n * -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n */\n\nimport { EventEmitter } from 'events';\nimport { randomUUID } from 'crypto';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                               Domain Models                                */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/** Social-interaction signal enriched by the SocialOps ingestion pipeline. */\nexport interface SocialSignal {\n  readonly id: string;\n  readonly timestamp: number; // epoch millis\n  readonly likes: number;\n  readonly comments: number;\n  readonly shares: number;\n  readonly livestreamViewers: number;\n  readonly hashtag?: string;\n}\n\n/** Infrastructure metric captured by Prometheus / OpenTelemetry collectors. */\nexport interface InfrastructureMetric {\n  readonly id: string;\n  readonly timestamp: number; // epoch millis\n  readonly cpuUtil: number; // 0\u2013100\n  readonly memoryUtil: number; // 0\u2013100\n  readonly reqRate: number; // HTTP requests/sec\n  readonly errorRate: number; // 0\u20131\n  readonly cluster: string;\n}\n\n/** Outcome produced by a CorrelationStrategy. */\nexport interface CorrelationResult {\n  readonly correlationId: string;\n  readonly timestamp: number;\n  readonly signal: SocialSignal;\n  readonly metric: InfrastructureMetric;\n  /** -1 (perfect inverse) \u2026 1 (perfect direct), NaN if indeterminate. */\n  readonly coefficient: number;\n  /** Whether a significant correlation/spike was identified. */\n  readonly significant: boolean;\n  /** Human-readable summary. */\n  readonly summary: string;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Utility Helpers                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nconst now = (): number => Date.now();\n\n/**\n * Simple logger \u2013 in production wire this to your centralized logging stack\n * (e.g., Pino, Winston, Elastic, or OpenTelemetry).\n */\nconst log = {\n  info: (msg: string, meta?: unknown): void =>\n    console.log(`[INFO]  ${new Date().toISOString()} | ${msg}`, meta ?? ''),\n  warn: (msg: string, meta?: unknown): void =>\n    console.warn(`[WARN]  ${new Date().toISOString()} | ${msg}`, meta ?? ''),\n  error: (msg: string, meta?: unknown): void =>\n    console.error(`[ERROR] ${new Date().toISOString()} | ${msg}`, meta ?? ''),\n};\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                       Strategy: Correlation Algorithms                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Pluggable algorithm contract \u2013 compute correlation coefficient for one pair\n * of social signal + infra metric.\n */\nexport interface CorrelationStrategy {\n  readonly name: string;\n  compute(\n    signal: SocialSignal,\n    metric: InfrastructureMetric,\n  ): CorrelationResult;\n}\n\n/**\n * Baseline Pearson correlation using likes + reqRate (simplified).\n */\nexport class PearsonLikesToRequestStrategy implements CorrelationStrategy {\n  readonly name = 'PearsonLikesToRequest';\n\n  compute(\n    signal: SocialSignal,\n    metric: InfrastructureMetric,\n  ): CorrelationResult {\n    // na\u00efve calculation: treat two samples as series [likes, reqRate]\n    const meanLikes = signal.likes;\n    const meanReq = metric.reqRate;\n\n    // Single-point \u201cseries\u201d \u2013 coefficient is undefined; default to heuristic.\n    const coefficient =\n      meanLikes === 0 && meanReq === 0\n        ? NaN\n        : meanLikes > 0 && meanReq > 0\n        ? 1\n        : -1;\n\n    const significant =\n      !Number.isNaN(coefficient) &&\n      coefficient > 0.8 &&\n      signal.likes > 1_000 &&\n      metric.reqRate > 10_000;\n\n    return {\n      correlationId: randomUUID(),\n      timestamp: now(),\n      signal,\n      metric,\n      coefficient,\n      significant,\n      summary: significant\n        ? `High correlation (\u03c1\u2248${coefficient.toFixed(\n            2,\n          )}) between likes and request rate`\n        : 'No significant correlation',\n    };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Real-time spike detection comparing current metric against rolling average.\n */\nexport class SpikeDetectionStrategy implements CorrelationStrategy {\n  readonly name = 'SpikeDetection';\n  private readonly windowMs: number;\n  private readonly history: Map<string, InfrastructureMetric[]> = new Map();\n\n  constructor(windowMs = 5 * 60_000 /* 5 min */) {\n    this.windowMs = windowMs;\n  }\n\n  compute(\n    signal: SocialSignal,\n    metric: InfrastructureMetric,\n  ): CorrelationResult {\n    const list = this.history.get(metric.cluster) ?? [];\n    // prune old samples\n    const freshSamples = list.filter(\n      (m) => metric.timestamp - m.timestamp <= this.windowMs,\n    );\n    freshSamples.push(metric);\n    this.history.set(metric.cluster, freshSamples);\n\n    const avgCpu =\n      freshSamples.reduce((acc, m) => acc + m.cpuUtil, 0) /\n      Math.max(freshSamples.length, 1);\n    const cpuSpike = metric.cpuUtil > avgCpu * 1.5 && metric.cpuUtil > 80;\n\n    const coefficient = cpuSpike ? 0.9 : 0.1;\n    const significant = cpuSpike && signal.livestreamViewers > 5_000;\n\n    return {\n      correlationId: randomUUID(),\n      timestamp: now(),\n      signal,\n      metric,\n      coefficient,\n      significant,\n      summary: significant\n        ? `CPU spike in cluster ${metric.cluster} mirrors livestream popularity`\n        : 'No notable spike',\n    };\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                   Chain-of-Responsibility: Remediation                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport interface RemediationContext {\n  readonly correlation: CorrelationResult;\n}\n\nexport abstract class RemediationHandler {\n  private next?: RemediationHandler;\n\n  setNext(handler: RemediationHandler): RemediationHandler {\n    this.next = handler;\n    return handler;\n  }\n\n  async handle(ctx: RemediationContext): Promise<void> {\n    if (await this.process(ctx)) {\n      return;\n    }\n    if (this.next) {\n      await this.next.handle(ctx);\n    }\n  }\n\n  protected abstract process(ctx: RemediationContext): Promise<boolean>;\n}\n\n/** Auto-scales cluster if correlated spike detected. */\nexport class ScaleUpHandler extends RemediationHandler {\n  protected async process({ correlation }: RemediationContext): Promise<boolean> {\n    if (!correlation.significant) return false;\n\n    // Stubbed scaling request \u2013 replace with k8s/hcloud/ecs API call.\n    log.info(\n      `Scaling up cluster due to correlation ${correlation.correlationId}`,\n    );\n    // Simulate async action\n    await new Promise((res) => setTimeout(res, 250));\n    return true; // handled\n  }\n}\n\n/** Warms CDN / cache layers to pre-fetch probable hot content. */\nexport class CacheWarmUpHandler extends RemediationHandler {\n  protected async process({ correlation }: RemediationContext): Promise<boolean> {\n    if (!correlation.significant) return false;\n    if (!correlation.signal.hashtag) return false;\n\n    log.info(\n      `Warming cache for hashtag #${correlation.signal.hashtag} (corr ${correlation.correlationId})`,\n    );\n    await new Promise((res) => setTimeout(res, 100));\n    return true;\n  }\n}\n\n/** Notifies human on-call SRE if automatic responses are skipped. */\nexport class NotifySREHandler extends RemediationHandler {\n  protected async process({ correlation }: RemediationContext): Promise<boolean> {\n    log.warn(\n      `Routing notification to SRE \u2013 no auto-remediation matched for corr ${correlation.correlationId}`,\n    );\n    // send pagerduty / slack / opsgenie, etc.\n    return true;\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         SocialCorrelationEngine                            */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ninterface CorrelationEngineOptions {\n  strategies?: CorrelationStrategy[];\n  remediationChain?: RemediationHandler;\n  // In milliseconds; time to keep unmatched events before discarding\n  orphanWindowMs?: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Subscribes to two EventEmitters (social + infra) and attempts to correlate\n * events sharing the nearest timestamp within `orphanWindowMs`.\n */\nexport class SocialCorrelationEngine extends EventEmitter {\n  private readonly socialStream = new EventEmitter();\n  private readonly infraStream = new EventEmitter();\n  private readonly strategies: CorrelationStrategy[];\n  private readonly orphanWindowMs: number;\n  private readonly orphanSocial: SocialSignal[] = [];\n  private readonly orphanInfra: InfrastructureMetric[] = [];\n  private readonly remediationChain: RemediationHandler;\n\n  constructor({\n    strategies = [\n      new PearsonLikesToRequestStrategy(),\n      new SpikeDetectionStrategy(),\n    ],\n    remediationChain,\n    orphanWindowMs = 15_000,\n  }: CorrelationEngineOptions = {}) {\n    super();\n    this.strategies = strategies;\n    this.orphanWindowMs = orphanWindowMs;\n    this.remediationChain =\n      remediationChain ??\n      new ScaleUpHandler()\n        .setNext(new CacheWarmUpHandler())\n        .setNext(new NotifySREHandler());\n\n    this.socialStream.on('social', (sig: SocialSignal) =>\n      this.handleSocial(sig),\n    );\n    this.infraStream.on('infra', (met: InfrastructureMetric) =>\n      this.handleInfra(met),\n    );\n  }\n\n  /* ----------------------- public publishing APIs ----------------------- */\n\n  publishSocial(signal: SocialSignal): void {\n    this.socialStream.emit('social', signal);\n  }\n\n  publishInfra(metric: InfrastructureMetric): void {\n    this.infraStream.emit('infra', metric);\n  }\n\n  /* --------------------------- private logic ---------------------------- */\n\n  private handleSocial(signal: SocialSignal): void {\n    try {\n      const match = this.findClosestInfra(signal.timestamp);\n      if (match) {\n        this.correlate(signal, match);\n      } else {\n        this.orphanSocial.push(signal);\n        this.cleanupOrphans();\n      }\n    } catch (err) {\n      log.error('Failed to handle social signal', err);\n    }\n  }\n\n  private handleInfra(metric: InfrastructureMetric): void {\n    try {\n      const match = this.findClosestSocial(metric.timestamp);\n      if (match) {\n        this.correlate(match, metric);\n      } else {\n        this.orphanInfra.push(metric);\n        this.cleanupOrphans();\n      }\n    } catch (err) {\n      log.error('Failed to handle infra metric', err);\n    }\n  }\n\n  private findClosestInfra(ts: number): InfrastructureMetric | undefined {\n    let closestIdx = -1;\n    let minDelta = Infinity;\n    for (let i = 0; i < this.orphanInfra.length; i++) {\n      const delta = Math.abs(this.orphanInfra[i].timestamp - ts);\n      if (delta < minDelta && delta <= this.orphanWindowMs) {\n        minDelta = delta;\n        closestIdx = i;\n      }\n    }\n    if (closestIdx > -1) {\n      return this.orphanInfra.splice(closestIdx, 1)[0];\n    }\n    return undefined;\n  }\n\n  private findClosestSocial(ts: number): SocialSignal | undefined {\n    let closestIdx = -1;\n    let minDelta = Infinity;\n    for (let i = 0; i < this.orphanSocial.length; i++) {\n      const delta = Math.abs(this.orphanSocial[i].timestamp - ts);\n      if (delta < minDelta && delta <= this.orphanWindowMs) {\n        minDelta = delta;\n        closestIdx = i;\n      }\n    }\n    if (closestIdx > -1) {\n      return this.orphanSocial.splice(closestIdx, 1)[0];\n    }\n    return undefined;\n  }\n\n  private async correlate(\n    signal: SocialSignal,\n    metric: InfrastructureMetric,\n  ): Promise<void> {\n    for (const strategy of this.strategies) {\n      const result = strategy.compute(signal, metric);\n      this.emit('correlation', result);\n      log.info(\n        `Strategy ${strategy.name} produced corrId ${result.correlationId} (significant=${result.significant})`,\n      );\n      // Fire remediation asynchronously; do not block stream\n      /* eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises */\n      this.remediationChain.handle({ correlation: result });\n    }\n  }\n\n  private cleanupOrphans(): void {\n    const threshold = now() - this.orphanWindowMs;\n    this.orphanSocial.splice(\n      0,\n      this.orphanSocial.findIndex((s) => s.timestamp >= threshold),\n    );\n    this.orphanInfra.splice(\n      0,\n      this.orphanInfra.findIndex((m) => m.timestamp >= threshold),\n    );\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Usage Example                                 */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n// The following block demonstrates local usage. Remove or convert to unit\n// tests as appropriate in production code.\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  const engine = new SocialCorrelationEngine();\n\n  engine.on('correlation', (res: CorrelationResult) =>\n    log.info(`Got correlation result: ${res.summary}`),\n  );\n\n  // Simulate incoming events\n  setInterval(() => {\n    engine.publishSocial({\n      id: randomUUID(),\n      timestamp: now(),\n      likes: Math.floor(Math.random() * 5_000),\n      comments: Math.floor(Math.random() * 2_000),\n      shares: Math.floor(Math.random() * 1_000),\n      livestreamViewers: Math.floor(Math.random() * 10_000),\n      hashtag: Math.random() > 0.5 ? 'AI' : undefined,\n    });\n  }, 1_000);\n\n  setInterval(() => {\n    engine.publishInfra({\n      id: randomUUID(),\n      timestamp: now(),\n      cpuUtil: Math.random() * 100,\n      memoryUtil: Math.random() * 100,\n      reqRate: Math.random() * 20_000,\n      errorRate: Math.random(),\n      cluster: `cluster-${Math.ceil(Math.random() * 3)}`,\n    });\n  }, 1_200);\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_21.ts",
    "numLines": 448,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 448
  },

    "content": "{\n  \"name\": \"@pulsesphere/socialops-system-monitoring\",\n  \"version\": \"1.0.0\",\n  \"description\": \"Core monitoring service for PulseSphere SocialOps. Enriches metrics, logs and traces with real-time social interaction signals to unlock socially-aware observability.\",\n  \"keywords\": [\n    \"pulseSphere\",\n    \"socialops\",\n    \"observability\",\n    \"system-monitoring\",\n    \"microservices\",\n    \"kafka\",\n    \"nats\",\n    \"opentelemetry\",\n    \"typescript\"\n  ],\n  \"license\": \"Apache-2.0\",\n  \"author\": \"PulseSphere Engineering <engineering@pulsesphere.io>\",\n  \"repository\": {\n    \"type\": \"git\",\n    \"url\": \"https://github.com/pulsesphere/socialops-system-monitoring.git\"\n  },\n  \"homepage\": \"https://pulsesphere.io/socialops\",\n  \"engines\": {\n    \"node\": \">=18.17\"\n  },\n  \"type\": \"module\",\n  \"main\": \"./dist/main.js\",\n  \"files\": [\n    \"dist\"\n  ],\n },\n  \"dependencies\": {\n    \"@opentelemetry/api\": \"^1.8.0\",\n    \"@opentelemetry/auto-instrumentations-node\": \"^0.41.0\",\n    \"@opentelemetry/sdk-node\": \"^0.41.0\",\n \"rxjs\": \"^7.8.1\",\n    \"reflect-metadata\": \"^0.1.13\"\n  },\n  \"devDependencies\": {\n    \"@types/express\": \"^4.17.17\",\n    \"@types/node\": \"^20.8.10\",\n    \"@types/uuid\":   "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Configuration Module\n * ============================================\n * src/config.ts\n *\n * A strongly-typed, runtime-validated configuration layer that powers every\n * PulseSphere service.  Configuration is constructed from three sources\n * (ordered by ascending priority):\n *\n *   1. Hard-coded, version-controlled defaults              (./defaults)\n *   2. Environment-specific override file  (JSON / YAML)    (PULSE_CONFIG_PATH)\n *   3. Process environment variables                        (process.env)\n *\n * On boot-time the config is validated with `zod`; any mismatch terminates the\n * process early to prevent undefined states.  When running in non-production\n * stages, the override file is `fs.watch`-ed and hot-reloaded to offer an\n * improved developer experience.\n *\n * Usage:\n *   import { config } from '@/config';\n *   const { kafka } = config(); // lazy, memoized accessor\n */\n\nimport fs from 'node:fs';\nimport path from 'node:path';\nimport { EventEmitter } from 'node:events';\nimport { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';\n\nimport dotenv from 'dotenv';\nimport yaml from 'js-yaml';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// Bootstrap / Env Loading\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n// Load variables from .env file *once* during module initialization\ndotenv.config();\n\n/**\n * Resolve directory of current module (to enable ESM & CJS compatibility)\n */\nconst __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);\nconst __dirname = path.dirname(__filename);\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// Configuration Schema (zod) \u2013 change with care!\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst zConfig = z.object({\n  app: z.object({\n    name: z.string().default('pulse-sphere'),\n    version: z.string().default('0.0.0'),\n    stage: z.enum(['development', 'staging', 'production', 'test']).default('development'),\n  }),\n\n  server: z.object({\n    host: z.string().ip().default('0.0.0.0'),\n    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).default(8080),\n  }),\n\n  kafka: z.object({\n    clientId: z.string().default('pulse-sphere-client'),\n    brokers: z.array(z.string()).min(1).default(['localhost:9092']),\n    /** The maximum size (bytes) of any Kafka message produced  */\n    maxMessageBytes: z.number().int().positive().default(1024 * 1024), // 1 MiB\n    ssl: z.boolean().default(false),\n  }),\n\n  nats: z.object({\n    url: z.string().url().default('nats://localhost:4222'),\n    name: z.string().default('pulse-sphere-nats'),\n  }),\n\n  observability: z.object({\n    logLevel: z.enum(['trace', 'debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error', 'fatal']).default('info'),\n    metricsEnabled: z.boolean().default(true),\n    traceSamplingRate: z.number().min(0).max(1).default(0.1),\n  }),\n\n  security: z.object({\n    jwtSecret: z.string().min(32, { message: 'JWT secret must be at least 32 characters long' }),\n    corsAllowedOrigins: z.array(z.string()).default(['*']),\n  }),\n\n  backup: z.object({\n    snapshotIntervalMinutes: z.number().int().positive().default(60),\n    retentionDays: z.number().int().positive().default(7),\n    destinationS3Bucket: z.string().optional(),\n  }),\n\n  featureFlags: z.object({\n    alertingEnabled: z.boolean().default(true),\n    autoScalingEnabled: z.boolean().default(false),\n    socialContextEnrichment: z.boolean().default(true),\n  }),\n});\n\nexport type Config = z.infer<typeof zConfig>;\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Default Configuration (source 1)\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst defaults: Config = {\n  app: {\n    name: 'pulse-sphere',\n    version: process.env.npm_package_version ?? '0.0.0',\n    stage: (process.env.NODE_ENV as Config['app']['stage']) ?? 'development',\n  },\n  server: { host: '0.0.0.0', port: 8080 },\n  kafka: {\n    clientId: 'pulse-sphere-client',\n    brokers: ['localhost:9092'],\n    maxMessageBytes: 1024 * 1024,\n    ssl: false,\n  },\n  nats: { url: 'nats://localhost:4222', name: 'pulse-sphere-nats' },\n  observability: { logLevel: 'info', metricsEnabled: true, traceSamplingRate: 0.1 },\n  security: {\n    jwtSecret: 'PLEASE_REPLACE_ME_WITH_A_REAL_SECRET___________',\n    corsAllowedOrigins: ['*'],\n  },\n  backup: { snapshotIntervalMinutes: 60, retentionDays: 7, destinationS3Bucket: undefined },\n  featureFlags: {\n    alertingEnabled: true,\n    autoScalingEnabled: false,\n    socialContextEnrichment: true,\n  },\n};\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Deep freeze utility to guarantee immutability after load */\nfunction deepFreeze<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(obj: T): Readonly<T> {\n  Object.freeze(obj);\n  Object.getOwnPropertyNames(obj).forEach((prop) => {\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment\n    // @ts-ignore\n    if (obj[prop] !== null\n        // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment\n        // @ts-ignore\n        && (typeof obj[prop] === 'object')\n        // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment\n        // @ts-ignore\n        && !Object.isFrozen(obj[prop])) {\n      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment\n      // @ts-ignore\n      deepFreeze(obj[prop]);\n    }\n  });\n  return obj;\n}\n\n/**\n * Merge two POJOs deeply \u2013 rhs takes precedence over lhs.\n */\nfunction deepMerge<T extends Record<string, unknown>, U extends Record<string, unknown>>(lhs: T, rhs: U): T & U {\n  const result = { ...lhs } as T & U;\n\n  Object.entries(rhs).forEach(([key, value]) => {\n    if (value === undefined) return;\n\n    if (Array.isArray(value)) {\n      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment\n      // @ts-ignore\n      result[key] = value as unknown as (T & U)[keyof (T & U)];\n    } else if (value !== null && typeof value === 'object') {\n      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment\n      // @ts-ignore\n      result[key] = deepMerge(lhs[key] ?? {}, value);\n    } else {\n      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment\n      // @ts-ignore\n      result[key] = value as (T & U)[keyof (T & U)];\n    }\n  });\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n/** Parse boolean valued environment variables safely. */\nconst toBoolean = (val?: string): boolean | undefined =>\n  val === undefined ? undefined : /^(true|1|yes)$/i.test(val);\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Environment Variable mapping (source 3)\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Convert strongly-typed env variables to partial Config object.\n * NB: Keep names consistent & document them inside README / ADR!\n */\nfunction envToConfig(): Partial<Config> {\n  return {\n    app: {\n      stage: process.env.NODE_ENV as Config['app']['stage'] | undefined,\n    },\n\n    server: {\n      port: process.env.PORT ? Number(process.env.PORT) : undefined,\n    },\n\n    kafka: {\n      brokers: process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS?.split(',').filter(Boolean),\n      clientId: process.env.KAFKA_CLIENT_ID,\n      ssl: toBoolean(process.env.KAFKA_SSL),\n      maxMessageBytes: process.env.KAFKA_MAX_MSG_BYTES\n        ? Number(process.env.KAFKA_MAX_MSG_BYTES)\n        : undefined,\n    },\n\n    nats: {\n      url: process.env.NATS_URL,\n      name: process.env.NATS_NAME,\n    },\n\n    observability: {\n      logLevel: process.env.LOG_LEVEL as Config['observability']['logLevel'] | undefined,\n      metricsEnabled: toBoolean(process.env.METRICS_ENABLED),\n      traceSamplingRate: process.env.TRACE_SAMPLING_RATE\n        ? Number(process.env.TRACE_SAMPLING_RATE)\n        : undefined,\n    },\n\n    security: {\n      jwtSecret: process.env.JWT_SECRET,\n      corsAllowedOrigins: process.env.CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS?.split(',').filter(Boolean),\n    },\n\n    backup: {\n      snapshotIntervalMinutes: process.env.BACKUP_SNAPSHOT_INTERVAL\n        ? Number(process.env.BACKUP_SNAPSHOT_INTERVAL)\n        : undefined,\n      retentionDays: process.env.BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS\n        ? Number(process.env.BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS)\n        : undefined,\n      destinationS3Bucket: process.env.BACKUP_DESTINATION_BUCKET,\n    },\n\n    featureFlags: {\n      alertingEnabled: toBoolean(process.env.FEATURE_ALERTING_ENABLED),\n      autoScalingEnabled: toBoolean(process.env.FEATURE_AUTOSCALING_ENABLED),\n      socialContextEnrichment: toBoolean(process.env.FEATURE_SOCIAL_ENRICHMENT),\n    },\n  };\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Override File Loader (source 2)\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Allowed override file formats */\nconst SUPPORTED_EXT = new Set(['.json', '.yaml', '.yml']);\n\nfunction loadOverrideFile(): Record<string, unknown> | undefined {\n  const overridePath = process.env.PULSE_CONFIG_PATH;\n\n  if (!overridePath) return undefined;\n\n  const absPath = path.isAbsolute(overridePath)\n    ? overridePath\n    : path.join(__dirname, overridePath);\n\n  const ext = path.extname(absPath).toLowerCase();\n  if (!SUPPORTED_EXT.has(ext)) {\n    // eslint-disable-next-line no-console\n    console.warn(\n      `[config] Unsupported override file extension \"${ext}\". Supported: ${Array.from(\n        SUPPORTED_EXT,\n      ).join(', ')}`,\n    );\n    return undefined;\n  }\n\n  if (!fs.existsSync(absPath)) {\n    // eslint-disable-next-line no-console\n    console.warn(`[config] Override file not found at: ${absPath}`);\n    return undefined;\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const raw = fs.readFileSync(absPath, 'utf8');\n    return ext === '.json' ? JSON.parse(raw) : (yaml.load(raw) as Record<string, unknown>);\n  } catch (err) {\n    // eslint-disable-next-line no-console\n    console.error(`[config] Failed to load override file: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : err}`);\n    return undefined;\n  }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// ConfigManager (Singleton) \u2013 public API\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass ConfigManager extends EventEmitter {\n  private static _instance: ConfigManager;\n\n  private _config: Readonly<Config>;\n\n  private constructor() {\n    super();\n\n    const environment = envToConfig();\n    const override = loadOverrideFile();\n\n    const merged = deepMerge(defaults, override ?? {});\n    const withEnv = deepMerge(merged, environment);\n\n    // Validate & freeze\n    const validated = zConfig.parse(withEnv);\n\n    this._config = deepFreeze(validated);\n\n    this.setupHotReload();\n  }\n\n  static instance(): ConfigManager {\n    if (!ConfigManager._instance) ConfigManager._instance = new ConfigManager();\n    return ConfigManager._instance;\n  }\n\n  /** Typed getter shortcut \u2013 guarantees immutability */\n  get config(): Readonly<Config> {\n    return this._config;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Hot reload configuration file (only in non-production and if override file exists).\n   * Emits 'reload' event on success.\n   */\n  private setupHotReload(): void {\n    const overridePath = process.env.PULSE_CONFIG_PATH;\n    if (!overridePath) return;\n    if (this._config.app.stage === 'production') return;\n\n    const absPath = path.isAbsolute(overridePath)\n      ? overridePath\n      : path.join(__dirname, overridePath);\n\n    fs.watch(\n      absPath,\n      { persistent: false },\n      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-misused-promises\n      async (_event, filename) => {\n        if (!filename) return;\n\n        try {\n          const newData = loadOverrideFile();\n          if (!newData) return;\n\n          const withEnv = deepMerge(deepMerge(defaults, newData), envToConfig());\n          const validated = zConfig.parse(withEnv);\n          this._config = deepFreeze(validated);\n\n          this.emit('reload', this._config);\n          // eslint-disable-next-line no-console\n          console.info('[config] Hot-reloaded configuration');\n        } catch (err) {\n          // eslint-disable-next-line no-console\n          console.error(\n            `[config] Failed to hot-reload configuration: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : err}`,\n          );\n        }\n      },\n    );\n  }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Public Export \u2013 as lazy getter function\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Retrieve the immutable configuration object\n *\n * Example:\n *   import { config } from '@/config';\n *   const port = config().server.port;\n */\nexport function config(): Readonly<Config> {\n  return ConfigManager.instance().config;\n}\n\n/**\n * Listen for hot-reload events (only emitted in non-production environments)\n *\n * Example:\n *   import { onConfigReload } from '@/config';\n *   onConfigReload((newCfg) => console.log('Config changed', newCfg));\n */\nexport function onConfigReload(listener: (cfg: Readonly<Config>) => void): void {\n  ConfigManager.instance().on('reload', listener);\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/config.ts",
    "numLines": 398,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 398
  },
  "type": "text"
}
