Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/constants.ts"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**\n *  constants.ts\n *  ==========================================================================\n *  Canonical, platform-wide constants shared by every micro-service in the\n *  PulseSphere SocialOps stack.\n *\n *  \u2757  DO NOT store secrets here. Use Vault / Kubernetes Secrets instead.\n */\n\nimport ms from 'ms';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\nimport { version as pulseSphereVersion } from '../package.json';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                                 Enums                                      */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * NODE_ENV values understood by the platform.\n */\nexport enum NodeEnvironment {\n  Development = 'development',\n  Test = 'test',\n  Staging = 'staging',\n  Production = 'production',\n}\n\n/**\n * Canonical micro-service names\u2014used for discovery, observability labels,\n * traffic shadowing and RBAC scopes.\n */\nexport enum ServiceName {\n  API_GATEWAY = 'api-gateway',\n  TELEMETRY_INGESTOR = 'telemetry-ingestor',\n  SIGNAL_ENRICHER = 'signal-enricher',\n  ANOMALY_DETECTOR = 'anomaly-detector',\n  CAPACITY_ORCHESTRATOR = 'capacity-orchestrator',\n  ALERT_DISPATCHER = 'alert-dispatcher',\n  CONFIGURATION_SERVICE = 'configuration-service',\n  BACKUP_SERVICE = 'backup-service',\n  AUTH_SERVICE = 'auth-service',\n}\n\n/**\n * Kafka topic catalogue (append-only; renames are BREAKING).\n */\nexport enum KafkaTopic {\n  RAW_TELEMETRY = 'telemetry.raw.v1',\n  ENRICHED_TELEMETRY = 'telemetry.enriched.v1',\n  ANOMALY_EVENT = 'anomaly.event.v1',\n  ORCHESTRATION_COMMAND = 'orchestration.command.v1',\n  USER_SIGNAL = 'user-signal.v1',\n  AUDIT_LOG = 'audit.log.v1',\n}\n\n/**\n * Feature flag keys toggled via the configuration service.\n */\nexport enum FeatureFlag {\n  ENABLE_REALTIME_TREND_PREDICTION = 'enable_realtime_trend_prediction',\n  USE_LATEST_ANOMALY_MODEL = 'use_latest_anomaly_model',\n  FAIL_OPEN_ON_RATE_LIMITER = 'fail_open_on_rate_limiter',\n  ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_UI = 'enable_experimental_ui',\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                          Environment Variable Helpers                      */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Retrieve and validate environment variables via zod schema.\n */\nfunction env<T extends z.ZodTypeAny>(\n  key: string,\n  schema: T,\n  fallback?: z.infer<T>,\n): z.infer<T> {\n  const raw = process.env[key];\n\n  if ((raw === undefined || raw === '') && fallback !== undefined) {\n    return fallback;\n  }\n\n  const parsed = schema.safeParse(raw);\n  if (!parsed.success) {\n    // eslint-disable-next-line no-console\n    console.error(\n      `\u274c  Environment variable ${key} is missing or invalid. ${parsed.error.message}`,\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  return parsed.data;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                               Core Constants                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Semantic version from package.json\n */\nexport const APP_VERSION = pulseSphereVersion;\n\n/**\n * Parsed runtime environment.\n */\nexport const RUNTIME_ENV: NodeEnvironment = env(\n  'NODE_ENV',\n  z.nativeEnum(NodeEnvironment),\n  NodeEnvironment.Development,\n);\n\n/**\n * Start service in read-only mode (no mutating operations).\n */\nexport const READ_ONLY_MODE: boolean = env(\n  'READ_ONLY_MODE',\n  z\n    .string()\n    .regex(/^(true|false)$/i)\n    .transform(val => val.toLowerCase() === 'true'),\n  false,\n);\n\n/**\n * Default retry/back-off policy for outbound calls.\n */\nexport const DEFAULT_RETRY_STRATEGY = {\n  maxRetries: env('DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES', z.coerce.number().int().min(0).max(10), 5),\n  initialDelayMs: env('DEFAULT_INITIAL_RETRY_DELAY', z.string(), '500ms'),\n  backoffMultiplier: env('DEFAULT_RETRY_MULTIPLIER', z.coerce.number().min(1).max(10), 2),\n} as const;\n\n/**\n * Same as above but with parsed delay number.\n */\nexport const DEFAULT_RETRY_STRATEGY_MS = {\n  ...DEFAULT_RETRY_STRATEGY,\n  initialDelayMs: ms(DEFAULT_RETRY_STRATEGY.initialDelayMs),\n};\n\n/**\n * Istio / Consul side-car ports.\n */\nexport const SIDECAR_PORTS = {\n  HTTP: 15020,\n  GRPC: 15021,\n  METRICS: 15090,\n} as const;\n\n/**\n * Labels automatically attached to all logs, metrics and traces.\n */\nexport const BASE_OBSERVABILITY_LABELS = {\n  service: process.env.SERVICE_NAME ?? 'unknown',\n  version: APP_VERSION,\n  hostname: process.env.HOSTNAME ?? 'unknown',\n} as const;\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                             Social Signal Types                            */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport const SOCIAL_SIGNAL_TYPES = [\n  'like',\n  'comment',\n  'share',\n  'follow',\n  'hashtag-trend',\n  'live-stream-start',\n  'live-stream-end',\n  'reaction',\n  'mention',\n] as const;\n\nexport type SocialSignalType = (typeof SOCIAL_SIGNAL_TYPES)[number];\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Platform SLO / Timeouts                            */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport const SLO = {\n  apiRequest: ms('250ms'),\n  kafkaRoundTrip: ms('500ms'),\n  traceEndToEnd: ms('3s'),\n  configPropagation: ms('2s'),\n} as const;\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                           Graceful Shutdown                                */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport const SHUTDOWN_SIGNALS = ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM', 'SIGQUIT'] as const;\nexport type ShutdownSignal = (typeof SHUTDOWN_SIGNALS)[number];\n\nexport const SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS = ms(\n  env('SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT', z.string(), '15s'),\n);\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Helper Types                                  */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport interface RetryPolicy {\n  readonly maxRetries: number;\n  readonly initialDelayMs: number;\n  readonly backoffMultiplier: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Frozen configuration tree usable by DI containers.\n */\nexport const CONFIG = Object.freeze({\n  env: RUNTIME_ENV,\n  featureFlags: FeatureFlag,\n  kafkaTopics: KafkaTopic,\n  service: {\n    name: process.env.SERVICE_NAME as ServiceName | undefined,\n    ports: SIDECAR_PORTS,\n  },\n  retry: DEFAULT_RETRY_STRATEGY_MS,\n  slo: SLO,\n  shutdown: {\n    signals: SHUTDOWN_SIGNALS,\n    timeoutMs: SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS,\n  },\n  appVersion: APP_VERSION,\n});\n\n/**\n * Validate SERVICE_NAME early in the bootstrap process.\n */\n(function verifyServiceName(): void {\n  const raw = process.env.SERVICE_NAME;\n  if (!raw) {\n    console.warn(\n      '\u26a0\ufe0f  SERVICE_NAME env var not set; observability data will lack precision.',\n    );\n    return;\n  }\n  if (!(Object.values(ServiceName) as string[]).includes(raw)) {\n    console.error(`\u274c  Unknown SERVICE_NAME '${raw}'.`);\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n})();\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                             Type Guards                                    */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Check if a string is a recognised social signal.\n */\nexport function isSocialSignalType(value: string): value is SocialSignalType {\n  return (SOCIAL_SIGNAL_TYPES as readonly string[]).includes(value);\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a string is a valid feature flag key.\n */\nexport function isFeatureFlag(value: string): value is FeatureFlag {\n  return (Object.values(FeatureFlag) as readonly string[]).includes(value);\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/constants.ts",
    "numLines": 266,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 266
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "todos": [

    },

39. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Unified Notification Service\n * ======================================================\n * src/services/notificationService.ts\n *\n * This module provides a centralized, extensible notification service for\n * dispatching alerts across multiple channels (email, webhooks, logs).\n * It consolidates disparate alerting mechanisms scattered across the codebase\n * into a single, cohesive interface.\n *\n * Design Patterns:\n *  - Strategy Pattern: Each NotificationChannel is a pluggable strategy\n *  - Singleton Pattern: A single NotificationService instance for the app\n *  - Observer/Publisher: Channels are notified when alerts are dispatched\n *\n * Usage:\n *   import { notificationService, AlertMessage } from '@/services/notificationService';\n *\n *   await notificationService.dispatch({\n *     severity: 'critical',\n *     title: 'CPU Usage Alert',\n *     details: { cpuUsage: 95, threshold: 80 },\n *     sourceModule: 'module_11',\n *   });\n */\n\nimport pino from 'pino';\nimport { notificationConfig } from '../config/notifications';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Type Definitions                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Severity levels for alert messages.\n */\nexport type AlertSeverity = 'critical' | 'warning' | 'info';\n\n/**\n * Unified alert message structure used throughout the application.\n */\nexport interface AlertMessage {\n  /** Severity level of the alert */\n  severity: AlertSeverity;\n  /** Short, descriptive title of the alert */\n  title: string;\n  /** Additional context and details about the alert */\n  details: Record<string, any>;\n  /** Identifier of the module that generated the alert */\n  sourceModule: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Contract for notification channel implementations.\n * Each channel is responsible for delivering alerts through a specific medium.\n */\nexport interface NotificationChannel {\n  /** Unique identifier for the channel */\n  readonly name: string;\n  /**\n   * Send an alert message through this channel.\n   * @param message The alert message to send\n   */\n  send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Logger                                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nconst logger = pino({\n  name: 'notification-service',\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                        Concrete Channel Implementations                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Email notification channel.\n * Sends alert emails via SMTP endpoint.\n */\nexport class EmailChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name = 'email';\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly smtpEndpoint: string,\n    private readonly recipients: string[],\n  ) {}\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      // In production, this would integrate with SES, SendGrid, SMTP, etc.\n      // For now, we simulate the HTTP call\n      const payload = {\n        to: this.recipients,\n        subject: `[PulseSphere] ${message.severity.toUpperCase()} \u2013 ${message.title}`,\n        body: JSON.stringify(\n          {\n            severity: message.severity,\n            title: message.title,\n            details: message.details,\n            sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n            timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n          },\n          null,\n          2,\n        ),\n      };\n\n      logger.info(\n        { channel: this.name, recipients: this.recipients, title: message.title },\n        'Email notification sent',\n      );\n\n      // Placeholder for actual HTTP call:\n      // await axios.post(`${this.smtpEndpoint}/send`, payload);\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err, channel: this.name }, 'Failed to send email notification');\n      throw err;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Webhook notification channel.\n * Posts alerts to external services like Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, etc.\n */\nexport class WebhookChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name: string;\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly webhookUrl: string,\n    private readonly channelName: string = 'webhook',\n  ) {\n    this.name = channelName;\n  }\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      const payload = this.formatPayload(message);\n\n      logger.info(\n        { channel: this.name, title: message.title, severity: message.severity },\n        'Webhook notification sent',\n      );\n\n      // Placeholder for actual HTTP call:\n      // await axios.post(this.webhookUrl, payload);\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err, channel: this.name }, 'Failed to send webhook notification');\n      throw err;\n    }\n  }\n\n  private formatPayload(message: AlertMessage): Record<string, unknown> {\n    // Format for Slack-style webhooks\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('hooks.slack.com')) {\n      return {\n        text: `*${message.severity.toUpperCase()}* alert from *${message.sourceModule}*`,\n        attachments: [\n          {\n            color: this.getSeverityColor(message.severity),\n            title: message.title,\n            fields: Object.entries(message.details).map(([key, value]) => ({\n              title: key,\n              value: String(value),\n              short: true,\n            })),\n          },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Format for Microsoft Teams\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('teams.microsoft.com')) {\n      return {\n        '@type': 'MessageCard',\n        themeColor: this.getSeverityColor(message.severity),\n        title: `${message.severity.toUpperCase()}: ${message.title}`,\n        text: `Alert from ${message.sourceModule}`,\n        sections: [\n          {\n            facts: Object.entries(message.details).map(([key, value]) => ({\n              name: key,\n              value: String(value),\n            })),\n          },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Format for PagerDuty\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('pagerduty.com')) {\n      return {\n        routing_key: notificationConfig.pagerDutyKey,\n        event_action: 'trigger',\n        payload: {\n          summary: `${message.title} [${message.sourceModule}]`,\n          severity: message.severity === 'critical' ? 'critical' : message.severity === 'warning' ? 'warning' : 'info',\n          source: `PulseSphere/${message.sourceModule}`,\n          custom_details: message.details,\n        },\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Generic format\n    return {\n      severity: message.severity,\n      title: message.title,\n      details: message.details,\n      sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    };\n  }\n\n  private getSeverityColor(severity: AlertSeverity): string {\n    switch (severity) {\n      case 'critical':\n        return '#dc3545'; // red\n      case 'warning':\n        return '#ffc107'; // yellow\n      case 'info':\n        return '#17a2b8'; // blue\n      default:\n        return '#6c757d'; // gray\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Log notification channel.\n * Writes alerts to the application log with appropriate severity level.\n */\nexport class LogChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name = 'log';\n\n  private readonly channelLogger: pino.Logger;\n\n  constructor() {\n    this.channelLogger = pino({\n      name: 'alert-log-channel',\n      level: 'trace',\n    });\n  }\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    const logPayload = {\n      alertTitle: message.title,\n      alertSeverity: message.severity,\n      sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n      details: message.details,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    };\n\n    switch (message.severity) {\n      case 'critical':\n        this.channelLogger.fatal(logPayload, `CRITICAL ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n      case 'warning':\n        this.channelLogger.warn(logPayload, `WARNING ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n      case 'info':\n      default:\n        this.channelLogger.info(logPayload, `INFO ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                           NotificationService                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Central notification service that dispatches alerts to configured channels.\n */\nexport class NotificationService {\n  private readonly channels: NotificationChannel[];\n  private readonly serviceLogger: pino.Logger;\n\n  constructor(channels: NotificationChannel[]) {\n    this.channels = channels;\n    this.serviceLogger = pino({\n      name: 'notification-service',\n      level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Dispatch an alert message to all configured notification channels.\n   * Errors from individual channels are logged but do not stop other channels.\n   *\n   * @param alert The alert message to dispatch\n   */\n  async dispatch(alert: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    this.serviceLogger.debug(\n      { alert, channelCount: this.channels.length },\n      'Dispatching alert to notification channels',\n    );\n\n    const results = await Promise.allSettled(\n      this.channels.map(async (channel) => {\n        try {\n          await channel.send(alert);\n          return { channel: channel.name, success: true };\n        } catch (err) {\n          this.serviceLogger.error(\n            { err, channel: channel.name, alertTitle: alert.title },\n            'Channel failed to send notification',\n          );\n          return { channel: channel.name, success: false, error: err };\n        }\n      }),\n    );\n\n    const failedChannels = results.filter(\n      (r) => r.status === 'rejected' || (r.status === 'fulfilled' && !r.value.success),\n    );\n\n    if (failedChannels.length > 0) {\n      this.serviceLogger.warn(\n        { failedCount: failedChannels.length, totalChannels: this.channels.length },\n        'Some notification channels failed',\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get the list of configured channel names.\n   */\n  getChannelNames(): string[] {\n    return this.channels.map((c) => c.name);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Singleton Instance Factory                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Create the default NotificationService instance with channels\n * configured from the notification config.\n */\nfunction createNotificationService(): NotificationService {\n  const channels: NotificationChannel[] = [];\n\n  // Always add the log channel as a fallback\n  channels.push(new LogChannel());\n\n  // Add email channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.smtpEndpoint && notificationConfig.emailRecipients.length > 0) {\n    channels.push(\n      new EmailChannel(notificationConfig.smtpEndpoint, notificationConfig.emailRecipients),\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Add Slack webhook channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.slackWebhookUrl) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.slackWebhookUrl, 'slack'));\n  }\n\n  // Add PagerDuty channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.pagerDutyEndpoint && notificationConfig.pagerDutyKey) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.pagerDutyEndpoint, 'pagerduty'));\n  }\n\n  // Add Teams webhook channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.teamsWebhookUrl) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.teamsWebhookUrl, 'teams'));\n  }\n\n  logger.info(\n    { channels: channels.map((c) => c.name) },\n    'NotificationService initialized with channels',\n  );\n\n  return new NotificationService(channels);\n}\n\n/**\n * Singleton instance of the NotificationService.\n * Import this to send alerts from anywhere in the application.\n */\nexport const notificationService = createNotificationService();\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Convenience Exports                            */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport { notificationConfig };\n",
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/services/notificationService.ts"
}
Output
"Error: EACCES: permission denied, mkdir '/app/project/src/services'"
40. Bash @ -

  "command": "whoami && id && ls -la /app/project/",
  "description": "Check user and permissions"
}

  "isImage": false,

  "stdout": "claude\nuid=1000(claude) gid=1000(claude) groups=1000(claude)\ntotal 40\ndrwxr-xr-x 5 root root 4096 Feb  2 20:45 .\ndrwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Feb  2 20:45 ..\n-rw-rw-r-- 1 root root 2240 Feb  1 02:26 .gitignore\n-rw-rw-r-- 1 root root 3993 Feb  1 02:26 Dockerfile\n-rw-rw-r-- 1 root root 6854 Feb  1 02:26 README.md\ndrwxrwxr-x 2 root root 4096 Feb  2 20:45 docs\n-rw-rw-r-- 1 root root 3801 Feb  1 02:26 package.json\ndrwxrwxr-x 2 root root 4096 Feb  2 20:45 src\ndrwxrwxr-x 2 root root 4096 Feb  2 20:45 tests"
}

{
  "content": "/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Unified Notification Service\n * ======================================================\n * src/notificationService.ts\n *\n * This module provides a centralized, extensible notification service for\n * dispatching alerts across multiple channels (email, webhooks, logs).\n * It consolidates disparate alerting mechanisms scattered across the codebase\n * into a single, cohesive interface.\n *\n * Design Patterns:\n *  - Strategy Pattern: Each NotificationChannel is a pluggable strategy\n *  - Singleton Pattern: A single NotificationService instance for the app\n *  - Observer/Publisher: Channels are notified when alerts are dispatched\n *\n * Usage:\n *   import { notificationService, AlertMessage } from './notificationService';\n *\n *   await notificationService.dispatch({\n *     severity: 'critical',\n *     title: 'CPU Usage Alert',\n *     details: { cpuUsage: 95, threshold: 80 },\n *     sourceModule: 'module_11',\n *   });\n */\n\nimport pino from 'pino';\nimport { notificationConfig } from './notificationConfig';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Type Definitions                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Severity levels for alert messages.\n */\nexport type AlertSeverity = 'critical' | 'warning' | 'info';\n\n/**\n * Unified alert message structure used throughout the application.\n */\nexport interface AlertMessage {\n  /** Severity level of the alert */\n  severity: AlertSeverity;\n  /** Short, descriptive title of the alert */\n  title: string;\n  /** Additional context and details about the alert */\n  details: Record<string, any>;\n  /** Identifier of the module that generated the alert */\n  sourceModule: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Contract for notification channel implementations.\n * Each channel is responsible for delivering alerts through a specific medium.\n */\nexport interface NotificationChannel {\n  /** Unique identifier for the channel */\n  readonly name: string;\n  /**\n   * Send an alert message through this channel.\n   * @param message The alert message to send\n   */\n  send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Logger                                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nconst logger = pino({\n  name: 'notification-service',\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                        Concrete Channel Implementations                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Email notification channel.\n * Sends alert emails via SMTP endpoint.\n */\nexport class EmailChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name = 'email';\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly smtpEndpoint: string,\n    private readonly recipients: string[],\n  ) {}\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      // In production, this would integrate with SES, SendGrid, SMTP, etc.\n      // For now, we simulate the HTTP call\n      const payload = {\n        to: this.recipients,\n        subject: `[PulseSphere] ${message.severity.toUpperCase()} \u2013 ${message.title}`,\n        body: JSON.stringify(\n          {\n            severity: message.severity,\n            title: message.title,\n            details: message.details,\n            sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n            timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n          },\n          null,\n          2,\n        ),\n      };\n\n      logger.info(\n        { channel: this.name, recipients: this.recipients, title: message.title },\n        'Email notification sent',\n      );\n\n      // Placeholder for actual HTTP call:\n      // await axios.post(`${this.smtpEndpoint}/send`, payload);\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err, channel: this.name }, 'Failed to send email notification');\n      throw err;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Webhook notification channel.\n * Posts alerts to external services like Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, etc.\n */\nexport class WebhookChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name: string;\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly webhookUrl: string,\n    private readonly channelName: string = 'webhook',\n  ) {\n    this.name = channelName;\n  }\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      const payload = this.formatPayload(message);\n\n      logger.info(\n        { channel: this.name, title: message.title, severity: message.severity },\n        'Webhook notification sent',\n      );\n\n      // Placeholder for actual HTTP call:\n      // await axios.post(this.webhookUrl, payload);\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err, channel: this.name }, 'Failed to send webhook notification');\n      throw err;\n    }\n  }\n\n  private formatPayload(message: AlertMessage): Record<string, unknown> {\n    // Format for Slack-style webhooks\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('hooks.slack.com')) {\n      return {\n        text: `*${message.severity.toUpperCase()}* alert from *${message.sourceModule}*`,\n        attachments: [\n          {\n            color: this.getSeverityColor(message.severity),\n            title: message.title,\n            fields: Object.entries(message.details).map(([key, value]) => ({\n              title: key,\n              value: String(value),\n              short: true,\n            })),\n          },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Format for Microsoft Teams\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('teams.microsoft.com')) {\n      return {\n        '@type': 'MessageCard',\n        themeColor: this.getSeverityColor(message.severity),\n        title: `${message.severity.toUpperCase()}: ${message.title}`,\n        text: `Alert from ${message.sourceModule}`,\n        sections: [\n          {\n            facts: Object.entries(message.details).map(([key, value]) => ({\n              name: key,\n              value: String(value),\n            })),\n          },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Format for PagerDuty\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('pagerduty.com')) {\n      return {\n        routing_key: notificationConfig.pagerDutyKey,\n        event_action: 'trigger',\n        payload: {\n          summary: `${message.title} [${message.sourceModule}]`,\n          severity: message.severity === 'critical' ? 'critical' : message.severity === 'warning' ? 'warning' : 'info',\n          source: `PulseSphere/${message.sourceModule}`,\n          custom_details: message.details,\n        },\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Generic format\n    return {\n      severity: message.severity,\n      title: message.title,\n      details: message.details,\n      sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    };\n  }\n\n  private getSeverityColor(severity: AlertSeverity): string {\n    switch (severity) {\n      case 'critical':\n        return '#dc3545'; // red\n      case 'warning':\n        return '#ffc107'; // yellow\n      case 'info':\n        return '#17a2b8'; // blue\n      default:\n        return '#6c757d'; // gray\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Log notification channel.\n * Writes alerts to the application log with appropriate severity level.\n */\nexport class LogChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name = 'log';\n\n  private readonly channelLogger: pino.Logger;\n\n  constructor() {\n    this.channelLogger = pino({\n      name: 'alert-log-channel',\n      level: 'trace',\n    });\n  }\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    const logPayload = {\n      alertTitle: message.title,\n      alertSeverity: message.severity,\n      sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n      details: message.details,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    };\n\n    switch (message.severity) {\n      case 'critical':\n        this.channelLogger.fatal(logPayload, `CRITICAL ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n      case 'warning':\n        this.channelLogger.warn(logPayload, `WARNING ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n      case 'info':\n      default:\n        this.channelLogger.info(logPayload, `INFO ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                           NotificationService                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Central notification service that dispatches alerts to configured channels.\n */\nexport class NotificationService {\n  private readonly channels: NotificationChannel[];\n  private readonly serviceLogger: pino.Logger;\n\n  constructor(channels: NotificationChannel[]) {\n    this.channels = channels;\n    this.serviceLogger = pino({\n      name: 'notification-service',\n      level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Dispatch an alert message to all configured notification channels.\n   * Errors from individual channels are logged but do not stop other channels.\n   *\n   * @param alert The alert message to dispatch\n   */\n  async dispatch(alert: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    this.serviceLogger.debug(\n      { alert, channelCount: this.channels.length },\n      'Dispatching alert to notification channels',\n    );\n\n    const results = await Promise.allSettled(\n      this.channels.map(async (channel) => {\n        try {\n          await channel.send(alert);\n          return { channel: channel.name, success: true };\n        } catch (err) {\n          this.serviceLogger.error(\n            { err, channel: channel.name, alertTitle: alert.title },\n            'Channel failed to send notification',\n          );\n          return { channel: channel.name, success: false, error: err };\n        }\n      }),\n    );\n\n    const failedChannels = results.filter(\n      (r) => r.status === 'rejected' || (r.status === 'fulfilled' && !r.value.success),\n    );\n\n    if (failedChannels.length > 0) {\n      this.serviceLogger.warn(\n        { failedCount: failedChannels.length, totalChannels: this.channels.length },\n        'Some notification channels failed',\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get the list of configured channel names.\n   */\n  getChannelNames(): string[] {\n    return this.channels.map((c) => c.name);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Singleton Instance Factory                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Create the default NotificationService instance with channels\n * configured from the notification config.\n */\nfunction createNotificationService(): NotificationService {\n  const channels: NotificationChannel[] = [];\n\n  // Always add the log channel as a fallback\n  channels.push(new LogChannel());\n\n  // Add email channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.smtpEndpoint && notificationConfig.emailRecipients.length > 0) {\n    channels.push(\n      new EmailChannel(notificationConfig.smtpEndpoint, notificationConfig.emailRecipients),\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Add Slack webhook channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.slackWebhookUrl) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.slackWebhookUrl, 'slack'));\n  }\n\n  // Add PagerDuty channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.pagerDutyEndpoint && notificationConfig.pagerDutyKey) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.pagerDutyEndpoint, 'pagerduty'));\n  }\n\n  // Add Teams webhook channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.teamsWebhookUrl) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.teamsWebhookUrl, 'teams'));\n  }\n\n  logger.info(\n    { channels: channels.map((c) => c.name) },\n    'NotificationService initialized with channels',\n  );\n\n  return new NotificationService(channels);\n}\n\n/**\n * Singleton instance of the NotificationService.\n * Import this to send alerts from anywhere in the application.\n */\nexport const notificationService = createNotificationService();\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Convenience Exports                            */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport { notificationConfig };\n",
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/notificationService.ts"
}
Output
"Error: EACCES: permission denied, open '/app/project/src/notificationService.ts'"

49. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Notification Configuration\n * ====================================================\n * src/config/notifications.ts\n *\n * Centralized configuration for the notification service.\n * All hardcoded alerting configuration (email addresses, webhook URLs, API keys)\n * is consolidated here for easy management and environment-specific overrides.\n *\n * Configuration is loaded from environment variables with sensible defaults.\n */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Configuration Interface                             */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Configuration structure for the notification service.\n */\nexport interface NotificationConfig {\n  /** SMTP endpoint for sending email alerts */\n  smtpEndpoint: string;\n  /** List of email recipients for alert notifications */\n  emailRecipients: string[];\n  /** Slack webhook URL for posting alerts */\n  slackWebhookUrl: string | undefined;\n  /** Microsoft Teams webhook URL for posting alerts */\n  teamsWebhookUrl: string | undefined;\n  /** PagerDuty events API endpoint */\n  pagerDutyEndpoint: string;\n  /** PagerDuty routing/integration key */\n  pagerDutyKey: string | undefined;\n  /** OpsGenie API endpoint */\n  opsGenieEndpoint: string | undefined;\n  /** OpsGenie API key */\n  opsGenieKey: string | undefined;\n  /** Whether alerting is globally enabled */\n  alertingEnabled: boolean;\n  /** Minimum severity level to trigger alerts: 'info', 'warning', 'critical' */\n  minAlertSeverity: 'info' | 'warning' | 'critical';\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Environment Configuration                           */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Parse a comma-separated environment variable into an array.\n */\nfunction parseArrayEnv(value: string | undefined, defaultValue: string[] = []): string[] {\n  if (!value) return defaultValue;\n  return value\n    .split(',')\n    .map((s) => s.trim())\n    .filter(Boolean);\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse a boolean environment variable.\n */\nfunction parseBoolEnv(value: string | undefined, defaultValue: boolean): boolean {\n  if (!value) return defaultValue;\n  return /^(true|1|yes)$/i.test(value);\n}\n\n/**\n * The centralized notification configuration.\n *\n * Values are loaded from environment variables with the following mapping:\n *\n * | Config Property      | Environment Variable           | Default Value                              |\n * |---------------------|--------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|\n * | smtpEndpoint        | SMTP_ENDPOINT                  | https://smtp.internal                     |\n * | emailRecipients     | ALERT_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS         | ['ops@pulsesphere.io']                    |\n * | slackWebhookUrl     | SLACK_WEBHOOK                  | https://hooks.slack.com/services/dummy    |\n * | teamsWebhookUrl     | TEAMS_WEBHOOK                  | undefined                                 |\n * | pagerDutyEndpoint   | PAGERDUTY_ENDPOINT             | https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue   |\n * | pagerDutyKey        | PAGERDUTY_KEY                  | undefined                                 |\n * | opsGenieEndpoint    | OPSGENIE_ENDPOINT              | undefined                                 |\n * | opsGenieKey         | OPSGENIE_KEY                   | undefined                                 |\n * | alertingEnabled     | ALERTING_ENABLED               | true                                      |\n * | minAlertSeverity    | MIN_ALERT_SEVERITY             | 'warning'                                 |\n */\nexport const notificationConfig: NotificationConfig = {\n  // Email configuration\n  smtpEndpoint: process.env.SMTP_ENDPOINT ?? 'https://smtp.internal',\n  emailRecipients: parseArrayEnv(process.env.ALERT_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS, ['ops@pulsesphere.io']),\n\n  // Slack configuration\n  slackWebhookUrl:\n    process.env.SLACK_WEBHOOK ??\n    process.env.PS_ALERT_SLACK_WEBHOOK ??\n    'https://hooks.slack.com/services/dummy',\n\n  // Microsoft Teams configuration\n  teamsWebhookUrl: process.env.TEAMS_WEBHOOK ?? process.env.TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL,\n\n  // PagerDuty configuration\n  pagerDutyEndpoint:\n    process.env.PAGERDUTY_ENDPOINT ?? 'https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue',\n  pagerDutyKey: process.env.PAGERDUTY_KEY ?? process.env.PS_ALERT_PD_KEY,\n\n  // OpsGenie configuration\n  opsGenieEndpoint: process.env.OPSGENIE_ENDPOINT,\n  opsGenieKey: process.env.OPSGENIE_KEY,\n\n  // Global alerting settings\n  alertingEnabled: parseBoolEnv(process.env.ALERTING_ENABLED, true),\n  minAlertSeverity: (process.env.MIN_ALERT_SEVERITY as NotificationConfig['minAlertSeverity']) ?? 'warning',\n};\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Validation                                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Validate the notification configuration at startup.\n * Logs warnings for missing optional configuration.\n */\nexport function validateNotificationConfig(): void {\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\n\n  if (!notificationConfig.pagerDutyKey) {\n    warnings.push('PAGERDUTY_KEY not set \u2013 PagerDuty alerts will be disabled');\n  }\n\n  if (notificationConfig.slackWebhookUrl?.includes('dummy')) {\n    warnings.push('SLACK_WEBHOOK appears to be a placeholder \u2013 Slack alerts may not work');\n  }\n\n  if (warnings.length > 0) {\n    // eslint-disable-next-line no-console\n    console.warn('[NotificationConfig] Configuration warnings:');\n    warnings.forEach((w) => console.warn(`  - ${w}`));\n  }\n}\n\n// Run validation on module load in non-test environments\nif (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'test') {\n  validateNotificationConfig();\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/config/notifications.ts"
}
Output
{
  "content": "/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Notification Configuration\n * ====================================================\n * src/config/notifications.ts\n *\n * Centralized configuration for the notification service.\n * All hardcoded alerting configuration (email addresses, webhook URLs, API keys)\n * is consolidated here for easy management and environment-specific overrides.\n *\n * Configuration is loaded from environment variables with sensible defaults.\n */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Configuration Interface                             */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Configuration structure for the notification service.\n */\nexport interface NotificationConfig {\n  /** SMTP endpoint for sending email alerts */\n  smtpEndpoint: string;\n  /** List of email recipients for alert notifications */\n  emailRecipients: string[];\n  /** Slack webhook URL for posting alerts */\n  slackWebhookUrl: string | undefined;\n  /** Microsoft Teams webhook URL for posting alerts */\n  teamsWebhookUrl: string | undefined;\n  /** PagerDuty events API endpoint */\n  pagerDutyEndpoint: string;\n  /** PagerDuty routing/integration key */\n  pagerDutyKey: string | undefined;\n  /** OpsGenie API endpoint */\n  opsGenieEndpoint: string | undefined;\n  /** OpsGenie API key */\n  opsGenieKey: string | undefined;\n  /** Whether alerting is globally enabled */\n  alertingEnabled: boolean;\n  /** Minimum severity level to trigger alerts: 'info', 'warning', 'critical' */\n  minAlertSeverity: 'info' | 'warning' | 'critical';\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Environment Configuration                           */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Parse a comma-separated environment variable into an array.\n */\nfunction parseArrayEnv(value: string | undefined, defaultValue: string[] = []): string[] {\n  if (!value) return defaultValue;\n  return value\n    .split(',')\n    .map((s) => s.trim())\n    .filter(Boolean);\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse a boolean environment variable.\n */\nfunction parseBoolEnv(value: string | undefined, defaultValue: boolean): boolean {\n  if (!value) return defaultValue;\n  return /^(true|1|yes)$/i.test(value);\n}\n\n/**\n * The centralized notification configuration.\n *\n * Values are loaded from environment variables with the following mapping:\n *\n * | Config Property      | Environment Variable           | Default Value                              |\n * |---------------------|--------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|\n * | smtpEndpoint        | SMTP_ENDPOINT                  | https://smtp.internal                     |\n * | emailRecipients     | ALERT_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS         | ['ops@pulsesphere.io']                    |\n * | slackWebhookUrl     | SLACK_WEBHOOK                  | https://hooks.slack.com/services/dummy    |\n * | teamsWebhookUrl     | TEAMS_WEBHOOK                  | undefined                                 |\n * | pagerDutyEndpoint   | PAGERDUTY_ENDPOINT             | https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue   |\n * | pagerDutyKey        | PAGERDUTY_KEY                  | undefined                                 |\n * | opsGenieEndpoint    | OPSGENIE_ENDPOINT              | undefined                                 |\n * | opsGenieKey         | OPSGENIE_KEY                   | undefined                                 |\n * | alertingEnabled     | ALERTING_ENABLED               | true                                      |\n * | minAlertSeverity    | MIN_ALERT_SEVERITY             | 'warning'                                 |\n */\nexport const notificationConfig: NotificationConfig = {\n  // Email configuration\n  smtpEndpoint: process.env.SMTP_ENDPOINT ?? 'https://smtp.internal',\n  emailRecipients: parseArrayEnv(process.env.ALERT_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS, ['ops@pulsesphere.io']),\n\n  // Slack configuration\n  slackWebhookUrl:\n    process.env.SLACK_WEBHOOK ??\n    process.env.PS_ALERT_SLACK_WEBHOOK ??\n    'https://hooks.slack.com/services/dummy',\n\n  // Microsoft Teams configuration\n  teamsWebhookUrl: process.env.TEAMS_WEBHOOK ?? process.env.TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL,\n\n  // PagerDuty configuration\n  pagerDutyEndpoint:\n    process.env.PAGERDUTY_ENDPOINT ?? 'https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue',\n  pagerDutyKey: process.env.PAGERDUTY_KEY ?? process.env.PS_ALERT_PD_KEY,\n\n  // OpsGenie configuration\n  opsGenieEndpoint: process.env.OPSGENIE_ENDPOINT,\n  opsGenieKey: process.env.OPSGENIE_KEY,\n\n  // Global alerting settings\n  alertingEnabled: parseBoolEnv(process.env.ALERTING_ENABLED, true),\n  minAlertSeverity: (process.env.MIN_ALERT_SEVERITY as NotificationConfig['minAlertSeverity']) ?? 'warning',\n};\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Validation                                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Validate the notification configuration at startup.\n * Logs warnings for missing optional configuration.\n */\nexport function validateNotificationConfig(): void {\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\n\n  if (!notificationConfig.pagerDutyKey) {\n    warnings.push('PAGERDUTY_KEY not set \u2013 PagerDuty alerts will be disabled');\n  }\n\n  if (notificationConfig.slackWebhookUrl?.includes('dummy')) {\n    warnings.push('SLACK_WEBHOOK appears to be a placeholder \u2013 Slack alerts may not work');\n  }\n\n  if (warnings.length > 0) {\n    // eslint-disable-next-line no-console\n    console.warn('[NotificationConfig] Configuration warnings:');\n    warnings.forEach((w) => console.warn(`  - ${w}`));\n  }\n}\n\n// Run validation on module load in non-test environments\nif (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'test') {\n  validateNotificationConfig();\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/config/notifications.ts",

50. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Unified Notification Service\n * ======================================================\n * src/services/notificationService.ts\n *\n * This module provides a centralized, extensible notification service for\n * dispatching alerts across multiple channels (email, webhooks, logs).\n * It consolidates disparate alerting mechanisms scattered across the codebase\n * into a single, cohesive interface.\n *\n * Design Patterns:\n *  - Strategy Pattern: Each NotificationChannel is a pluggable strategy\n *  - Singleton Pattern: A single NotificationService instance for the app\n *  - Observer/Publisher: Channels are notified when alerts are dispatched\n *\n * Usage:\n *   import { notificationService, AlertMessage } from './services/notificationService';\n *\n *   await notificationService.dispatch({\n *     severity: 'critical',\n *     title: 'CPU Usage Alert',\n *     details: { cpuUsage: 95, threshold: 80 },\n *     sourceModule: 'module_11',\n *   });\n */\n\nimport pino from 'pino';\nimport { notificationConfig } from '../config/notifications';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Type Definitions                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Severity levels for alert messages.\n */\nexport type AlertSeverity = 'critical' | 'warning' | 'info';\n\n/**\n * Unified alert message structure used throughout the application.\n */\nexport interface AlertMessage {\n  /** Severity level of the alert */\n  severity: AlertSeverity;\n  /** Short, descriptive title of the alert */\n  title: string;\n  /** Additional context and details about the alert */\n  details: Record<string, any>;\n  /** Identifier of the module that generated the alert */\n  sourceModule: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Contract for notification channel implementations.\n * Each channel is responsible for delivering alerts through a specific medium.\n */\nexport interface NotificationChannel {\n  /** Unique identifier for the channel */\n  readonly name: string;\n  /**\n   * Send an alert message through this channel.\n   * @param message The alert message to send\n   */\n  send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Logger                                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nconst logger = pino({\n  name: 'notification-service',\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                        Concrete Channel Implementations                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Email notification channel.\n * Sends alert emails via SMTP endpoint.\n */\nexport class EmailChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name = 'email';\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly smtpEndpoint: string,\n    private readonly recipients: string[],\n  ) {}\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      // In production, this would integrate with SES, SendGrid, SMTP, etc.\n      // For now, we simulate the HTTP call\n      const payload = {\n        to: this.recipients,\n        subject: `[PulseSphere] ${message.severity.toUpperCase()} \u2013 ${message.title}`,\n        body: JSON.stringify(\n          {\n            severity: message.severity,\n            title: message.title,\n            details: message.details,\n            sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n            timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n          },\n          null,\n          2,\n        ),\n      };\n\n      logger.info(\n        { channel: this.name, recipients: this.recipients, title: message.title },\n        'Email notification sent',\n      );\n\n      // Placeholder for actual HTTP call:\n      // await axios.post(`${this.smtpEndpoint}/send`, payload);\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err, channel: this.name }, 'Failed to send email notification');\n      throw err;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Webhook notification channel.\n * Posts alerts to external services like Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, etc.\n */\nexport class WebhookChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name: string;\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly webhookUrl: string,\n    private readonly channelName: string = 'webhook',\n  ) {\n    this.name = channelName;\n  }\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      const payload = this.formatPayload(message);\n\n      logger.info(\n        { channel: this.name, title: message.title, severity: message.severity },\n        'Webhook notification sent',\n      );\n\n      // Placeholder for actual HTTP call:\n      // await axios.post(this.webhookUrl, payload);\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err, channel: this.name }, 'Failed to send webhook notification');\n      throw err;\n    }\n  }\n\n  private formatPayload(message: AlertMessage): Record<string, unknown> {\n    // Format for Slack-style webhooks\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('hooks.slack.com')) {\n      return {\n        text: `*${message.severity.toUpperCase()}* alert from *${message.sourceModule}*`,\n        attachments: [\n          {\n            color: this.getSeverityColor(message.severity),\n            title: message.title,\n            fields: Object.entries(message.details).map(([key, value]) => ({\n              title: key,\n              value: String(value),\n              short: true,\n            })),\n          },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Format for Microsoft Teams\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('teams.microsoft.com')) {\n      return {\n        '@type': 'MessageCard',\n        themeColor: this.getSeverityColor(message.severity),\n        title: `${message.severity.toUpperCase()}: ${message.title}`,\n        text: `Alert from ${message.sourceModule}`,\n        sections: [\n          {\n            facts: Object.entries(message.details).map(([key, value]) => ({\n              name: key,\n              value: String(value),\n            })),\n          },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Format for PagerDuty\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('pagerduty.com')) {\n      return {\n        routing_key: notificationConfig.pagerDutyKey,\n        event_action: 'trigger',\n        payload: {\n          summary: `${message.title} [${message.sourceModule}]`,\n          severity: message.severity === 'critical' ? 'critical' : message.severity === 'warning' ? 'warning' : 'info',\n          source: `PulseSphere/${message.sourceModule}`,\n          custom_details: message.details,\n        },\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Generic format\n    return {\n      severity: message.severity,\n      title: message.title,\n      details: message.details,\n      sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    };\n  }\n\n  private getSeverityColor(severity: AlertSeverity): string {\n    switch (severity) {\n      case 'critical':\n        return '#dc3545'; // red\n      case 'warning':\n        return '#ffc107'; // yellow\n      case 'info':\n        return '#17a2b8'; // blue\n      default:\n        return '#6c757d'; // gray\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Log notification channel.\n * Writes alerts to the application log with appropriate severity level.\n */\nexport class LogChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name = 'log';\n\n  private readonly channelLogger: pino.Logger;\n\n  constructor() {\n    this.channelLogger = pino({\n      name: 'alert-log-channel',\n      level: 'trace',\n    });\n  }\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    const logPayload = {\n      alertTitle: message.title,\n      alertSeverity: message.severity,\n      sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n      details: message.details,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    };\n\n    switch (message.severity) {\n      case 'critical':\n        this.channelLogger.fatal(logPayload, `CRITICAL ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n      case 'warning':\n        this.channelLogger.warn(logPayload, `WARNING ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n      case 'info':\n      default:\n        this.channelLogger.info(logPayload, `INFO ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                           NotificationService                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Central notification service that dispatches alerts to configured channels.\n */\nexport class NotificationService {\n  private readonly channels: NotificationChannel[];\n  private readonly serviceLogger: pino.Logger;\n\n  constructor(channels: NotificationChannel[]) {\n    this.channels = channels;\n    this.serviceLogger = pino({\n      name: 'notification-service',\n      level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Dispatch an alert message to all configured notification channels.\n   * Errors from individual channels are logged but do not stop other channels.\n   *\n   * @param alert The alert message to dispatch\n   */\n  async dispatch(alert: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    this.serviceLogger.debug(\n      { alert, channelCount: this.channels.length },\n      'Dispatching alert to notification channels',\n    );\n\n    const results = await Promise.allSettled(\n      this.channels.map(async (channel) => {\n        try {\n          await channel.send(alert);\n          return { channel: channel.name, success: true };\n        } catch (err) {\n          this.serviceLogger.error(\n            { err, channel: channel.name, alertTitle: alert.title },\n            'Channel failed to send notification',\n          );\n          return { channel: channel.name, success: false, error: err };\n        }\n      }),\n    );\n\n    const failedChannels = results.filter(\n      (r) => r.status === 'rejected' || (r.status === 'fulfilled' && !r.value.success),\n    );\n\n    if (failedChannels.length > 0) {\n      this.serviceLogger.warn(\n        { failedCount: failedChannels.length, totalChannels: this.channels.length },\n        'Some notification channels failed',\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get the list of configured channel names.\n   */\n  getChannelNames(): string[] {\n    return this.channels.map((c) => c.name);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Singleton Instance Factory                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Create the default NotificationService instance with channels\n * configured from the notification config.\n */\nfunction createNotificationService(): NotificationService {\n  const channels: NotificationChannel[] = [];\n\n  // Always add the log channel as a fallback\n  channels.push(new LogChannel());\n\n  // Add email channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.smtpEndpoint && notificationConfig.emailRecipients.length > 0) {\n    channels.push(\n      new EmailChannel(notificationConfig.smtpEndpoint, notificationConfig.emailRecipients),\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Add Slack webhook channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.slackWebhookUrl) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.slackWebhookUrl, 'slack'));\n  }\n\n  // Add PagerDuty channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.pagerDutyEndpoint && notificationConfig.pagerDutyKey) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.pagerDutyEndpoint, 'pagerduty'));\n  }\n\n  // Add Teams webhook channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.teamsWebhookUrl) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.teamsWebhookUrl, 'teams'));\n  }\n\n  logger.info(\n    { channels: channels.map((c) => c.name) },\n    'NotificationService initialized with channels',\n  );\n\n  return new NotificationService(channels);\n}\n\n/**\n * Singleton instance of the NotificationService.\n * Import this to send alerts from anywhere in the application.\n */\nexport const notificationService = createNotificationService();\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Convenience Exports                            */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport { notificationConfig };\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/services/notificationService.ts"
}
Output
{
  "content": "/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Unified Notification Service\n * ======================================================\n * src/services/notificationService.ts\n *\n * This module provides a centralized, extensible notification service for\n * dispatching alerts across multiple channels (email, webhooks, logs).\n * It consolidates disparate alerting mechanisms scattered across the codebase\n * into a single, cohesive interface.\n *\n * Design Patterns:\n *  - Strategy Pattern: Each NotificationChannel is a pluggable strategy\n *  - Singleton Pattern: A single NotificationService instance for the app\n *  - Observer/Publisher: Channels are notified when alerts are dispatched\n *\n * Usage:\n *   import { notificationService, AlertMessage } from './services/notificationService';\n *\n *   await notificationService.dispatch({\n *     severity: 'critical',\n *     title: 'CPU Usage Alert',\n *     details: { cpuUsage: 95, threshold: 80 },\n *     sourceModule: 'module_11',\n *   });\n */\n\nimport pino from 'pino';\nimport { notificationConfig } from '../config/notifications';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Type Definitions                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Severity levels for alert messages.\n */\nexport type AlertSeverity = 'critical' | 'warning' | 'info';\n\n/**\n * Unified alert message structure used throughout the application.\n */\nexport interface AlertMessage {\n  /** Severity level of the alert */\n  severity: AlertSeverity;\n  /** Short, descriptive title of the alert */\n  title: string;\n  /** Additional context and details about the alert */\n  details: Record<string, any>;\n  /** Identifier of the module that generated the alert */\n  sourceModule: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Contract for notification channel implementations.\n * Each channel is responsible for delivering alerts through a specific medium.\n */\nexport interface NotificationChannel {\n  /** Unique identifier for the channel */\n  readonly name: string;\n  /**\n   * Send an alert message through this channel.\n   * @param message The alert message to send\n   */\n  send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Logger                                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nconst logger = pino({\n  name: 'notification-service',\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                        Concrete Channel Implementations                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Email notification channel.\n * Sends alert emails via SMTP endpoint.\n */\nexport class EmailChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name = 'email';\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly smtpEndpoint: string,\n    private readonly recipients: string[],\n  ) {}\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      // In production, this would integrate with SES, SendGrid, SMTP, etc.\n      // For now, we simulate the HTTP call\n      const payload = {\n        to: this.recipients,\n        subject: `[PulseSphere] ${message.severity.toUpperCase()} \u2013 ${message.title}`,\n        body: JSON.stringify(\n          {\n            severity: message.severity,\n            title: message.title,\n            details: message.details,\n            sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n            timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n          },\n          null,\n          2,\n        ),\n      };\n\n      logger.info(\n        { channel: this.name, recipients: this.recipients, title: message.title },\n        'Email notification sent',\n      );\n\n      // Placeholder for actual HTTP call:\n      // await axios.post(`${this.smtpEndpoint}/send`, payload);\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err, channel: this.name }, 'Failed to send email notification');\n      throw err;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Webhook notification channel.\n * Posts alerts to external services like Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, etc.\n */\nexport class WebhookChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name: string;\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly webhookUrl: string,\n    private readonly channelName: string = 'webhook',\n  ) {\n    this.name = channelName;\n  }\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      const payload = this.formatPayload(message);\n\n      logger.info(\n        { channel: this.name, title: message.title, severity: message.severity },\n        'Webhook notification sent',\n      );\n\n      // Placeholder for actual HTTP call:\n      // await axios.post(this.webhookUrl, payload);\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error({ err, channel: this.name }, 'Failed to send webhook notification');\n      throw err;\n    }\n  }\n\n  private formatPayload(message: AlertMessage): Record<string, unknown> {\n    // Format for Slack-style webhooks\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('hooks.slack.com')) {\n      return {\n        text: `*${message.severity.toUpperCase()}* alert from *${message.sourceModule}*`,\n        attachments: [\n          {\n            color: this.getSeverityColor(message.severity),\n            title: message.title,\n            fields: Object.entries(message.details).map(([key, value]) => ({\n              title: key,\n              value: String(value),\n              short: true,\n            })),\n          },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Format for Microsoft Teams\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('teams.microsoft.com')) {\n      return {\n        '@type': 'MessageCard',\n        themeColor: this.getSeverityColor(message.severity),\n        title: `${message.severity.toUpperCase()}: ${message.title}`,\n        text: `Alert from ${message.sourceModule}`,\n        sections: [\n          {\n            facts: Object.entries(message.details).map(([key, value]) => ({\n              name: key,\n              value: String(value),\n            })),\n          },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Format for PagerDuty\n    if (this.webhookUrl.includes('pagerduty.com')) {\n      return {\n        routing_key: notificationConfig.pagerDutyKey,\n        event_action: 'trigger',\n        payload: {\n          summary: `${message.title} [${message.sourceModule}]`,\n          severity: message.severity === 'critical' ? 'critical' : message.severity === 'warning' ? 'warning' : 'info',\n          source: `PulseSphere/${message.sourceModule}`,\n          custom_details: message.details,\n        },\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Generic format\n    return {\n      severity: message.severity,\n      title: message.title,\n      details: message.details,\n      sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    };\n  }\n\n  private getSeverityColor(severity: AlertSeverity): string {\n    switch (severity) {\n      case 'critical':\n        return '#dc3545'; // red\n      case 'warning':\n        return '#ffc107'; // yellow\n      case 'info':\n        return '#17a2b8'; // blue\n      default:\n        return '#6c757d'; // gray\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Log notification channel.\n * Writes alerts to the application log with appropriate severity level.\n */\nexport class LogChannel implements NotificationChannel {\n  readonly name = 'log';\n\n  private readonly channelLogger: pino.Logger;\n\n  constructor() {\n    this.channelLogger = pino({\n      name: 'alert-log-channel',\n      level: 'trace',\n    });\n  }\n\n  async send(message: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    const logPayload = {\n      alertTitle: message.title,\n      alertSeverity: message.severity,\n      sourceModule: message.sourceModule,\n      details: message.details,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    };\n\n    switch (message.severity) {\n      case 'critical':\n        this.channelLogger.fatal(logPayload, `CRITICAL ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n      case 'warning':\n        this.channelLogger.warn(logPayload, `WARNING ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n      case 'info':\n      default:\n        this.channelLogger.info(logPayload, `INFO ALERT: ${message.title}`);\n        break;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                           NotificationService                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Central notification service that dispatches alerts to configured channels.\n */\nexport class NotificationService {\n  private readonly channels: NotificationChannel[];\n  private readonly serviceLogger: pino.Logger;\n\n  constructor(channels: NotificationChannel[]) {\n    this.channels = channels;\n    this.serviceLogger = pino({\n      name: 'notification-service',\n      level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Dispatch an alert message to all configured notification channels.\n   * Errors from individual channels are logged but do not stop other channels.\n   *\n   * @param alert The alert message to dispatch\n   */\n  async dispatch(alert: AlertMessage): Promise<void> {\n    this.serviceLogger.debug(\n      { alert, channelCount: this.channels.length },\n      'Dispatching alert to notification channels',\n    );\n\n    const results = await Promise.allSettled(\n      this.channels.map(async (channel) => {\n        try {\n          await channel.send(alert);\n          return { channel: channel.name, success: true };\n        } catch (err) {\n          this.serviceLogger.error(\n            { err, channel: channel.name, alertTitle: alert.title },\n            'Channel failed to send notification',\n          );\n          return { channel: channel.name, success: false, error: err };\n        }\n      }),\n    );\n\n    const failedChannels = results.filter(\n      (r) => r.status === 'rejected' || (r.status === 'fulfilled' && !r.value.success),\n    );\n\n    if (failedChannels.length > 0) {\n      this.serviceLogger.warn(\n        { failedCount: failedChannels.length, totalChannels: this.channels.length },\n        'Some notification channels failed',\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get the list of configured channel names.\n   */\n  getChannelNames(): string[] {\n    return this.channels.map((c) => c.name);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Singleton Instance Factory                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Create the default NotificationService instance with channels\n * configured from the notification config.\n */\nfunction createNotificationService(): NotificationService {\n  const channels: NotificationChannel[] = [];\n\n  // Always add the log channel as a fallback\n  channels.push(new LogChannel());\n\n  // Add email channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.smtpEndpoint && notificationConfig.emailRecipients.length > 0) {\n    channels.push(\n      new EmailChannel(notificationConfig.smtpEndpoint, notificationConfig.emailRecipients),\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Add Slack webhook channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.slackWebhookUrl) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.slackWebhookUrl, 'slack'));\n  }\n\n  // Add PagerDuty channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.pagerDutyEndpoint && notificationConfig.pagerDutyKey) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.pagerDutyEndpoint, 'pagerduty'));\n  }\n\n  // Add Teams webhook channel if configured\n  if (notificationConfig.teamsWebhookUrl) {\n    channels.push(new WebhookChannel(notificationConfig.teamsWebhookUrl, 'teams'));\n  }\n\n  logger.info(\n    { channels: channels.map((c) => c.name) },\n    'NotificationService initialized with channels',\n  );\n\n  return new NotificationService(channels);\n}\n\n/**\n * Singleton instance of the NotificationService.\n * Import this to send alerts from anywhere in the application.\n */\nexport const notificationService = createNotificationService();\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Convenience Exports                            */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport { notificationConfig };\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/services/notificationService.ts",
  "originalFile": null,
