52. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_11.ts"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**************************************************************************************************\n * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Adaptive Threshold Manager                                             *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * File:        src/module_11.ts                                                                  *\n * Description: Dynamically adjusts alert thresholds for infrastructure metrics by weaving        *\n *              real-time social signals (likes, comments, shares, hashtag bursts) into           *\n *              the decision model. Combines Strategy, Observer and Chain-of-Responsibility       *\n *              patterns to (1) calculate adaptive thresholds, (2) observe social-metric events   *\n *              over Kafka, and (3) escalate incidents through multiple notifiers.                *\n *                                                                                                \n * Author:      PulseSphere Engineering Team                                                      *\n **************************************************************************************************/\n\n/* eslint-disable import/order */\nimport { Kafka, EachMessagePayload } from 'kafkajs';\nimport { Counter, Gauge, register } from 'prom-client';\nimport axios from 'axios';\nimport winston from 'winston';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Domain Models\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * A domain event emitted when a social interaction spike is detected.\n */\nexport interface SocialMetricEvent {\n  readonly tenantId: string;            // e.g. 'acme-social'\n  readonly metric: 'LIKE' | 'COMMENT' | 'SHARE' | 'STREAM_VIEW';\n  readonly delta: number;               // delta per second\n  readonly timestamp: number;           // epoch millis\n}\n\n/**\n * Infrastructure alert model.\n */\nexport interface Alert {\n  readonly tenantId: string;\n  readonly severity: 'INFO' | 'WARN' | 'CRIT';\n  readonly metricName: string;          // e.g. 'cpu_usage'\n  readonly observedValue: number;\n  readonly threshold: number;\n  readonly context: Record<string, unknown>;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logging\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nconst logger = winston.createLogger({\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n  format: winston.format.combine(\n    winston.format.timestamp(),\n    winston.format.errors({ stack: true }),\n    winston.format.json(),\n  ),\n  transports: [\n    new winston.transports.Console({ stderrLevels: ['error', 'warn'] }),\n  ],\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Strategy Pattern \u2013 Threshold calculators\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * Strategy interface for transforming a base threshold.\n */\nexport interface ThresholdStrategy {\n  /**\n   * Compute the adjusted threshold based on a social metric event.\n   */\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Boosts threshold proportionally when sudden spikes (virality) occur.\n */\nexport class TrendingBoostStrategy implements ThresholdStrategy {\n  private readonly boostFactor: number;\n\n  constructor(boostFactor = 1.5) {\n    this.boostFactor = boostFactor;\n  }\n\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    // Simple heuristic: amplify threshold when delta exceeds 1k events/second.\n    if (event.delta > 1_000) {\n      const boosted = baseThreshold * this.boostFactor;\n      logger.debug(`TrendingBoostStrategy applied: ${baseThreshold} -> ${boosted}`);\n      return boosted;\n    }\n    return baseThreshold;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Relaxes threshold during off-peak night hours to reduce false positives.\n */\nexport class NightTimeRelaxStrategy implements ThresholdStrategy {\n  constructor(private readonly relaxFactor = 0.8) {}\n\n  private isNightHour(utcMillis: number): boolean {\n    const hour = new Date(utcMillis).getUTCHours();\n    return hour >= 0 && hour <= 6; // midnight \u2013 6 AM UTC\n  }\n\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    if (this.isNightHour(event.timestamp)) {\n      const relaxed = baseThreshold * this.relaxFactor;\n      logger.debug(`NightTimeRelaxStrategy applied: ${baseThreshold} -> ${relaxed}`);\n      return relaxed;\n    }\n    return baseThreshold;\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Chain-of-Responsibility \u2013 Escalation pipeline\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * Abstract escalation handler.\n */\nabstract class EscalationHandler {\n  private nextHandler?: EscalationHandler;\n\n  setNext(handler: EscalationHandler): EscalationHandler {\n    this.nextHandler = handler;\n    return handler;\n  }\n\n  async handle(alert: Alert): Promise<void> {\n    const processed = await this.process(alert);\n    if (!processed && this.nextHandler) {\n      await this.nextHandler.handle(alert);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * @returns true if handled, false to delegate.\n   */\n  protected abstract process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Sends alerts to PagerDuty.\n */\nclass PagerDutyHandler extends EscalationHandler {\n  private readonly routingKey: string;\n\n  constructor(routingKey: string) {\n    super();\n    this.routingKey = routingKey;\n  }\n\n  /* c8 ignore next 14 */\n  protected async process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean> {\n    if (alert.severity === 'CRIT') {\n      try {\n        await axios.post('https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue', {\n          routing_key: this.routingKey,\n          event_action: 'trigger',\n          payload: {\n            summary: `CRITICAL alert for ${alert.metricName}`,\n            severity: 'critical',\n            source: 'PulseSphere',\n            custom_details: alert,\n          },\n        });\n        logger.info(`PagerDuty notified for alert=${alert.metricName}`);\n        return true;\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error('PagerDuty notification failed', err as Error);\n      }\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Posts notifications to Slack.\n */\nclass SlackHandler extends EscalationHandler {\n  constructor(private readonly webhookUrl: string) {\n    super();\n  }\n\n  protected async process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean> {\n    if (alert.severity !== 'INFO') {\n      try {\n        await axios.post(this.webhookUrl, {\n          text: `*${alert.severity}* alert for *${alert.metricName}* \u2011 ` +\n                `observed=${alert.observedValue}, threshold=${alert.threshold}`,\n        });\n        logger.info(`Slack notified for alert=${alert.metricName}`);\n        return true;\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error('Slack notification failed', err as Error);\n      }\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Fallback email notifier.\n */\nclass EmailHandler extends EscalationHandler {\n  constructor(private readonly smtpEndpoint: string) {\n    super();\n  }\n\n  protected async process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean> {\n    // In production we'd integrate with SES, SendGrid, etc.\n    try {\n      await axios.post(`${this.smtpEndpoint}/send`, {\n        to: 'ops@pulsesphere.io',\n        subject: `[PulseSphere] ${alert.severity} alert \u2013 ${alert.metricName}`,\n        body: JSON.stringify(alert, null, 2),\n      });\n      logger.info(`Email sent for alert=${alert.metricName}`);\n      return true;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error('Email notification failed', err as Error);\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Observer Pattern \u2013 Kafka social metrics consumer\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nclass KafkaSocialMetricObserver {\n  private readonly kafka: Kafka;\n\n  constructor(private readonly brokers: string[], private readonly groupId: string) {\n    this.kafka = new Kafka({ clientId: 'social-metric-consumer', brokers });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Subscribes to topic and invokes callback for each message.\n   */\n  async onEvent(\n    topic: string,\n    callback: (event: SocialMetricEvent) => Promise<void>,\n  ): Promise<void> {\n    const consumer = this.kafka.consumer({ groupId: this.groupId });\n\n    await consumer.connect();\n    await consumer.subscribe({ topic, fromBeginning: false });\n\n    await consumer.run({\n      // 10 concurrent partitions\n      eachMessage: async ({ message }: EachMessagePayload) => {\n        try {\n          if (!message.value) return;\n          const parsed: SocialMetricEvent = JSON.parse(message.value.toString());\n          await callback(parsed);\n        } catch (err) {\n          logger.warn('Failed to process social metric event', err as Error);\n        }\n      },\n    });\n\n    logger.info(`KafkaSocialMetricObserver subscribed to ${topic}`);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Adaptive Threshold Manager \u2013 Orchestrates strategies & escalations\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nexport class AdaptiveThresholdManager {\n  private readonly strategies: ThresholdStrategy[] = [];\n  private readonly escalationChain: EscalationHandler;\n  private readonly cpuUsageGauge: Gauge<number>;\n  private readonly thresholdGauge: Gauge<number>;\n  private readonly alertCounter: Counter;\n\n  constructor(\n    escalationChain: EscalationHandler,\n    private readonly defaultThreshold: number,\n  ) {\n    this.escalationChain = escalationChain;\n\n    // Prometheus metrics\n    this.cpuUsageGauge = new Gauge({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_cpu_usage',\n      help: 'Current CPU usage',\n      labelNames: ['tenant'],\n    });\n\n    this.thresholdGauge = new Gauge({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_cpu_threshold',\n      help: 'Dynamically calculated CPU threshold',\n      labelNames: ['tenant'],\n    });\n\n    this.alertCounter = new Counter({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_alert_total',\n      help: 'Number of alerts emitted',\n      labelNames: ['tenant', 'severity'],\n    });\n  }\n\n  addStrategy(strategy: ThresholdStrategy): AdaptiveThresholdManager {\n    this.strategies.push(strategy);\n    return this;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Recalculate thresholds using registered strategies.\n   */\n  private computeThreshold(event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    return this.strategies.reduce(\n      (threshold, strat) => strat.compute(threshold, event),\n      this.defaultThreshold,\n    );\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Simulates reading infra metric (e.g., CPU) from monitoring stack.\n   * In production, this would fetch from Prometheus, Datadog, etc.\n   */\n  /* c8 ignore next */\n  private async getCurrentCpuUsage(tenantId: string): Promise<number> {\n    // Placeholder random CPU usage for demonstration.\n    return Math.random() * 100;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Processes a social metric event \u2192 recompute threshold \u2192 evaluate infra metrics \u2192\n   * possibly trigger alert & escalation.\n   */\n  async handleSocialEvent(event: SocialMetricEvent): Promise<void> {\n    const tenantLabel = { tenant: event.tenantId };\n    const threshold = this.computeThreshold(event);\n    this.thresholdGauge.set(tenantLabel, threshold);\n\n    const cpuUsage = await this.getCurrentCpuUsage(event.tenantId);\n    this.cpuUsageGauge.set(tenantLabel, cpuUsage);\n\n    if (cpuUsage > threshold) {\n      const severity: Alert['severity'] = cpuUsage > threshold * 1.2 ? 'CRIT' : 'WARN';\n\n      const alert: Alert = {\n        tenantId: event.tenantId,\n        severity,\n        metricName: 'cpu_usage',\n        observedValue: cpuUsage,\n        threshold,\n        context: { socialMetric: event },\n      };\n\n      this.alertCounter.inc({ ...tenantLabel, severity });\n      logger.warn(\n        `Alert triggered tenant=${event.tenantId} cpu=${cpuUsage.toFixed(2)} ` +\n        `threshold=${threshold.toFixed(2)} severity=${severity}`,\n      );\n\n      await this.escalationChain.handle(alert);\n    } else {\n      logger.debug(\n        `CPU within threshold tenant=${event.tenantId} cpu=${cpuUsage.toFixed(2)} ` +\n        `threshold=${threshold.toFixed(2)}`,\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Bootstrap \u2013 wire everything together if executed directly\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  (async () => {\n    try {\n      // Build escalation chain: PagerDuty \u2192 Slack \u2192 Email\n      const pagerDuty = new PagerDutyHandler(process.env.PAGERDUTY_KEY ?? 'dummy');\n      const slack = new SlackHandler(process.env.SLACK_WEBHOOK ?? 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/dummy');\n      const email = new EmailHandler(process.env.SMTP_ENDPOINT ?? 'https://smtp.internal');\n      pagerDuty.setNext(slack).setNext(email);\n\n      // Instantiate manager with strategies\n      const manager = new AdaptiveThresholdManager(pagerDuty, 75 /* default CPU threshold */)\n        .addStrategy(new TrendingBoostStrategy())\n        .addStrategy(new NightTimeRelaxStrategy());\n\n      // Observer listens to Kafka topic\n      const observer = new KafkaSocialMetricObserver(\n        (process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS ?? 'localhost:9092').split(','),\n        'adaptive-threshold-group',\n      );\n\n      await observer.onEvent('social-metrics', (evt) => manager.handleSocialEvent(evt));\n\n      // Expose Prometheus endpoint\n      const port = Number(process.env.METRICS_PORT ?? 9100);\n      /* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires */\n      const http = require('http');\n      http.createServer((_, res) => {\n        res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': register.contentType });\n        register.metrics().then((m: string) => res.end(m));\n      }).listen(port, () => logger.info(`Metrics exposed on :${port}/`));\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error('Failed to bootstrap AdaptiveThresholdManager', err as Error);\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n  })();\n}\n```\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_11.ts",
    "numLines": 415,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 415
  },
  "type": "text"
}
53. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/**************************************************************************************************\n * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Adaptive Threshold Manager                                             *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * File:        src/module_11.ts                                                                  *\n * Description: Dynamically adjusts alert thresholds for infrastructure metrics by weaving        *\n *              real-time social signals (likes, comments, shares, hashtag bursts) into           *\n *              the decision model. Combines Strategy, Observer and Chain-of-Responsibility       *\n *              patterns to (1) calculate adaptive thresholds, (2) observe social-metric events   *\n *              over Kafka, and (3) escalate incidents through the centralized NotificationService*\n *\n * Author:      PulseSphere Engineering Team                                                      *\n **************************************************************************************************/\n\n/* eslint-disable import/order */\nimport { Kafka, EachMessagePayload } from 'kafkajs';\nimport { Counter, Gauge, register } from 'prom-client';\nimport winston from 'winston';\nimport { notificationService, AlertMessage } from './services/notificationService';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Domain Models\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * A domain event emitted when a social interaction spike is detected.\n */\nexport interface SocialMetricEvent {\n  readonly tenantId: string;            // e.g. 'acme-social'\n  readonly metric: 'LIKE' | 'COMMENT' | 'SHARE' | 'STREAM_VIEW';\n  readonly delta: number;               // delta per second\n  readonly timestamp: number;           // epoch millis\n}\n\n/**\n * Infrastructure alert model.\n */\nexport interface Alert {\n  readonly tenantId: string;\n  readonly severity: 'INFO' | 'WARN' | 'CRIT';\n  readonly metricName: string;          // e.g. 'cpu_usage'\n  readonly observedValue: number;\n  readonly threshold: number;\n  readonly context: Record<string, unknown>;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logging\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nconst logger = winston.createLogger({\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n  format: winston.format.combine(\n    winston.format.timestamp(),\n    winston.format.errors({ stack: true }),\n    winston.format.json(),\n  ),\n  transports: [\n    new winston.transports.Console({ stderrLevels: ['error', 'warn'] }),\n  ],\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Strategy Pattern \u2013 Threshold calculators\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * Strategy interface for transforming a base threshold.\n */\nexport interface ThresholdStrategy {\n  /**\n   * Compute the adjusted threshold based on a social metric event.\n   */\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Boosts threshold proportionally when sudden spikes (virality) occur.\n */\nexport class TrendingBoostStrategy implements ThresholdStrategy {\n  private readonly boostFactor: number;\n\n  constructor(boostFactor = 1.5) {\n    this.boostFactor = boostFactor;\n  }\n\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    // Simple heuristic: amplify threshold when delta exceeds 1k events/second.\n    if (event.delta > 1_000) {\n      const boosted = baseThreshold * this.boostFactor;\n      logger.debug(`TrendingBoostStrategy applied: ${baseThreshold} -> ${boosted}`);\n      return boosted;\n    }\n    return baseThreshold;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Relaxes threshold during off-peak night hours to reduce false positives.\n */\nexport class NightTimeRelaxStrategy implements ThresholdStrategy {\n  constructor(private readonly relaxFactor = 0.8) {}\n\n  private isNightHour(utcMillis: number): boolean {\n    const hour = new Date(utcMillis).getUTCHours();\n    return hour >= 0 && hour <= 6; // midnight \u2013 6 AM UTC\n  }\n\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    if (this.isNightHour(event.timestamp)) {\n      const relaxed = baseThreshold * this.relaxFactor;\n      logger.debug(`NightTimeRelaxStrategy applied: ${baseThreshold} -> ${relaxed}`);\n      return relaxed;\n    }\n    return baseThreshold;\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Alert Dispatcher \u2013 Uses centralized NotificationService\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * Convert internal alert severity to NotificationService severity.\n */\nfunction mapSeverity(severity: Alert['severity']): AlertMessage['severity'] {\n  switch (severity) {\n    case 'CRIT':\n      return 'critical';\n    case 'WARN':\n      return 'warning';\n    case 'INFO':\n    default:\n      return 'info';\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Dispatches alerts through the centralized NotificationService.\n */\nasync function dispatchAlert(alert: Alert): Promise<void> {\n  const alertMessage: AlertMessage = {\n    severity: mapSeverity(alert.severity),\n    title: `${alert.severity} alert for ${alert.metricName}`,\n    details: {\n      tenantId: alert.tenantId,\n      metricName: alert.metricName,\n      observedValue: alert.observedValue,\n      threshold: alert.threshold,\n      ...alert.context,\n    },\n    sourceModule: 'module_11',\n  };\n\n  await notificationService.dispatch(alertMessage);\n  logger.info(`Alert dispatched via NotificationService: ${alert.metricName}`);\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Observer Pattern \u2013 Kafka social metrics consumer\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nclass KafkaSocialMetricObserver {\n  private readonly kafka: Kafka;\n\n  constructor(private readonly brokers: string[], private readonly groupId: string) {\n    this.kafka = new Kafka({ clientId: 'social-metric-consumer', brokers });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Subscribes to topic and invokes callback for each message.\n   */\n  async onEvent(\n    topic: string,\n    callback: (event: SocialMetricEvent) => Promise<void>,\n  ): Promise<void> {\n    const consumer = this.kafka.consumer({ groupId: this.groupId });\n\n    await consumer.connect();\n    await consumer.subscribe({ topic, fromBeginning: false });\n\n    await consumer.run({\n      // 10 concurrent partitions\n      eachMessage: async ({ message }: EachMessagePayload) => {\n        try {\n          if (!message.value) return;\n          const parsed: SocialMetricEvent = JSON.parse(message.value.toString());\n          await callback(parsed);\n        } catch (err) {\n          logger.warn('Failed to process social metric event', err as Error);\n        }\n      },\n    });\n\n    logger.info(`KafkaSocialMetricObserver subscribed to ${topic}`);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Adaptive Threshold Manager \u2013 Orchestrates strategies & alert dispatching\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nexport class AdaptiveThresholdManager {\n  private readonly strategies: ThresholdStrategy[] = [];\n  private readonly cpuUsageGauge: Gauge<number>;\n  private readonly thresholdGauge: Gauge<number>;\n  private readonly alertCounter: Counter;\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly defaultThreshold: number,\n  ) {\n    // Prometheus metrics\n    this.cpuUsageGauge = new Gauge({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_cpu_usage',\n      help: 'Current CPU usage',\n      labelNames: ['tenant'],\n    });\n\n    this.thresholdGauge = new Gauge({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_cpu_threshold',\n      help: 'Dynamically calculated CPU threshold',\n      labelNames: ['tenant'],\n    });\n\n    this.alertCounter = new Counter({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_alert_total',\n      help: 'Number of alerts emitted',\n      labelNames: ['tenant', 'severity'],\n    });\n  }\n\n  addStrategy(strategy: ThresholdStrategy): AdaptiveThresholdManager {\n    this.strategies.push(strategy);\n    return this;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Recalculate thresholds using registered strategies.\n   */\n  private computeThreshold(event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    return this.strategies.reduce(\n      (threshold, strat) => strat.compute(threshold, event),\n      this.defaultThreshold,\n    );\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Simulates reading infra metric (e.g., CPU) from monitoring stack.\n   * In production, this would fetch from Prometheus, Datadog, etc.\n   */\n  /* c8 ignore next */\n  private async getCurrentCpuUsage(tenantId: string): Promise<number> {\n    // Placeholder random CPU usage for demonstration.\n    return Math.random() * 100;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Processes a social metric event \u2192 recompute threshold \u2192 evaluate infra metrics \u2192\n   * possibly trigger alert & dispatch via NotificationService.\n   */\n  async handleSocialEvent(event: SocialMetricEvent): Promise<void> {\n    const tenantLabel = { tenant: event.tenantId };\n    const threshold = this.computeThreshold(event);\n    this.thresholdGauge.set(tenantLabel, threshold);\n\n    const cpuUsage = await this.getCurrentCpuUsage(event.tenantId);\n    this.cpuUsageGauge.set(tenantLabel, cpuUsage);\n\n    if (cpuUsage > threshold) {\n      const severity: Alert['severity'] = cpuUsage > threshold * 1.2 ? 'CRIT' : 'WARN';\n\n      const alert: Alert = {\n        tenantId: event.tenantId,\n        severity,\n        metricName: 'cpu_usage',\n        observedValue: cpuUsage,\n        threshold,\n        context: { socialMetric: event },\n      };\n\n      this.alertCounter.inc({ ...tenantLabel, severity });\n      logger.warn(\n        `Alert triggered tenant=${event.tenantId} cpu=${cpuUsage.toFixed(2)} ` +\n        `threshold=${threshold.toFixed(2)} severity=${severity}`,\n      );\n\n      await dispatchAlert(alert);\n    } else {\n      logger.debug(\n        `CPU within threshold tenant=${event.tenantId} cpu=${cpuUsage.toFixed(2)} ` +\n        `threshold=${threshold.toFixed(2)}`,\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Bootstrap \u2013 wire everything together if executed directly\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  (async () => {\n    try {\n      // Instantiate manager with strategies (no longer needs escalation chain)\n      const manager = new AdaptiveThresholdManager(75 /* default CPU threshold */)\n        .addStrategy(new TrendingBoostStrategy())\n        .addStrategy(new NightTimeRelaxStrategy());\n\n      // Observer listens to Kafka topic\n      const observer = new KafkaSocialMetricObserver(\n        (process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS ?? 'localhost:9092').split(','),\n        'adaptive-threshold-group',\n      );\n\n      await observer.onEvent('social-metrics', (evt) => manager.handleSocialEvent(evt));\n\n      // Expose Prometheus endpoint\n      const port = Number(process.env.METRICS_PORT ?? 9100);\n      /* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires */\n      const http = require('http');\n      http.createServer((_, res) => {\n        res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': register.contentType });\n        register.metrics().then((m: string) => res.end(m));\n      }).listen(port, () => logger.info(`Metrics exposed on :${port}/`));\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error('Failed to bootstrap AdaptiveThresholdManager', err as Error);\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n  })();\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_11.ts"
}

Combines Strategy, Observer and Chain-of-Responsibility       *\n *              patterns to (1) calculate adaptive thresholds, (2) observe social-metric events   *\n *              over Kafka, and (3) escalate incidents through the centralized NotificationService*\n *\n * Author:      PulseSphere Engineering Team                                                      *\n **************************************************************************************************/\n\n/* eslint-disable import/order */\nimport { Kafka, EachMessagePayload } from 'kafkajs';\nimport { Counter, Gauge, register } from 'prom-client';\nimport winston from 'winston';\nimport { notificationService, AlertMessage } from './services/notificationService';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Domain Models\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * A domain event emitted when a social interaction spike is detected.\n */\nexport interface SocialMetricEvent {\n  readonly tenantId: string;            // e.g. 'acme-social'\n  readonly metric: 'LIKE' | 'COMMENT' | 'SHARE' | 'STREAM_VIEW';\n  readonly delta: number;               // delta per second\n  readonly timestamp: number;           // epoch millis\n}\n\n/**\n * Infrastructure alert model.\n */\nexport interface Alert {\n  readonly tenantId: string;\n  readonly severity: 'INFO' | 'WARN' | 'CRIT';\n  readonly metricName: string;          // e.g. 'cpu_usage'\n  readonly observedValue: number;\n  readonly threshold: number;\n  readonly context: Record<string, unknown>;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logging\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nconst logger = winston.createLogger({\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n  format: winston.format.combine(\n    winston.format.timestamp(),\n    winston.format.errors({ stack: true }),\n    winston.format.json(),\n  ),\n  transports: [\n    new winston.transports.Console({ stderrLevels: ['error', 'warn'] }),\n  ],\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Strategy Pattern \u2013 Threshold calculators\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * Strategy interface for transforming a base threshold.\n */\nexport interface ThresholdStrategy {\n  /**\n   * Compute the adjusted threshold based on a social metric event.\n   */\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Boosts threshold proportionally when sudden spikes (virality) occur.\n */\nexport class TrendingBoostStrategy implements ThresholdStrategy {\n  private readonly boostFactor: number;\n\n  constructor(boostFactor = 1.5) {\n    this.boostFactor = boostFactor;\n  }\n\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    // Simple heuristic: amplify threshold when delta exceeds 1k events/second.\n    if (event.delta > 1_000) {\n      const boosted = baseThreshold * this.boostFactor;\n      logger.debug(`TrendingBoostStrategy applied: class NightTimeRelaxStrategy implements ThresholdStrategy {\n  constructor(private readonly relaxFactor = 0.8) {}\n\n  private isNightHour(utcMillis: number): boolean {\n    const hour = new Date(utcMillis).getUTCHours();\n    return hour >= 0 && hour <= 6; // midnight \u2013 6 AM UTC\n  }\n\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    if (this.isNightHour(event.timestamp)) {\n      const relaxed = baseThreshold * this.relaxFactor;\n      logger.debug(`NightTimeRelaxStrategy applied: ${baseThreshold} -> ${relaxed}`);\n      return relaxed;\n    }\n    return baseThreshold;\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Alert Dispatcher \u2013 Uses centralized NotificationService\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * Convert internal alert severity to NotificationService severity.\n */\nfunction mapSeverity(severity: Alert['severity']): AlertMessage['severity'] {\n  switch (severity) {\n    case 'CRIT':\n      return 'critical';\n    case 'WARN':\n      return 'warning';\n    case 'INFO':\n    default:\n      return 'info';\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Dispatches alerts through the centralized NotificationService.\n */\nasync function dispatchAlert(alert: Alert): Promise<void> {\n  const alertMessage: AlertMessage = {\n    severity: mapSeverity(alert.severity),\n    title: `${alert.severity} alert for ${alert.metricName}`,\n    details: {\n      tenantId: alert.tenantId,\n      metricName: alert.metricName,\n      observedValue: alert.observedValue,\n      threshold: alert.threshold,\n      ...alert.context,\n    },\n    sourceModule: 'module_11',\n  };\n\n  await notificationService.dispatch(alertMessage);\n  logger.info(`Alert dispatched via NotificationService: ${alert.metricName}`);\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Observer Pattern \u2013 Kafka social metrics consumer\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nclass KafkaSocialMetricObserver {\n  private readonly kafka: Kafka;\n\n  constructor(private readonly brokers: string[], private readonly groupId: string) {\n    this.kafka = new Kafka({ clientId: 'social-metric-consumer', brokers });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Subscribes to topic and invokes callback for each message.\n   */\n  async onEvent(\n    topic: string,\n    callback: (event: SocialMetricEvent) => Promise<void>,\n  ): Promise<void> {\n    const consumer = this.kafka.consumer({ groupId: this.groupId });\n\n    await consumer.connect();\n    await consumer.subscribe({ topic, fromBeginning: false });\n\n    await consumer.run({\n      // 10 concurrent partitions\n      eachMessage: async ({ message }: EachMessagePayload) => {\n        try {\n          if (!message.value) return;\n          const parsed: SocialMetricEvent = JSON.parse(message.value.toString());\n logger.info(`KafkaSocialMetricObserver subscribed to ${topic}`);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Adaptive Threshold AdaptiveThresholdManager {\n  private readonly strategies: ThresholdStrategy[] = [];\n  private readonly cpuUsageGauge: Gauge<number>;\n  private readonly thresholdGauge: Gauge<number>;\n  private readonly alertCounter: Counter;\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly defaultThreshold: number,\n  ) {\n    // Prometheus metrics\n    this.cpuUsageGauge = new Gauge({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_cpu_usage',\n      help: 'Current CPU usage',\n      labelNames: ['tenant'],\n    });\n\n    this.thresholdGauge = new Gauge({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_cpu_threshold',\n      help: 'Dynamically calculated CPU threshold',\n      labelNames: ['tenant'],\n    });\n\n    this.alertCounter = new Counter({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_alert_total',\n      help: 'Number of alerts emitted',\n      labelNames: ['tenant', 'severity'],\n    });\n  }\n\n  addStrategy(strategy: ThresholdStrategy): AdaptiveThresholdManager {\n    this.strategies.push(strategy);\n    return this;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Recalculate thresholds using registered strategies.\n   */\n  private computeThreshold(event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    return this.strategies.reduce(\n      (threshold, strat) => strat.compute(threshold, event),\n      this.defaultThreshold,\n    );\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Simulates reading infra metric (e.g., CPU) from monitoring stack.\n   * In production, this would fetch from Prometheus, Datadog, etc.\n   */\n  /* c8 ignore next */\n  private async getCurrentCpuUsage(tenantId: string): Promise<number> {\n    // Placeholder random CPU usage for demonstration.\n    return Math.random() * 100;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Processes a social metric event \u2192 recompute threshold \u2192 evaluate infra metrics \u2192\n   * possibly trigger alert & dispatch via NotificationService.\n   */\n  async handleSocialEvent(event: SocialMetricEvent): Promise<void> {\n    const tenantLabel = { tenant: event.tenantId };\n    const threshold = this.computeThreshold(event);\n    this.thresholdGauge.set(tenantLabel, threshold);\n\n    const cpuUsage = await this.getCurrentCpuUsage(event.tenantId);\n    this.cpuUsageGauge.set(tenantLabel, cpuUsage);\n\n    if (cpuUsage > threshold) {\n      const severity: Alert['severity'] = cpuUsage > threshold * 1.2 ? 'CRIT' : 'WARN';\n\n      const alert: Alert = {\n        tenantId: event.tenantId,\n        severity,\n        metricName: 'cpu_usage',\n        observedValue: cpuUsage,\n        threshold,\n        context: { socialMetric: event },\n      };\n\n      this.alertCounter.inc({ ...tenantLabel, severity });\n      logger.warn(\n        `Alert triggered tenant=${event.tenantId} cpu=${cpuUsage.toFixed(2)} ` +\n        `threshold=${threshold.toFixed(2)} severity=${severity}`,\n      );\n\n      await dispatchAlert(alert);\n    } else {\n      logger.debug(\n        `CPU within threshold tenant=${event.tenantId} cpu=${cpuUsage.toFixed(2)} ` +\n        `threshold=${threshold.toFixed(2)}`,\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Bootstrap \u2013 wire everything together if executed directly\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  (async () => {\n    try {\n      // Instantiate manager with strategies (no longer needs escalation chain)\n      const manager = new AdaptiveThresholdManager(75 /* default CPU threshold */)\n        .addStrategy(new TrendingBoostStrategy())\n        .addStrategy(new NightTimeRelaxStrategy());\n\n      // Observer listens to Kafka topic\n      const observer = new KafkaSocialMetricObserver(\n        (process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS ?? 'localhost:9092').split(','),\n        'adaptive-threshold-group',\n      );\n\n      await observer.onEvent('social-metrics', (evt) => manager.handleSocialEvent(evt));\n\n      // Expose Prometheus endpoint\n      const port = Number(process.env.METRICS_PORT ?? 9100);\n      /* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires */\n      const http = require('http');\n      http.createServer((_, res) => {\n        res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': register.contentType });\n        register.metrics().then((m: string) => res.end(m));\n      }).listen(port, () => logger.info(`Metrics exposed on :${port}/`));\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error('Failed to bootstrap AdaptiveThresholdManager', err as Error);\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n  })();\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_11.ts",
  "originalFile": "```typescript\n/**************************************************************************************************\n * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Adaptive Threshold Manager                                             *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * File:        src/module_11.ts                                                                  *\n * Description: Dynamically adjusts alert thresholds for infrastructure metrics by weaving        *\n *              real-time social signals (likes, comments, shares, hashtag bursts) into           *\n *              the decision model. Combines Strategy, Observer and Chain-of-Responsibility       *\n *              patterns to (1) calculate adaptive thresholds, (2) observe social-metric events   *\n *              over Kafka, and (3) escalate incidents through multiple notifiers.                *\n *                                                                                                \n * Author:      PulseSphere Engineering Team                                                      *\n **************************************************************************************************/\n\n/* eslint-disable import/order */\nimport { Kafka, EachMessagePayload } from 'kafkajs';\nimport { Counter, Gauge, register } from 'prom-client';\nimport axios from 'axios';\nimport winston from 'winston';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Domain Models\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * A domain event emitted when a social interaction spike is detected.\n */\nexport interface SocialMetricEvent {\n  readonly tenantId: string;            // e.g. 'acme-social'\n  readonly metric: 'LIKE' | 'COMMENT' | 'SHARE' | 'STREAM_VIEW';\n  readonly delta: number;               // delta per second\n  readonly timestamp: number;           // epoch millis\n}\n\n/**\n * Infrastructure alert model.\n */\nexport interface Alert {\n  readonly tenantId: string;\n  readonly severity: 'INFO' | 'WARN' | 'CRIT';\n  readonly metricName: string;          // e.g. 'cpu_usage'\n  readonly observedValue: number;\n  readonly threshold: number;\n  readonly context: Record<string, unknown>;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logging\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nconst logger = winston.createLogger({\n  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',\n  format: winston.format.combine(\n    winston.format.timestamp(),\n    winston.format.errors({ stack: true }),\n    winston.format.json(),\n  ),\n  transports: [\n    new winston.transports.Console({ stderrLevels: ['error', 'warn'] }),\n  ],\n});\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Strategy Pattern \u2013 Threshold calculators\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * Strategy interface for transforming a base threshold.\n */\nexport interface ThresholdStrategy {\n  /**\n   * Compute the adjusted threshold based on a social metric event.\n   */\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Boosts threshold proportionally when sudden spikes (virality) occur.\n */\nexport class TrendingBoostStrategy implements ThresholdStrategy {\n  private readonly boostFactor: number;\n\n  constructor(boostFactor = 1.5) {\n    this.boostFactor = boostFactor;\n  }\n\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    // Simple heuristic: amplify threshold when delta exceeds 1k events/second.\n    if (event.delta > 1_000) {\n      const boosted = baseThreshold * this.boostFactor;\n      logger.debug(`TrendingBoostStrategy applied: ${baseThreshold} -> ${boosted}`);\n      return boosted;\n    }\n    return baseThreshold;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Relaxes threshold during off-peak night hours to reduce false positives.\n */\nexport class NightTimeRelaxStrategy implements ThresholdStrategy {\n  constructor(private readonly relaxFactor = 0.8) {}\n\n  private isNightHour(utcMillis: number): boolean {\n    const hour = new Date(utcMillis).getUTCHours();\n    return hour >= 0 && hour <= 6; // midnight \u2013 6 AM UTC\n  }\n\n  compute(baseThreshold: number, event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    if (this.isNightHour(event.timestamp)) {\n      const relaxed = baseThreshold * this.relaxFactor;\n      logger.debug(`NightTimeRelaxStrategy applied: ${baseThreshold} -> ${relaxed}`);\n      return relaxed;\n    }\n    return baseThreshold;\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Chain-of-Responsibility \u2013 Escalation pipeline\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * Abstract escalation handler.\n */\nabstract class EscalationHandler {\n  private nextHandler?: EscalationHandler;\n\n  setNext(handler: EscalationHandler): EscalationHandler {\n    this.nextHandler = handler;\n    return handler;\n  }\n\n  async handle(alert: Alert): Promise<void> {\n    const processed = await this.process(alert);\n    if (!processed && this.nextHandler) {\n      await this.nextHandler.handle(alert);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * @returns true if handled, false to delegate.\n   */\n  protected abstract process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Sends alerts to PagerDuty.\n */\nclass PagerDutyHandler extends EscalationHandler {\n  private readonly routingKey: string;\n\n  constructor(routingKey: string) {\n    super();\n    this.routingKey = routingKey;\n  }\n\n  /* c8 ignore next 14 */\n  protected async process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean> {\n    if (alert.severity === 'CRIT') {\n      try {\n        await axios.post('https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue', {\n          routing_key: this.routingKey,\n          event_action: 'trigger',\n          payload: {\n            summary: `CRITICAL alert for ${alert.metricName}`,\n            severity: 'critical',\n            source: 'PulseSphere',\n            custom_details: alert,\n          },\n        });\n        logger.info(`PagerDuty notified for alert=${alert.metricName}`);\n        return true;\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error('PagerDuty notification failed', err as Error);\n      }\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Posts notifications to Slack.\n */\nclass SlackHandler extends EscalationHandler {\n  constructor(private readonly webhookUrl: string) {\n    super();\n  }\n\n  protected async process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean> {\n    if (alert.severity !== 'INFO') {\n      try {\n        await axios.post(this.webhookUrl, {\n          text: `*${alert.severity}* alert for *${alert.metricName}* \u2011 ` +\n                `observed=${alert.observedValue}, threshold=${alert.threshold}`,\n        });\n        logger.info(`Slack notified for alert=${alert.metricName}`);\n        return true;\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error('Slack notification failed', err as Error);\n      }\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Fallback email notifier.\n */\nclass EmailHandler extends EscalationHandler {\n  constructor(private readonly smtpEndpoint: string) {\n    super();\n  }\n\n  protected async process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean> {\n    // In production we'd integrate with SES, SendGrid, etc.\n    try {\n      await axios.post(`${this.smtpEndpoint}/send`, {\n        to: 'ops@pulsesphere.io',\n        subject: `[PulseSphere] ${alert.severity} alert \u2013 ${alert.metricName}`,\n        body: JSON.stringify(alert, null, 2),\n      });\n      logger.info(`Email sent for alert=${alert.metricName}`);\n      return true;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error('Email notification failed', err as Error);\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Observer Pattern \u2013 Kafka social metrics consumer\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nclass KafkaSocialMetricObserver {\n  private readonly kafka: Kafka;\n\n  constructor(private readonly brokers: string[], private readonly groupId: string) {\n    this.kafka = new Kafka({ clientId: 'social-metric-consumer', brokers });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Subscribes to topic and invokes callback for each message.\n   */\n  async onEvent(\n    topic: string,\n    callback: (event: SocialMetricEvent) => Promise<void>,\n  ): Promise<void> {\n    const consumer = this.kafka.consumer({ groupId: this.groupId });\n\n    await consumer.connect();\n    await consumer.subscribe({ topic, fromBeginning: false });\n\n    await consumer.run({\n      // 10 concurrent partitions\n      eachMessage: async ({ message }: EachMessagePayload) => {\n        try {\n          if (!message.value) return;\n          const parsed: SocialMetricEvent = JSON.parse(message.value.toString());\n          await callback(parsed);\n        } catch (err) {\n          logger.warn('Failed to process social metric event', err as Error);\n        }\n      },\n    });\n\n    logger.info(`KafkaSocialMetricObserver subscribed to ${topic}`);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Adaptive Threshold Manager \u2013 Orchestrates strategies & escalations\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nexport class AdaptiveThresholdManager {\n  private readonly strategies: ThresholdStrategy[] = [];\n  private readonly escalationChain: EscalationHandler;\n  private readonly cpuUsageGauge: Gauge<number>;\n  private readonly thresholdGauge: Gauge<number>;\n  private readonly alertCounter: Counter;\n\n  constructor(\n    escalationChain: EscalationHandler,\n    private readonly defaultThreshold: number,\n  ) {\n    this.escalationChain = escalationChain;\n\n    // Prometheus metrics\n    this.cpuUsageGauge = new Gauge({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_cpu_usage',\n      help: 'Current CPU usage',\n      labelNames: ['tenant'],\n    });\n\n    this.thresholdGauge = new Gauge({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_cpu_threshold',\n      help: 'Dynamically calculated CPU threshold',\n      labelNames: ['tenant'],\n    });\n\n    this.alertCounter = new Counter({\n      name: 'pulsesphere_alert_total',\n      help: 'Number of alerts emitted',\n      labelNames: ['tenant', 'severity'],\n    });\n  }\n\n  addStrategy(strategy: ThresholdStrategy): AdaptiveThresholdManager {\n    this.strategies.push(strategy);\n    return this;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Recalculate thresholds using registered strategies.\n   */\n  private computeThreshold(event: SocialMetricEvent): number {\n    return this.strategies.reduce(\n      (threshold, strat) => strat.compute(threshold, event),\n      this.defaultThreshold,\n    );\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Simulates reading infra metric (e.g., CPU) from monitoring stack.\n   * In production, this would fetch from Prometheus, Datadog, etc.\n   */\n  /* c8 ignore next */\n  private async getCurrentCpuUsage(tenantId: string): Promise<number> {\n    // Placeholder random CPU usage for demonstration.\n    return Math.random() * 100;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Processes a social metric event \u2192 recompute threshold \u2192 evaluate infra metrics \u2192\n   * possibly trigger alert & escalation.\n   */\n  async handleSocialEvent(event: SocialMetricEvent): Promise<void> {\n    const tenantLabel = { tenant: event.tenantId };\n    const threshold = this.computeThreshold(event);\n    this.thresholdGauge.set(tenantLabel, threshold);\n\n    const cpuUsage = await this.getCurrentCpuUsage(event.tenantId);\n    this.cpuUsageGauge.set(tenantLabel, cpuUsage);\n\n    if (cpuUsage > threshold) {\n      const severity: Alert['severity'] = cpuUsage > threshold * 1.2 ? 'CRIT' : 'WARN';\n\n      const alert: Alert = {\n        tenantId: event.tenantId,\n        severity,\n        metricName: 'cpu_usage',\n        observedValue: cpuUsage,\n        threshold,\n        context: { socialMetric: event },\n      };\n\n      this.alertCounter.inc({ ...tenantLabel, severity });\n      logger.warn(\n        `Alert triggered tenant=${event.tenantId} cpu=${cpuUsage.toFixed(2)} ` +\n        `threshold=${threshold.toFixed(2)} severity=${severity}`,\n      );\n\n      await this.escalationChain.handle(alert);\n    } else {\n      logger.debug(\n        `CPU within threshold tenant=${event.tenantId} cpu=${cpuUsage.toFixed(2)} ` +\n        `threshold=${threshold.toFixed(2)}`,\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Bootstrap \u2013 wire everything together if executed directly\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  (async () => {\n    try {\n      // Build escalation chain: PagerDuty \u2192 Slack \u2192 Email\n      const pagerDuty = new PagerDutyHandler(process.env.PAGERDUTY_KEY ?? 'dummy');\n      const slack = new SlackHandler(process.env.SLACK_WEBHOOK ?? 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/dummy');\n      const email = new EmailHandler(process.env.SMTP_ENDPOINT ?? 'https://smtp.internal');\n      pagerDuty.setNext(slack).setNext(email);\n\n      // Instantiate manager with strategies\n      const manager = new AdaptiveThresholdManager(pagerDuty, 75 /* default CPU threshold */)\n        .addStrategy(new TrendingBoostStrategy())\n        .addStrategy(new NightTimeRelaxStrategy());\n\n      // Observer listens to Kafka topic\n      const observer = new KafkaSocialMetricObserver(\n        (process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS ?? 'localhost:9092').split(','),\n        'adaptive-threshold-group',\n      );\n\n      await observer.onEvent('social-metrics', (evt) => manager.handleSocialEvent(evt));\n\n      // Expose Prometheus endpoint\n      const port = Number(process.env.METRICS_PORT ?? 9100);\n      /* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires */\n      const http = require('http');\n      http.createServer((_, res) => {\n        res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': register.contentType });\n        register.metrics().then((m: string) => res.end(m));\n      }).listen(port, () => logger.info(`Metrics exposed on :${port}/`));\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error('Failed to bootstrap AdaptiveThresholdManager', err as Error);\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n  })();\n}\n```\n",
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "-```typescript",
        " /**************************************************************************************************",
        "  * PulseSphere SocialOps \u2013 Adaptive Threshold Manager                                             *",
        "  * ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------"
      ],

      "newStart": 1,
      "oldLines": 4,
      "oldStart": 1
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        "  *              real-time social signals (likes, comments, shares, hashtag bursts) into           *",
        "  *              the decision model. Combines Strategy, Observer and Chain-of-Responsibility       *",
        "  *              patterns to (1) calculate adaptive thresholds, (2) observe social-metric events   *",
        "- *              over Kafka, and (3) escalate incidents through multiple notifiers.                *",
        "- *                                                                                                ",
        "+ *              over Kafka, and (3) escalate incidents through the centralized NotificationService*",
        "+ *",
        "  * Author:      PulseSphere Engineering Team                                                      *",
        "  **************************************************************************************************/",
        " ",
        " /* eslint-disable import/order */",
        " import { Kafka, EachMessagePayload } from 'kafkajs';",
        " import { Counter, Gauge, register } from 'prom-client';",
        "-import axios from 'axios';",
        " import winston from 'winston';",
        "+import { notificationService, AlertMessage } from './services/notificationService';",
        " ",
        " /* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "  * Domain Models"
      ],
      "newLines": 16,
      "newStart": 6,
      "oldLines": 16,
      "oldStart": 7
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        " }",
        " ",
        " /* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "- * Chain-of-Responsibility \u2013 Escalation pipeline",
        "+ * Alert Dispatcher \u2013 Uses centralized NotificationService",
        "  * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/",
        " ",
        " /**",
        "- * Abstract escalation handler.",
        "+ * Convert internal alert severity to NotificationService severity.",
        "  */",
        "-abstract class EscalationHandler {",
        "-  private nextHandler?: EscalationHandler;",
        "-",
        "-  setNext(handler: EscalationHandler): EscalationHandler {",
        "-    this.nextHandler = handler;",
        "-    return handler;",
        "+function mapSeverity(severity: Alert['severity']): AlertMessage['severity'] {",
        "+  switch (severity) {",
        "+    case 'CRIT':",
        "+      return 'critical';",
        "+    case 'WARN':",
        "+      return 'warning';",
        "+    case 'INFO':",
        "+    default:",
        "+      return 'info';",
        "   }",
        "-",
        "-  async handle(alert: Alert): Promise<void> {",
        "-    const processed = await this.process(alert);",
        "-    if (!processed && this.nextHandler) {",
        "-      await this.nextHandler.handle(alert);",
        "-    }",
        "-  }",
        "-",
        "-  /**",
        "-   * @returns true if handled, false to delegate.",
        "-   */",
        "-  protected abstract process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean>;",
        " }",
        " ",
        " /**",
        "- * Sends alerts to PagerDuty.",
        "+ * Dispatches alerts through the centralized NotificationService.",
        "  */",
        "-class PagerDutyHandler extends EscalationHandler {",
        "-  private readonly routingKey: string;",
        "+async function dispatchAlert(alert: Alert): Promise<void> {",
        "+  const alertMessage: AlertMessage = {",
        "+    severity: mapSeverity(alert.severity),",
        "+    title: `${alert.severity} alert for ${alert.metricName}`,",
        "+    details: {",
        "+      tenantId: alert.tenantId,",
        "+      metricName: alert.metricName,",
        "+      observedValue: alert.observedValue,",
        "+      threshold: alert.threshold,",
        "+      ...alert.context,",
        "+    },",
        "+    sourceModule: 'module_11',",
        "+  };",
        " ",
        "-  constructor(routingKey: string) {",
        "-    super();",
        "-    this.routingKey = routingKey;",
        "-  }",
        "-",
        "-  /* c8 ignore next 14 */",
        "-  protected async process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean> {",
        "-    if (alert.severity === 'CRIT') {",
        "-      try {",
        "-        await axios.post('https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue', {",
        "-          routing_key: this.routingKey,",
        "-          event_action: 'trigger',",
        "-          payload: {",
        "-            summary: `CRITICAL alert for ${alert.metricName}`,",
        "-            severity: 'critical',",
        "-            source: 'PulseSphere',",
        "-            custom_details: alert,",
        "-          },",
        "-        });",
        "-        logger.info(`PagerDuty notified for alert=${alert.metricName}`);",
        "-        return true;",
        "-      } catch (err) {",
        "-        logger.error('PagerDuty notification failed', err as Error);",
        "-      }",
        "-    }",
        "-    return false;",
        "-  }",
        "+  await notificationService.dispatch(alertMessage);",
        "+  logger.info(`Alert dispatched via NotificationService: ${alert.metricName}`);",
        " }",
        " ",
        "-/**",
        "- * Posts notifications to Slack.",
        "- */",
        "-class SlackHandler extends EscalationHandler {",
        "-  constructor(private readonly webhookUrl: string) {",
        "-    super();",
        "-  }",
        "-",
        "-  protected async process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean> {",
        "-    if (alert.severity !== 'INFO') {",
        "-      try {",
        "-        await axios.post(this.webhookUrl, {",
        "-          text: `*${alert.severity}* alert for *${alert.metricName}* \u2011 ` +",
        "-                `observed=${alert.observedValue}, threshold=${alert.threshold}`,",
        "-        });",
        "-        logger.info(`Slack notified for alert=${alert.metricName}`);",
        "-        return true;",
        "-      } catch (err) {",
        "-        logger.error('Slack notification failed', err as Error);",
        "-      }",
        "-    }",
        "-    return false;",
        "-  }",
        "-}",
        "-",
        "-/**",
        "- * Fallback email notifier.",
        "- */",
        "-class EmailHandler extends EscalationHandler {",
        "-  constructor(private readonly smtpEndpoint: string) {",
        "-    super();",
        "-  }",
        "-",
        "-  protected async process(alert: Alert): Promise<boolean> {",
        "-    // In production we'd integrate with SES, SendGrid, etc.",
        "-    try {",
        "-      await axios.post(`${this.smtpEndpoint}/send`, {",
        "-        to: 'ops@pulsesphere.io',",
        "-        subject: `[PulseSphere] ${alert.severity} alert \u2013 ${alert.metricName}`,",
        "-        body: JSON.stringify(alert, null, 2),",
        "-      });",
        "-      logger.info(`Email sent for alert=${alert.metricName}`);",
        "-      return true;",
        "-    } catch (err) {",
        "-      logger.error('Email notification failed', err as Error);",
        "-      return false;",
        "-    }",
        "-  }",
        "-}",
        "-",
        " /* -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "  * Observer Pattern \u2013 Kafka social metrics consumer",
        "  * -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/"
      ],

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_17.ts"

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```typescript\n/**\n * PulseSphere SocialOps\n * -------------------------------------------------------\n * Module: module_17 \u2013 Social-Aware Auto-Scaler & Alerting\n *\n * This module wires together multiple classic patterns to\n * provide a production-grade, socially-aware auto-scaling\n * component.\n *\n * Patterns in use\n *  \u2022 Observer            \u2013 MetricEventBus (EventEmitter)\n *  \u2022 Strategy            \u2013 ScalingStrategy implementations\n *  \u2022 Command             \u2013 Scale{Up,Down}Command\n *  \u2022 Chain-of-Responsibility \u2013 AlertHandler chain\n *  \u2022 Event Driven        \u2013 Kafka consumer feeding the bus\n *\n * External deps\n *  \u2022 kafkajs             \u2013 High-throughput metric ingestion\n *  \u2022 axios               \u2013 Mock service-mesh HTTP client\n *\n * Author:  PulseSphere SRE Platform Team\n * -------------------------------------------------------\n */\n\nimport { EventEmitter } from 'events';\nimport { Kafka, EachMessagePayload, logLevel as KafkaLogLevel } from 'kafkajs';\nimport axios, { AxiosInstance } from 'axios';\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                               Static typings                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/** A single enriched metric coming from pulse-fusion pipeline. */\nexport interface EnrichedMetric {\n  readonly appId: string;\n  readonly cpuPct: number;               // 0-100\n  readonly memPct: number;               // 0-100\n  readonly sentimentScore: number;       // \u20131 .. +1\n  readonly trendingScore: number;        // 0 .. 1 (probability of virality)\n  readonly timestamp: number;            // epoch ms\n}\n\nexport enum ScalingDecision {\n  SCALE_UP   = 'SCALE_UP',\n  SCALE_DOWN = 'SCALE_DOWN',\n  STABLE     = 'STABLE',\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                               Observer Layer                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/**\n * Central in-process event bus \u2013 translates Kafka messages into local events\n * that strategies/handlers can subscribe to without needing Kafka semantics.\n */\nclass MetricEventBus extends EventEmitter {\n  static readonly EVT_METRIC = 'metric';\n\n  emitMetric(metric: EnrichedMetric): void {\n    this.emit(MetricEventBus.EVT_METRIC, metric);\n  }\n\n  onMetric(listener: (metric: EnrichedMetric) => void): void {\n    this.on(MetricEventBus.EVT_METRIC, listener);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                           Strategy & Command Layer                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/** Scaling command contract (Command Pattern). */\ninterface ScalingCommand {\n  execute(): Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/** Concrete command \u2013 scale up by N replicas. */\nclass ScaleUpCommand implements ScalingCommand {\n  constructor(\n    private readonly meshClient: ServiceMeshClient,\n    private readonly appId: string,\n    private readonly factor: number,\n  ) {}\n\n  public async execute(): Promise<void> {\n    await this.meshClient.scale(this.appId, this.factor);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Concrete command \u2013 scale down by N replicas. */\nclass ScaleDownCommand implements ScalingCommand {\n  constructor(\n    private readonly meshClient: ServiceMeshClient,\n    private readonly appId: string,\n    private readonly factor: number,\n  ) {}\n\n  public async execute(): Promise<void> {\n    await this.meshClient.scale(this.appId, -this.factor);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Strategy contract \u2013 returns a Command or undefined when no action. */\ninterface ScalingStrategy {\n  evaluate(metric: EnrichedMetric): ScalingCommand | undefined;\n}\n\n/**\n * CPU-centric strategy \u2013 reacts when usage is beyond a threshold.\n * This is a simplistic threshold-based algorithm; production code\n * would rely on predictive analytics or RL models.\n */\nclass HighCpuStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  constructor(\n    private readonly meshClient: ServiceMeshClient,\n    private readonly highWaterMarkPct = 75,\n    private readonly lowWaterMarkPct  = 35,\n  ) {}\n\n  evaluate(metric: EnrichedMetric): ScalingCommand | undefined {\n    if (metric.cpuPct >= this.highWaterMarkPct) {\n      const factor = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(metric.cpuPct / 25));\n      return new ScaleUpCommand(this.meshClient, metric.appId, factor);\n    }\n    if (metric.cpuPct <= this.lowWaterMarkPct) {\n      return new ScaleDownCommand(this.meshClient, metric.appId, 1);\n    }\n    return undefined;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Sentiment spike strategy \u2013 anticipates virality before infrastructure\n * gets hammered. Combines sentiment & trending scores.\n */\nclass SentimentSpikeStrategy implements ScalingStrategy {\n  constructor(\n    private readonly meshClient: ServiceMeshClient,\n    private readonly threshold = 0.85,\n  ) {}\n\n  evaluate(metric: EnrichedMetric): ScalingCommand | undefined {\n    if (metric.trendingScore >= this.threshold && metric.sentimentScore > 0) {\n      // For viral events we aggressively over-provision.\n      return new ScaleUpCommand(this.meshClient, metric.appId, 3);\n    }\n    return undefined;\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                          Chain-of-Responsibility Layer                     */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ninterface AlertHandler {\n  setNext(handler: AlertHandler): AlertHandler;\n  handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void>;\n}\n\nabstract class BaseAlertHandler implements AlertHandler {\n  private nextHandler?: AlertHandler;\n\n  public setNext(handler: AlertHandler): AlertHandler {\n    this.nextHandler = handler;\n    return handler;\n  }\n\n  public async handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void> {\n    if (this.nextHandler) {\n      await this.nextHandler.handle(metric);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/** Debug-level handler \u2013 just noisy logs in dev environments. */\nclass DebugAlertHandler extends BaseAlertHandler {\n  async handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void> {\n    if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production') {\n      console.debug(\n        `[DEBUG] Metric \u2192 cpu:${metric.cpuPct}% mem:${metric.memPct}% ` +\n        `sentiment:${metric.sentimentScore.toFixed(2)} trending:${metric.trendingScore}`,\n      );\n    }\n    await super.handle(metric);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Warning-level handler \u2013 triggers standard alert channel. */\nclass WarningAlertHandler extends BaseAlertHandler {\n  async handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void> {\n    if (metric.cpuPct > 85 || metric.memPct > 85) {\n      await AlertChannel.slack(\n        `\u26a0\ufe0f High resource usage on app ${metric.appId}: ` +\n        `CPU ${metric.cpuPct}% / MEM ${metric.memPct}%`,\n      );\n    }\n    await super.handle(metric);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Critical handler \u2013 immediate pager-duty. */\nclass CriticalAlertHandler extends BaseAlertHandler {\n  async handle(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void> {\n    const isCrit = metric.cpuPct > 95 || metric.memPct > 95 || metric.trendingScore >= 0.95;\n    if (isCrit) {\n      await AlertChannel.pagerDuty(\n        `\ud83d\udea8 CRITICAL: ${metric.appId} likely overloaded! ` +\n        `cpu=${metric.cpuPct}% mem=${metric.memPct}% trending=${metric.trendingScore}`,\n      );\n    }\n    await super.handle(metric);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Service-mesh client                           */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nclass ServiceMeshClient {\n  private readonly http: AxiosInstance;\n\n  constructor(\n    readonly baseURL: string = process.env.SERVICE_MESH_BASE_URL ?? 'http://mesh-gateway',\n    readonly timeout = 5_000,\n  ) {\n    this.http = axios.create({ baseURL: this.baseURL, timeout: this.timeout });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Scale an application up/down by \"factor\" replicas.\n   * This interacts with the platform orchestrator through\n   * the mesh sidecar.\n   */\n  public async scale(appId: string, factor: number): Promise<void> {\n    try {\n      await this.http.post(`/apps/${appId}/scale`, { factor });\n      console.info(`[Scale] ${appId} scaled by ${factor}`);\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.error(`[Scale] Failed to scale ${appId}:`, err);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              Alerting helpers                              */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nclass AlertChannel {\n  static async slack(message: string): Promise<void> {\n    // Placeholder: in production we'd push to Slack webhook\n    console.log(`[Slack] ${message}`);\n  }\n\n  static async pagerDuty(message: string): Promise<void> {\n    // Placeholder: integration key & routing will sit in vault\n    console.log(`[PagerDuty] ${message}`);\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                            Main Orchestrator Class                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nexport class SocialAwareAutoScaler {\n  private readonly kafka: Kafka;\n  private readonly meshClient = new ServiceMeshClient();\n  private readonly eventBus = new MetricEventBus();\n  private readonly strategies: ScalingStrategy[] = [];\n  private readonly alertChain: AlertHandler;\n  private isShuttingDown = false;\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly kafkaBrokers: string[] = (process.env.KAFKA_BROKERS ?? 'localhost:9092').split(','),\n    private readonly groupId = 'social-auto-scaler',\n    private readonly topic = 'pulse.metric.enriched.v1',\n  ) {\n    this.kafka = new Kafka({\n      clientId: 'pulse-auto-scaler',\n      brokers: this.kafkaBrokers,\n      logLevel: KafkaLogLevel.NOTHING,\n    });\n\n    /* Strategy registry */\n    this.strategies.push(\n      new HighCpuStrategy(this.meshClient),\n      new SentimentSpikeStrategy(this.meshClient),\n    );\n\n    /* Alert chain bootstrap */\n    const debug   = new DebugAlertHandler();\n    const warn    = new WarningAlertHandler();\n    const crit    = new CriticalAlertHandler();\n    debug.setNext(warn).setNext(crit);\n    this.alertChain = debug; // entrypoint\n\n    /* Wiring observer */\n    this.eventBus.onMetric(async (metric) => {\n      await this.alertChain.handle(metric);\n      await this.applyStrategies(metric);\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Initialize kafka consumer & begin event pump.\n   */\n  public async start(): Promise<void> {\n    const consumer = this.kafka.consumer({ groupId: this.groupId });\n    await consumer.connect();\n    await consumer.subscribe({ topic: this.topic, fromBeginning: false });\n\n    console.info('[AutoScaler] started.');\n\n    await consumer.run({\n      eachMessage: async (payload: EachMessagePayload) => {\n        if (this.isShuttingDown) {\n          await consumer.disconnect();\n          return;\n        }\n        const metric = this.parseMetric(payload);\n        if (metric) this.eventBus.emitMetric(metric);\n      },\n    });\n\n    /* Graceful shutdown binding */\n    process.once('SIGINT' , () => this.shutdown());\n    process.once('SIGTERM', () => this.shutdown());\n  }\n\n  private async applyStrategies(metric: EnrichedMetric): Promise<void> {\n    for (const strategy of this.strategies) {\n      const cmd = strategy.evaluate(metric);\n      if (cmd) {\n        try {\n          await cmd.execute();\n          break; // one strategy acted \u2013 avoid conflicting actions\n        } catch (err) {\n          console.error('[AutoScaler] Command failed:', err);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Converts Kafka payload into EnrichedMetric object with validation.\n   */\n  private parseMetric({ message }: EachMessagePayload): EnrichedMetric | undefined {\n    try {\n      if (!message.value) return;\n      const obj = JSON.parse(message.value.toString());\n\n      /* Lightweight validation */\n      const metric: EnrichedMetric = {\n        appId          : obj.appId,\n        cpuPct         : Number(obj.cpuPct),\n        memPct         : Number(obj.memPct),\n        sentimentScore : Number(obj.sentimentScore),\n        trendingScore  : Number(obj.trendingScore),\n        timestamp      : Number(obj.timestamp),\n      };\n\n      if (!metric.appId || Number.isNaN(metric.cpuPct)) {\n        throw new Error('Invalid metric shape');\n      }\n      return metric;\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.warn('[AutoScaler] Discarded malformed metric:', err);\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Shutdown hook \u2013 ensures consumer disconnect & drains inflight tasks.\n   */\n  private async shutdown(): Promise<void> {\n    if (this.isShuttingDown) return;\n    this.isShuttingDown = true;\n\n    console.info('[AutoScaler] Shutting down\u2026');\n    // Additional cleanup (HTTP, etc.) could be awaited here.\n  }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                     Module bootstrap (only when run directly)              */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nif (require.main === module) {\n  (async () => {\n    try {\n      const scaler = new SocialAwareAutoScaler();\n      await scaler.start();\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.error('[AutoScaler] Fatal:', err);\n      process.exitCode = 1;\n    }\n  })();\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_17.ts",
    "numLines": 399,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 399
  },
  "type": "text"
}
